user_comment: Exceeded my expectations Really great leggings. I got the polka dot ones and another pair in black. Both fit perfect, nice compression without losing flexibility. Squat proof! No camel toe. I did also order the light blue ones but you could see more than I’d like to show. Not that they were not squat proof or gave me a cameltoe, but you could definitely see the outline even with seamless panties. Other girls may not have this problem and they were still great quality like the ones I am going to keep, just not for me. Highly recommend even though I’ll be sending the blue ones back. I am around 135-140 lbs, 5’7, usually size 4-6 in pants. I ordered size small.

Nice Lightweight Pants For Travel. Love The Color. I got these cargo/travel pants for my wife. I selected the Green/Grey color which was a nice earthy and natural tone. The stitching and zippers are good quality. She is 5'3" 135 lbs and we found the medium size fit her well. The pants are fitted at the hip with a looser style fit in the leg. The pants were not too long for her(so if you are tall these might not be for you). There are 4 zippered pockets - 2 slash pockets on the either side of the hip and 2 cargo pockets further down on the thigh. The cargo pockets will be convenient for carrying a phone or other items securely. The material is lightweight and dries quickly. Only drawback is the swishy noise when you walk. That is common to this type of pants. Overall a good lightweight pant for travel.

Super Comfy! I am absolutely loving these sweater pants. They are wide legged, high waisted, stretchy, and comfortable as can be. They have an elastic waist with a drawstring and I would say they are true to size. I weigh 150 pounds at 5’7 and I ordered a medium. I do have longer legs, and these fall at the perfect length. There is a cable knot design that goes down the outside seam on both legs. The material feels like a sweater and is incredibly soft. I’ve worn these pants twice and have not washed them yet, but should they not wash well I will come back and update my review. What I like about them is they can be dressed up or down. They’re perfect For a party but also perfect for lounging. I like the diversity of occasions that I can wear these pants too. They look and feel like a quality pant, even after wearing them. Some pants stretch out weirdly, but these pants are not one of them. Very happy!

Silky soft and warm knit pants with a bit of style. These straight leg, business / casual pants are quite comfortable. They are made of a super soft knit material that is a little fuzzy too. I got the X-Large Petite size and they fit well around my waist, but were a little shorter than I prefer on leg length. I am 5' 4", and often pants are too long. Since they had the petite sizes I went with that, but I guess I am borderline between sizes for the sizing of these pants. According to the chart and X-Large in regular would have 42.1" legs and I know those would be too long. Sort of a pickle on that one, lol. They are well made with solid stitching. The pleat down the front gives them a more stylish look for wearing away from home as well as lounging at home. They have a drawstring for waist adjustment, if needed. There are no pockets, though. With the thickness of the material, I thought they would be very warm, but the knit material must breath well as I wore them for 15 minutes or more while doing a couple things in the house and they were just as warm as the thin pants I had on before trying these on. They arrived in a zip bag and folded with them was a piece of tissue paper. Not sure why, but the tissue paper left tiny (pin-head sized) pieces of white here and there on the pants. The paper looked fine with no tears, but somehow it shed onto the pants, lol. Just mentioning as they were very visible on the black pair of pants I got. Over all, I'm recommending these knit pants. They are of a nice quality knit material and well made. Only caution I will say is study the size chart and hopefully the sizes will work better for the next person!

👠 Elongated Crotch - Runs True To Size For Reference I am ( 36C 162 lbs 5’4” size med / large ) Love the color on these khaki hue sweater pants, but what is most impressive is the wide range of sizes and lengths that is available. The size inclusivity for individuals who require a shorter inseam is Fantastic ! The only negative is that it is limited to only 2 colors ( black & brown ) I would love to see a wider range of color options. The sizing chart states the petite inseam as 26.8”. I measured mine to be a 27” inseam. The fabric is comprised of 50% Viscose, 28% Polyester and 22% Nylon. The material is soft and has a opaque appearance which falls right in between a knit and a sweater. It is not itchy but does offer a good amount of stretch and sight warmth. The elasticized waistband is 2” thick and offers a working drawstring for an adjustable fit. I did not find these to be a high rise as they sit right on the waist on me. What I did find is that the crotch area is elongated, which looks unflattering, but can be slightly corrected by rolling up the waistband as an option, which then will effect the length of the pants. The description refers to these as straight leg design, however the pants I received are straight just up to below the knee, it then flares out to a wide leg hem. What gives these pants some interest is the vertical outseam which runs the length of the pants from the waistband to the bottom hem. The seam in my opinion elevates the appearance of these pants so they can be utilized not only for casual or lounge purposes but with a pair of heels can be taken into the work place. Machine washable according to the care instruction label - cold delicate cycle but I found that hand washing has helped prevent shrinking due to the fabric makeup

Love! There is nothing I love more than an elevated knit pant for the fall and winter. Soft, cozy, and comfortable, boy do these fit the bill. You can easily throw them on with a blazer and loafer and look put together for work. Or a cute sweatshirt and sneaker or flat for running errands or a brunch. These feel to be a really nice thickness and very plush. They are high- waisted. I’m 5’2” and the length is perfect. They hit right at the ankle. I’m not sure of how they will wash and wear so for now they are a 4 star pant, but I will update if they seem to wash beautifully and wear nicely over time. So far, I love them!!

Size up if you want that low-slung fit. Why I love these sweatpants: * they are extremely well crafted. * the tie for the waist is actually sewn to the INSIDE of the waist band, which I very much appreciate. (Because usually the tie is on the outside, I am constantly tucking it in. Or, I just pull out the whole string because of the bump it creates.) * there are two very deep pockets. * the fabric weave on the inside is ribbed to be cooler * the outside of the fabric is smooth, and will not be picking up so much lint and cat hair! * the legs of the pants fit loosely down to the cuff at the bottom, which is elasticized. NOTE: I ordered these in a size large, and I’m very glad that I did. I wanted a pair of sweatpants that fit lower onto my hips. - For reference, I usually fit into a size small, and I am 5 foot two, approximately 110 pounds.

Not as flattering as I’d hoped I ordered the black in size Medium based on the hip measurement. They are comfortable, but the side pockets pucker out in a very unflattering way. I’m not sure that a larger size would remedy that or not. It seems like a possible design flaw, but I can't tell. They also drag the floor on me, and I’m 5’4.5”. I think I may be able to wear them with high heels without altering them. The waistband is stretchy and accommodating, but the top elastic band is very skinny, so while it’s not uncomfortable, it’s not as comfortable as it could be if the band were wider. They washed well, with no shrinkage, and I hung them to dry. If the pockets didn’t pucker out so much, I think I would like them, but they just are not flattering on me.

Comfortable stretchy pants I was really pleased with the fact that these pants are what I call a "true" wide-leg pant—not just straight-legged—they actually are wider at the bottom than they are at the top. I think this gives them a flattering fit that can be hard to achieve with pants that are a little stingier with the fabric usage. I'm 5'6", 125 pounds with a 27" waist and ordered the medium since the small would have been tight around the waist. The medium is a little loose, and I might have been able to get away with a small, but better safe than sorry! Overall, I found them to be a good size for me, especially if I wear them with longer tops that cover the waistband. They have a synthetic feel, just like you might expect from a polyester spandex blend, so my texture-sensitive side is a little nonplussed, but they make up for it by being super loose and stretchy and flattering!

Fabric looks very casual; unforgiving These are comfortable pants, but the fabric makes them look like strictly casual or lounge wear. The material is a stretchy, thin polyester/ spandex blend that feels like the fabric my sun protection shirts are made from. These pants seem more like swimwear coverups, and show every lump and bump. As for sizing, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 170 pounds, have a 36C bust with narrow shoulders, a short torso, and a wide but flat derriere, carry most of my weight in the middle, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 pants (medium size pants and joggers at Old Navy and H&M), or a woman's 10 or 12 in dresses. I ordered these pants in the "large", which seem to be the correct size for me (see my pics), though they are not flattering to my apple shape. Overall, these are comfortable pants, but very casual looking because of the fabric.

Absolutely stretchy with unbeatable comfort! This item arrived in the Amazon packaging but had its own plastic bag with tissue paper between folds. The material was as stated on the description. I got a size larger (XXL) but I could have gotten my size (XL) or even a size smaller (L). It really depends on how you like yours to fit. These were to be loungepants and something I can throw on to go to the parcel downstairs. The pockets are a decent size and fits my pixel phone without complications. The cuffs are elastic and stretch nicely when I want to wear them at the knees. I'm short 5'3 so the length is quite long and bunches up, but the cuff does help to keep it from dragging if I were to wear them at my ankles. They are extremely soft and buttery with A LOT of stretch. The light does go through them and if you were to get them really tight like leggings I'm almost positive that you will see what's underneath. These are meant to be slightly loose, but you could really get your size or maybe even one size smaller depending on your build. They are not for the winter weather but are good for maybe up until Autumn based on your location. Do not expect to replace your sweatpants for these in those cold 🌡️They can be worn high-waisted or even lower for a drop crotch appearance. The drawstring is long but that's most likely because I got a larger size. Overall, they are great lounge pants and quite easy to throw on when necessary for quick errands. Updates later on wash and wear but thus far it's worthy of 5 🌟.

My new favorite "I don't want to wear pants" pants The instant I tried these on, I knew I had found my new favorite pants for the days when, no matter how we may try to fight it, wearing pants is necessary. First, I'm 5'3" so finding joggers that aren't half a mile too long is a bit of a unicorn all on its own. Usually even the ones that have elasticized ankles will have fabric bunched up and puddling on top of my feet, which really is just more kinds of attractive than I can find words for. These fit true to the size chart in every measure provided. The fabric is like fleece and cotton jersey had a baby. Think the heaviest heavyweight t-shirt you've ever had, and add like 12% more heft to that fabric. So if you're looking for a pair of fleece sweatpants, these are not the pants you're looking for. But if you, like me, enjoy something a bit lighter and more breathable and just generally less there than fleece while it's still providing coziness, you will probably love these pants as much as I do. I have a 29" waist and 40ish" hips and I went with a Large to make sure they were a bit loose. The fabric has enough stretch to it that you could size down for a slimmer fit and still be able to move. The elastic waist and drawstring mean you can also size up if you want more of a "walking around in a blanket but not tripping over it because the universe loves me and wants me to be happy" fit. With pockets actually big enough that your phone isn't constantly falling out. So I will be grabbing these in at least one other color. Possibly four. That's the future and I know not what it holds. All I know is, I love these pants and think there's a good chance you may also love these pants.

Super Comfortable Every girl needs a good pair of sweatpants and I had a pair that i’ve loved for 5+years— they’ve become very tattered and old so it was time for a new pair. Pleasantly surprised with the quality of this pair, they are very soft and cozy. I am 5’2 and roughly 145lbs curvy athletic build, and they have a little bit extra room but are cuffed at the ends so not super long. I think if you are a tall girl or are longer in the legs, these might be too short. Only negative thing i have to say is they are sooo high waisted on me. I do have a shorter torso but they could easily sit right below my breast line— simple fix is i just roll the waist band a bit— i’m not planning on wearing them outside of the house so aesthetically the rolled waistband isnt great but my primary use for these pants is just to lounge so i don’t care how it looks. Update day 2 of wearing these and they have started to pill on the inner thigh part of the pants— i haven’t washed them yet and it’s just from wearing them

High waisted and baggy! Yea.... I can see how these would make one feel like MC Hammer. These are definitely ultimate lounge pants. I measure 32-24-36 and ordered the xs which is my normal size. The waistband is indeed very tight, it only fits best way up on my waist. The crotch is super super long. Don't even bother rolling these bad boys down either because you will have created one huge spare tire around your hips. Big enough to keep people out of your personal bubble though, so maybe that's a good thing. The material is very thick and warm which I absolutely love because I am always cold. Its soft on the inside. The pockets are nice as usual. After seeing how big these pants were when I opened the package I though maybe I could use them for period week pants, but because the waistband is so tight, I don't honestly know if that would give me the relief I'd be looking for. These feel pretty sturdy though and as long as I don't gain any inches right below my ribs I should be able to wear them for a long time.

High-waisted at natural waist, baggy sweatpants, loose ankle cuffs, cute with crop top These are nice quality, thick material, well-stitched, heather grey sweatpants. This High-Waisted style fits right at my natural waist. The elastic waist is slightly snug on me, plus there is a drawstring cord. The rest of the pants are pretty baggy all over, including the butt, front/crotch, hips, legs, and loose ankle cuffs. There are 2 good pockets, one on each side, plenty roomy for hands or phone or keys. I usually wear size Small in sweatpants, size 4 in jeans. I have gained a few pounds, so I feel thicker around the middle than usual. But for reference, I am 5'7" tall, 130#, and ordered size Small. These sweatpants look better on the online model than me, and look cute with a tight crop top since they are high-waisted and baggy. But I think they are generally comfy, loose sweats. I think my teenage daughter will probably steal them from me! Thank you!

The care tag says not to tumble dry, but I don't think sweatpants should be easy care, so I put them in the dryer. They seemed to do fine.The only downside for me was the inside isn't as soft as I like. The lining is not uncomfortable, but when I want to lounge in sweatpants, I want them to be very soft. These are so soft on the outside. I wish they'd made the inside just like the outside.

Wish it didn't have a hole LIKES: - Material is a breathable cotton blend that is soft and comfortable - It has pockets DISLIKES: - One of the pockets has a hole in it from bad sewing - The red is more of a tomato color. I would have liked a deeper, richer red. OTHER NOTES: - The inside of the pants is textured, so it can add a little warmth, but the material is fairly thin. These are thin sweatpants. - Leg cuffs have elastic. - I believe the size chart shows the measurements of the pants, not what your measurements should be for that size. Since the waist is elastic, I suggest getting the size just below your own measurement. My waist measures 32". If these were non-elastic pants, then I would get XL (chart says 32" waist), but past experience with elastic waistband pants is it would hang low on my hips and threaten to slide off. By getting the L instead (size chart indicates 29" waist) it turned out to be the correct size. The elastic band cinches at my waist at just the right tightness. - There is no drawstring.

No Draw Strings. Nice Feeling Fabric. These pants fit me fairly well, but I think that other people who are more blessed with some "junk-in-the-trunk" might find that these do not offer much room in the backside area.I carry most of my weight in my core/belly and I have a flat backside, but these pants seem to fit me well. I'm also only 5' 3" and the length is good with minimal pooling of fabric length near the ankles. The ankles have elastic though, so they don't go past my feet and there is enough extra fabric for individuals with longer legs. However, I think the length would be too short on my 5' 10.5" sister.The fabric has a nice smooth soft feel to it. It's not quite like standard sweats, so I do like that.I do wish that these pants had a drawstring, like most traditional sweatpants tend to have. Because of my rounded core, I often have to cinch draw strings in to keep my bottoms from slipping down. These sweats just have an elastic waist.

The quality of the material is outstanding, they don't feel synthetic at all, it is a cotton inside and outside and feels fery pleasant to the skin. These pants are quite stretchy and warm, can be worn during colder days, but I wore it in a summer and it didn't feel hot at all.

nice swim pants This review is for "ATTRACO Womens Swim Pants High Waisted Swim Leggings Long Swimming Pants with Pockets." The swim pants arrived in a resealable Ziploc-style plastic bag. There were tags attached to the pants. I really like these swim pants overall. they are well made and the stitching is good quality. I especially like the pockets. Unfortunately, the size chart in the listing photos does not match the size chart provided by Amazon. In the listing details. I went with the size chart provided by the seller in the photographs and ordered an extra large. (Amazon suggested I order a size large.) As it turns out, the extra large is too big around the waist and I'm going to have to take them in to wear them in the water or out jogging. They look good but if I move around they tend to slip down. On the other hand, I am quite tall at a little over 5 ft. 10 inches and I'm happy with the extra leg length provided by the XL size. I'm going with five stars because the sizing issue is not a product defect.

Comfortable, versatile slacks. Very comfortable slacks. I’m 5’3, 140 & medium fit well. Love the wide legs and versatility of these slacks that allow them to be dressed up or down. I bought black slacks but may be looking to get other colors in the future.

Flowy not linen pants Light weight flowy fabric just what I was looking for. These are full length. I’m 5’8” and didn’t want them short; wearing a XL. They are not linen which I was also considering for summer, but this will not look sloppy and get overly wrinkled. Great for travel! Yes! Pockets!

Lovely fabric, quality garment, great fit, flattering These trousers were selected for a woman 5'8", 165 lbs., and the Large size fit is perfect. These slacks are lovely, dressy, the fabric substantial with a nice drape, the needle work perfect. This is a quality garment that should sell for a lot more than $30. Very flattering! The buttons are pretty, but only the waist button is functional. The buttons on the pocket flaps are for aesthetics only as are the flaps themselves, but there really are front pockets. The two back pockets are aesthetic only. The two cargo pockets are real. I like that the cargo pockets are placed lower on the leg and away from the hip, keeping the hips looking slim. The elastic waist in the back is well done and allows a good fit while allowing for roominess. This is a good thing because there are only two belt loops which are located on the front of the slacks so a belt is not really an option. This is the only thing that I find to complain about. Again, this is a quality garment suitable for dress or casual wear. I highly recommend you get them while you can at this price. I know I will be further investigating the AusLook brand for more quality bargains.

These look so good! These sweatpants are amazing! These are so wonderful that I am officially throwing out all my old sweatpants and getting these in every color. Not only do they feel awesome on, but the quality is outstanding! The material is a thick quality without making you hot, yet such a great thickness that you know they will last. The stitching is well done so these will be great for the gym, running, a casual walk, to sleep or lounge in as they will hold up well even through the most intense tasks or workouts. There is an adjustable waist line, both via by an elastic and drawstring adjustments. The cargo pockets are such a super fun feature and take these sweatpants up a notch. You can wear them casually or dress them up with a cute jean jacket and kicks. I don't like my sweatpants baggy so I chose a more form fitting style and size which for me that was a size large. These are true to size for me at 175 pounds 5’4”. If you are similar in height and weight but want a more relaxed or baggier fit, then an xl should give you that fit. I as well love all the color options available. I selected the fantastic color of Coffee Grey and its perfect. It's a soft neutral beige tone. So far it has paired with all my tops and looked wonderful! I definitely recommend grabbing these!

Perfect Pants! I ordered these pants because I thought the idea of a cargo sweat pant was unique and I'm so glad I did! These pants are the most comfortable pants I own and I now just want to live in them 24/7. I'm 5'5, 135 lbs, 30" waist, and I ordered the medium and they fit me perfectly. These pants are so soft, and comfortable, and warm! I put them on and even with the air conditioner blowing on me I am warm and comfortable where usually I'd be freezing! I honestly wish this was a set because I want a hoodie in whatever material this is! The pants are stylish and comfy with all the pockets helping to make the pants not seem overly bulky for no reason. The black is a good solid dark black with no purple or blue undertones. The drawstring is solid and doesn't seem like it will easily break and even has little end caps so they don't fray. The waistband has good elasticity, they're thick enough to be warm without being heavy, and definitely versatile enough to wear both lounging at home and out to run errands without looking slobbish. I love these sweatpants and think they are worth every penny, if they had a pair in blue or green I'd buy them too! If you are looking for some stylish yet warm sweatpants, don't hesitate to get these! I promise you won't regret it.

Warm, Comfortable Fabric...Useful Leg Pockets...Attractive Style I ordered these sweatpants in size Small in the color Apricot, which is like a light beige and does not resemble the peachy color of the apricot fruit. I like the color very much. It is an attractive neutral shade that hides stains better than white and can be paired successfully with tops in the tones of cream, off-white, taupe, as well as with more vibrant colors. The soft fleece underside of the fabric feels comfy against the skin and the substantial thickness of the fabric keeps you nice and warm. The waistband is well-constructed and sturdy. The cargo pockets are fully functional. There are also 2 hidden side seam pockets for your hands but their lining is made from a different, muslin-type of fabric. These pants look more like trousers than regular sweatpants...much more attractive to wear out and about.For reference, I'm 5'4" with a 29" waist and 38" hips. In pants, I normally order size Mediums or oversized/roomy size Smalls, or size 8. I ordered these pants in size Small based upon the size chart and after having read some reviews of people who seemed to be between 2 sizes for which the smaller size fit. I was apprehensive about how much these pants might shrink though, as they are 47% cotton. Prior to having laundered them, the size Small fits me well without bagginess and the length is fine on me but I worry that they could become a bit snug after laundering, so I'll be vigilant to wash them in cold water and dry them on low. Upon future orders of different colors of these nice quality pants, I think I might size up to guarantee some roominess after the shrinkage that usually occurs when cotton garments have been laundered multiple times. I'm very happy with these super comfy, attractive sweatpants and would recommend them!

The weight of these pants is really nice for cool weather. They are not 'thin' at all. The drawstring at the waist is great and the cargo pockets are very roomy. Given that I am typically a large but was able to get these on without much trouble, I'm going to order another pair in a medium for myself. I think the large will be too long (I'm only 5'4"). I received these as part of the Vine program so I can't return them but I love them enough to purchase a 2nd pair.

Great Sweatpants I am very impressed with these sweatpants! According to other reviews, different colors fit differently, so I can only speak to the black. really long rise, consider sizing down Maybe I should have sized down. I am 5’4” 115 lbs and my measurements say I should be a small on the size chart, but these pants were very large on me. The most surprising thing was how long the rise is (13”). I have a long torso and this looked too long on me. I didn’t see high rise anywhere in the description. The extra long rise was almost enough to roll the waistband down once. The waistband is quite wide though. Also the waist band was too big at nearly 28” around before stretching the elastic. According to the size chart, should have been a size medium measurement. The length was 30” long. The pockets easily accommodated my I phone mini; however, if you wear the pants as high rise, the dimensions no longer allow for sitting while keeping the phone in your pocket. The pants are lightweight and the color was as pictured. The material would be good for warmer days (60+ degrees Fahrenheit). The fabric makes it tempting to order a size XS and hem the length, but if the rise is only slightly smaller that becomes too much to be worth altering.

The wine color works great with blue (bright, light and sky), or pink/light purple.

Don’t Hesitate:) These are exactly what I wanted!! A note on sizing: you could get your regular size or maybe size down. I think I totally depends on what you are wanting. I took my measurements and literally landed right in between XS and S. I opted for the XS and 32” inseam. I wanted them tighter and more like a compression legging and it was a PERFECT fit! With that being said, I definitely could have gotten a small, and that probably would have fit great too, just wouldn’t have hugged the thighs and butt as much ;) The material is workoutish, but I like that. And the flare is perfect for me! But I was also wanting actual flare pants (not bootcut pants claiming to be flared 🙄) they are not. Super flare like a bell bottom, but more flare than bootcut. The inseam is fine. I would have actually liked a 33” but I am all legs and like my pants on the long side. All that being said, I adore these pants and they are perfect 🤩

Warm and comfy; LOVE the zippered pockets! These are awesome leggings! They are warm and very comfortable, with a wide waistband that doesn't roll down, or dig into my skin. They help hold my jiggly belly in, without feeling constrictive. The zippered pockets are nice and deep (large enough to hold my cellphone), and I like the extra pocket on the back (it is quite large, and can even fit my oversized Galaxy S22 Ultra). I haven't yet worn them in wet conditions, but did splash them with water to see if they were actually water resistant as claimed, and the water beaded up without soaking into the fabric, so it seems they are. These are NOT see-through, and the black is a true, deep black. They are full-length leggings, and on my body go past my ankles. For size reference, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 165 pounds, have a short torso with a flat derriere and big stomach, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 x 32 (medium leggings and pants at Old Navy and H&M). I ordered these leggings in a medium, and they fit perfectly (see my pics). My one minor complaint about these leggings is the small reflective stripe on the lower sides of the legs. I would like to wear these not just as active wear, but also under my winter dresses and sweaters, and the stripe makes them look too sporty. Overall, these are very comfortable, warm leggings. I am happy with the fit and quality, and would not hesitate to order from this company again.

Buy these! I’m not a yoga/workout pants person due to not liking how tight to my leg they typically are. However, I thought I’d try these and I’m in love! I put them on for the first time this morning and am very happy I bought them. I’m 5’5” and usually wear a size 14 bottom. I bought these in xl and the fit is perfect to my shape. Kept me warm/Great Material! I'm about 5' 6" tall and 150 lbs and these fit great! They kept me warm during a chilly softball tournament weekend!

Super comfy! I bought more! Love, love the waist design on these! Super comfy if you've got a belly and flattering. The fleece lining is pretty thin so these aren't super warm but warmer than no lining. The flare bottoms obviously let in some cold air. I'm always cold, so these help me at 40 degrees, but any colder and they would not be warm enough for me. I live in Colorado. I would buy again. I really wish they came in some fun colors, like dark purple or fushia! Update: A year later and I've bought 4 more pairs of these and I'm still a BIG fan. Very comfy. I think I'll spend most of winter wearing these in the house. I gained weight so I had to buy new ones in XL. If you carry your weight in the middle, I feel the waist can be a tad tight. I'm 5'4 and 165 pounds. I'm 5'4 and I feel like the 29" length is perfect, which makes me think anyone taller might find them too short. But I'm grateful they aren't dragging like so many brands! One complaint now that I've bought 5 pairs. 2 pairs were perfect. But 3 pairs had what I consider a flaw in the stitching in the right cargo pocket that made the material kind of ripple or pucker. One pair was bad enough I returned it. I decided to live with it in the other two because it's not that noticeable. One last concern. I think the "wine red" is the ugliest color pant I've ever owned. Lol. I should have returned it but that's just personal preference. I like a burgundy to veer toward purple more than brown. This is more of a rust, depending on the light. I've attached a couple photos to show the color in different light. And also showing the length on my shoe. Fits my 5'4 perfectly. The rust colored ones didn't have the stitching flaw so I kept them even tho I hate the color because they are so comfortable!

Smooth, stretchy material but size up! I received the BALEAF Womens Fleece Lined Flare Leggings in Black. I am normally a size medium, however I ordered a size large as I already own a pair of BALEAF Leggings in Large that fit well. Thank god I ordered up because these are form fitting leggings except for the flare leg. The flare leg is not a tiny flare leg, but it is more pronounced than I expected. I am 140 lbs and 5’7” and the length is perfect! Even though these are form fitted, the material is so smooth and stretchy, so it does not feel uncomfortable. I love the side pockets and there is a flap that is sewed on each end that covers the opening to the pockets to really hold in all your valuables like a phone or money. The crisscross waistband is so comfortable and stylish if you tuck your shirt in. These are warm fleece pants so they are ideal for walking or hiking in the colder months, and it’s a bonus they are water resistant too! They would probably be too warm to wear to the gym or do indoor activities. The only negative I have about these pants is they are tight fitting so it shows your underwear lines. Definitely size up!

Thermal pants! I get cold super easily, so I was excited to try these thermal pants. They are so warm and comfortable! They are basically a stretchy legging material on the outside, but they have a thin but very warm inner fleece lining that makes them very cozy. I love that they have several well-placed pockets. Since I have long legs I wish they were maybe 1/2-inch longer but I think the length will be good for most people. As far as fit goes, they do fit in my usual size but are definitely more slim-fitting than my other Baleaf pants (in the jogger style) in the same size. So consider sizing up if you are between sizes. For reference, I am 5'9" around 160lb and got a Large.

Perfect travel pants These pants are fantastic. I've been a Baleaf fan for years, and am impressed with how long their products hold up and how thoughtfully designed their pants and shorts are. For reference, I am 5'6", size 16W bottoms. Both the xl and xxl fit me, but I personally prefer a slightly looser fit so I went with xxl. As with their other shorts and pants, the pockets are nice and deep. This style has two front pockets and one deeper zippered pocket on the right thigh. That pocket is big enough to hold my large samsung ultra in it's case. The fabric is lightweight, but not thin enough to see dimpled skin under it. At 5'6", the xxl comes down to almost the bottom of my heel, but not long enough to touch the floor when barefoot. I'm happy that the bottoms are straight (not flared), yet are wide enough to roll up and still have wiggle room around my plus sized calves. These pants are great for travel, since they don't wrinkle easily, stretch nicely to move with you whilehiking, & dry quickly. They are not "noisy" pants. The zippers are the same color as the pants, so at first glance they don't scream "hiking pants". Because of that, you can get away with wearing them with a dressier top for dinners out, which really makes them multi-functional.

She Loves Baleaf I got these pants in size medium for my wife to try. She is 5'3 and 135 lbs. Other than being a tad too long on her short legs, these pants fit her very well. They are a pull on pant with no zipper. The fabric is fairly thick and have a nice soft lining inside, so they are cozy pants for cooler weather. Because of the moderate amount of stretch they would still be good for fitness activities. The grey color is a nice neutral option to black. Since these pants have belt loops, a mock "fly" , 2 zippered pockets on the back and slash pockets on the front, they look a bit dressier than simple leggings making them a comfortable option for work or travel. She loves these and I think they look great on her as well.

Who you Hiking with? BALEAF, duh I purchased the BALEAF Women's Hiking Pants Quick Dry Lightweight Water Resistant Elastic Waist Cargo Pants/Black/Tall/Large for my trip to Costa Rica (July 21-26 2024). I WISH I HAD PURCHASED MORE THAN ONE PAIR! The pants were comfortable to wear and easy to pack. The pants kept my legs dry during the two mile trek through the rain forest, pouring rain included. They allowed for plenty of movement as I awkwardly climbed over lava rocks, during our volcano hike. Quick dry? Yes! I washed my pants after each hike and hung them on the line to dry. The pants would be dry by the next morning! For reference, I'm 5'7" and 168lbs (not curvy on the bottom half, lol) The Tall/Large fit a little tighter than I wanted. My second order of the BALEAF Women's Hiking Pants Quick Dry Lightweight Water Resistant Elastic Waist Cargo Pants will be in the size XL/Tall.

My New Favorite Hiking Pants At 5'7" and 135-140 pounds, these Baleaf hiking pants fit comfortably. I can sit, bend, and crouch easily because the fabric stretches. There is a drawstring waist, which is forgiving for women with a bit of a tummy, as well as four zippered pockets: two slash pockets, two cargo pockets on the side of the leg, and one rear pocket. All of the zippers move smoothly; I was even able to operate them with one hand while hiking. The cargo pockets are deep enough to hold my iPhone 15 Pro and still zip closed. There are drawstrings at the cuff. Water beaded up on the pants when I got caught in a light rain shower while hiking. The fabric is very lightweight and comfortable, washes well and hang dries quickly. I haven't had an opportunity to test abrasion resistance yet. I'll probably buy additional pairs of these pants.

Comfort & cool Bought these for a hiking trip. I am 5’4” 175 lbs carry most of my weight in my butt & gut. Size medium/regular fit me like a glove. It was 90 plus degrees yet I didn’t swelter in them. Walked thru briars & wild berry bushes that would grab the pants yet they did not tear or rip. Just Buy The Pants! I bought two pair (in grey and black) of these pants for a 3 week backpacking trip in New Zealand. These pants were perfect for the variable weather conditions, and withstood rainstorms and tramping through mud pits. They were super comfortable, regardless of how cold (as low as 2 degrees fahrenheit) or how hot (~75 degrees fahrenheit). I love the breathable netting, pockets, and adjustable ankles. As for sizing, I'm 5'3" and about 115lbs. I first ordered them in Small, which was way too baggy for me. I then exchanged for the XS, which was a much more flattering fit, with plenty of room left to be comfortable. Next time, I might try the petite XS, as these pants are slightly longer than ideal, so I have to wear extra high on my waist. I should also note that after hiking 100 miles in just these 2 pairs, I experienced zero chaffing the entire time. TLDR: Great pants for variable weather conditions, water resistance is excellent, consider ordering a size down, excellent quality for the price.

Just Buy The Pants! I bought two pair (in grey and black) of these pants for a 3 week backpacking trip in New Zealand. These pants were perfect for the variable weather conditions, and withstood rainstorms and tramping through mud pits. They were super comfortable, regardless of how cold (as low as 2 degrees fahrenheit) or how hot (~75 degrees fahrenheit). I love the breathable netting, pockets, and adjustable ankles. As for sizing, I'm 5'3" and about 115lbs. I first ordered them in Small, which was way too baggy for me. I then exchanged for the XS, which was a much more flattering fit, with plenty of room left to be comfortable. Next time, I might try the petite XS, as these pants are slightly longer than ideal, so I have to wear extra high on my waist. I should also note that after hiking 100 miles in just these 2 pairs, I experienced zero chaffing the entire time. TLDR: Great pants for variable weather conditions, water resistance is excellent, consider ordering a size down, excellent quality for the price.

Perfect pants! Okay so Amazon suggested I get an XL but I went with a L based on reviews. I am 5’0, 165lbs, and wear a 13 in juniors or 12 in womens. The large (petite) fit perfectly. I wore them for the first time today. This morning when I walked my son to school, it was 36F feels like 33. This afternoon when I picked him up it was 71F feels like 71. I was just as comfortable in the afternoon as I was in the morning. The cut is perfect. The length is perfect. The pockets are big enough for things to actually fit. The material is light and athletic but doesn’t make swishy noises. I have enough room that I could throw on some baselayer leggings or tights if I needed. My only complaint is that there aren’t enough colors in the petite. I might try a normal length though since I like them so much. These are worth it!

Perfect 💚 I loved these pants! I am 5’1”/125lbs on a good day and I ordered the small, the length and waist fit perfectly. They looked great, kept me cool, and fit perfectly. If you are a petite lady looking for a good pair of outdoor pants order these asap!

Great for the tropics! I wore these to Costa Rica, during the rainy season. They are water resistant but not water proof. However they dry incredibly fast, lightweight, and excellent coverage from the mosquitos in the jungle. The zipper pockets kept my keys and phone in place while swinging through the trees! I wore them hiking, zip lining, and they’re still nice enough to wear to dinner with a button up top. Highly recommend! I’m 5’11 and 186lbs. Ordered the XL

Have to wear as high rise or the crotch fabric irritates thighs I ordered the Baleaf in dark grey in a Medium. I’m 5’8”, ~120 lbs, usually a size 4 (My measurements from 2 years ago: Bra: 32A/B - 34 A/B, Waist: 65cm, Hips 92cm). Although they are cute, these are meant to be worn high waisted. When you do, they are comfy but the butt is less flattering. I prefer wearing my pants on my hips unless they are leggings in which I don’t mind them being high waisted. However, if I put the waist band at my hips, the crotch becomes low and the fabric rubs against my thighs and irritates my skin. Could I wear these high waisted? Yes. But Id like the option of being able not to. They do have some great features. The material is very lightweight and I’m sure would be quick to dry as advertised. The pockets are also very spacious and zippered (two hip pockets, one left thigh pocket, one right butt pocket). They also have drawstring cinching ankle cuffs and a drawstring to tie at the waist if you need extra support in addition to the elastic waistband.

The BEST! I bought these pants for my trip to Mexico. I knew it was going to be extremely hot and humid. I had gotten heat rash on my legs a few years back in Europe from wearing improper pants and didn’t want that to happen again. I absolutely LOVED these pants! They are lightweight and breathable. I liked the high waist and the fact that I could draw the strings at my feet to keep the mosquitos off of my legs. They fit me so perfectly and I felt very cute in them!!! I am 5’7 / 120lbs and bought extra small. I can’t speak as to whether or not they are water resistant but they are so thin and light weight that I know they would dry very fast if they got wet. I highly recommend these pants and can’t say enough good things about them!

Surpasses expectations I bought these pants as loungewear and to have something comfortable for traveling. They have surpassed my expectations. I have worn them several times and they wash up like a dream. They're lightweight but feel substantial. They'll pack well when going on trips. They're a little like parachute pants from the 80's but are a bit thinner and not quite as stiff. As for fit, I am 5'5" and weigh around 215. I bought the XL and they fit beautifully. I plan to buy more, definitely recommend! Edit: I purchased another pair if these pants and there's apparently a problem with their sizing. The first pair I bought was a size XL and fit perfectly. The second pair, size XL, were snug. They also had a flaw in the stitching. I did an exchange and received the 2nd pair, which were also snug. I returned them, again, and ordered a pair of XXL. I received them today and they're marked 3XL. I'll be keeping this pair because although they're a little big, it's better than snug. So, I suppose the very first pair I bought (steel gray) were perfect, and this second pair (suntan color) has been a struggle.

A+ Water Proof Outdoor Pants, Hands Down The Best! Love these pants. Absolutely waterproof, and well made. The best I've ever purchased for outdoors/kayaking. Water all over front legs and It never came through the pants. These pants dried quickly. Black Pant Size Med. Petite 27" L 132lbs 5'3"

Great pants! After getting bitten by mosquitos, gnats, and no see'ums when I am out gardening, I looked for some long pants made out of the quick dry, cooling fabric. I am short, 5'3, and weigh 166. The legs are long on me but with the draw cord are fine. I have large thighs also, but the large fit me perfectly. They wash well and dry quickly. They are no hotter than shorts, and if wet, they feel even cooler, so when I get really hot I will get the hose out and wet my clothes to cool off. Summers are really hot on the Georgia coast and the bugs are vicious, but with these and my mosquito netting under my cap and my long sleeve shirt I don't have to slather down in sunscreen and bug spray, and I don't get all scratched when I work. When I ride my bike the air just seems to go right through them, keeping me even cooler than the shorts I used to wear. Highly recommend them.

Comfortable, lightweight,perfection These fit absolutely perfect, you could use these for yoga with no issue, they are not restricting in any of the “problem areas” us ladies have like thighs and buttocks. I am a slender build but still have enough junk in my trunk to testify:) the length hits perfect on me to cover my ankles and the very top of my hiking boots. I did see a lot of people comment on the length being too short. If you wear an extra small but 5’7 or taller these may be too short. I purchased for an upcoming 3 day hiking trip so I have not tested the water resistance but I am confident I will continue to love them. Highly recommend so far

And they are super soft and comfy, perfect for layering with leggings underneath or to wear on own. I bought these for the Iceland trip. Tall girl approved - Comfy and functional These are exactly what I needed - cute, comfy and functional - for travel days and lounging. I was skeptical because there wasn’t much info on fit for taller women, but I’m comfy with the length of these. I was actually looking for a slightly cropped/ankle cut so these are perfect. I ordered a size M - I could’ve fit a size S as well but I prefer a roomier, more relaxed fit that isn’t too tight.

Not Good for Petite! I loved the pants. They were just what I wanted for camping. The material is lightweight and comfortable to wear. The issue is I'm only 5'1" on a good day. I thought the elastic at the bottom would work to go to the correct length. Unfortunately, when I tried this they looked like clown pants. Having this problem most of my life I looked at the bottom to see if I could shorten them. I had a better idea that I've used in the past on other jackets and pants. I folded them to the correct length and stitched right above the hem. I just need to cut and finish the edge with a stop stitch above the hem. I would have given the pants a 5 except for the length issue. I wish companies would realize we aren't all 5'7" or taller!! Pictures are in process - I need to cut and stopstich.

Great Exercise Pants for Curvy Women These pants are great for women with thick thighs and a bubble booty. The drawstring waist takes care of any gap, but the elastic waistband does great on it's own for hugging your waistline and not having gap. This is a pant that is great for hourglass shapes. The pants stretch just enough after wearing them for a couple hours to give you a little more room in the thigh. They are very comfortable, pajama pant level of comfort. They are thin, so panty lines and lumps are visible. With a darker color, that may help hide imperfections. They are great for working out in so far. Hopefully, they will be great in the summer months outdoors too. I ordered an XL in this pant. I'm happy with the size. I'm in between a 14 and 16 size pant. (16 is more comfortable for me in the butt and thigh, but the 14 fits better at the waist) I'm 5'9". The length seems perfect for me.

My favorite! These were kind of an accidental purchase, they’d been in the cart to be considered and ended up overlooked and bought, and I’m so glad. They are my favorite cozy pants. I have a different pair of similar pants and I far prefer these because of the way the waist elastic feels. They are light and have held up to my shenanigans for a few months now. I personally like to wear them lower, but you could really pull these up quite a lot. I’m 5’2 and a little less than 145lb. This is the fit with them tucked into my knee high DMs A few notes: -no pockets. There is a linen-y feeling liner that I’d assume is to keep the sheer nature of the fabric under control. It’s a perfect opportunity for a big, baggy pocket, but instead it’s just some extra white fabric that drapes around the hips under the elastic gathered bit (which is approx as long down as my hand is from heel to the tip of my middle finger) and it’s disappointing. It is easy to just put your phone in the elastic and for me at least it holds relatively well for just walking around the house and baking and whatnot, though I wouldn’t trust it if I were to do something outside. -there is an odd seam somewhere near below the knee that makes these really difficult to just pull on, your foot gets caught in it. I end up having to sit to fix it. It’s understandable to create the shape but I feel it could be adjusted to preserve the silhouette and feel and also be easier to pull on. -the ankle is very small. I wear knee high boots so I wear large socks as well and I absolutely have to put the socks on before the pants because I physically can’t pull the ankle back up more than about halfway up my shin. -I exclusively wear these as pants and the strings that are intended to go around the neck are really annoying. I guess I could just snip them, but I think it would be nice to have them maybe clip on? Like an adjustable bra strap, a wide hook and loop, or something similar. -my favorite thing about these is that they’re very light and stay off my skin, but if you’re trying to have pants that are warm or feel like you’re actually wearing pants, these don’t do that.

Paper thin I was excited to try this when the price dropped by $2. Took a chance and placed an order. Overall I like the way they look. I’m 5”8 and I can’t wear it as a dress. Too short. That’s fine. The waistbstrings whic can be used as a neck strap when worn as a rompers isn’t tighten around the waist. Overall I’m happy but the fabric is too thin you can see through depending on the lighting.

Design needs improvement I ordered your Bangkok harem style pans. I love the style but there are 2 major flaws in the way they made. 1.They are advertised the way that you can pull them up to your chest and over your boobs. It is not the main reason why I bought them, but I still would like to provide a feedback. I am 150 lbs, 5.7 tall and I was not able to do that. Because it would not pull up my caves . This pants are so tight around the bottom of my legs that I can’t even go halfway up.. But as I said that was not the reason why I bought the pants, I just really like the look of them. 2. This flaw is more major and pretty much a deal breaker for me. I do love the material, and I love how thin these pants are and I don’t mind that it’s see-through at all. But why in the world the inside material is the different color from the pants?! My pants are black and the under skirt is white. And because the pants material is see-through everyone can see the white under skirt. That would be fine if it was black, but it’s white and you can see that it’s white. And it’s so much more noticeable during yoga practice in a well-lit room than it is in my picture. That’s to me is just not putting enough consideration and care into making a quality product. So please please please make the under skirts the same color as the rest of the pants. The way it is right now males it look very silly and cheap

Fun to wear I have a similar pair of pants, so I was familiar with how they can be worn -- with either the tie in the front or back. I prefer to initially tie the pants in the front, and then bring panel between my legs to the front, and tie them in the back -- especially with the pattern of these pants. It looks more finished with the pattern unobstructed by the tied. However you choose to tie these pants, they're nicely made with lightweight rayon and all the edges seamed. The instructions say to hand-wash in cool water and not tumble dry. I'm 5'2" and a bit round (size 10). You can see the pants reach the floor, and the straps are long enough to tie around my waist, in the front and back. They would be shorter on a taller person. I'm looking forward to wearing them over a bathing suit or after I get out of a hot tub... or when out-and-about on a warm day. They're light enough to not feel hot, yet provides protection from the sun. The label also says, "carbon neutral," which is cool. I'm guessing the factory where the pants are sewn is environmentally friendly.

Very nice pants! Ordered size 2x. I'm 5'10" 230 lbs and they fit. I was afraid they would be too tight around my pudgy waist but the shirring is just right. I usually can't stand elastic around my waist but these are comfortable. They are just a little bit too short, showing quite a bit of ankle, but for this type of pants that's OK and it looks good. They do not have in-seam pockets, just a patch pocket on the side. You can wear them backward or forward to put the pocket on whichever side you prefer, but having no other pockets for my keys and wallet mean they are not good for me to wear anywhere requiring keys or wallet. The print is beautiful. I looked over them and they look like they are put together well. They are supposed to be made of bamboo rayon, a fabric I know nothing about. They are not sheer at all. The weight of the fabric is like a thin silk. They feel dressy. I would be hesitant to wear them casually like to work or school (where jeans would be appropriate), but they would be great for around the house where they won't get a lot of action or alternately to something where you stand around looking pretty. They definitely feel like something you would want to hand wash. However, they are lightweight so they will dry quickly just on a hangar. I bought several different kinds but this one came in today. Will update if there is any issue. My mirror has a scratch across it. Sorry if it makes the photos look weird. Normally I eschew having my picture on the internet but I found it very to see the fit from another buyer. I have a weight loss goal and these pants will fit as I work toward it, so that's a big plus. (pun intended)

The print is beautiful. I looked over them and they look like they are put together well. They are supposed to be made of bamboo rayon, a fabric I know nothing about. They are not sheer at all. The weight of the fabric is like a thin silk. They feel dressy. I would be hesitant to wear them casually like to work or school (where jeans would be appropriate), but they would be great for around the house where they won't get a lot of action or alternately to something where you stand around looking pretty. They definitely feel like something you would want to hand wash. However, they are lightweight so they will dry quickly just on a hangar. I bought several different kinds but this one came in today. Will update if there is any issue. My mirror has a scratch across it. Sorry if it makes the photos look weird. Normally I eschew having my picture on the internet but I found it very to see the fit from another buyer. I have a weight loss goal and these pants will fit as I work toward it, so that's a big plus. (pun intended)

Fat bottomed girls - do not fear! Sure, these look great on the skinny models, but I was so worried about the fit being too small, the rise too low, or that I would look fat/show bulges in these pants. But they are so pretty, and there were enough positive reviews from heavier women that finally said wtf and bought these. So glad I did!I am 5'5" and 170lbs, kinda bottom heavy, waist 32", hips 43", and wear a size 12. I wanted these pants to be loose and comfortable and would rather go over sized than under, so I went with a XL (supposed to fit sizes 16-20) - this size fit me perfectly. They are just barely loose enough to be comfy, not too big at all, and the rise is just enough that I can wear it up to my belly button, and they are long enough to reach my ankles. The last photo shows the pants pulled down a little lower to just below my belly button - not as flattering. The elastic at the ankles is not tight either, feels just right.It's true that the fabric is thin and sort of see thru, but because these pants drape well they do not look see thru when on. The only thing I worry about is that they might rip like I saw in some other reviews. The seam looks strong enough though, so hopefully they will hold up!

Buy the pants-No buy 2 I am 5'8 tall and weigh somewhere between 175-180 lbs. My height is due to my longs legs and a shorter torso. My weight for the most part, I believe to be pretty much evenly distributed. I usually wear size 8-10 jeans, or Med-Large spandex or athletic stretch pant, or a size 10-12 in a suit. I purchased size L and they are so roomy without being HUGE and puffy! I consider myself to be a large woman, and when I put on big items I feel it makes me look bigger than I already am, so I tend to wear my clothes more so fitted, not skin tight, but "fitted"... These Harem Hippie, Boho-Yoga or Palazzo pant ties comfortably above my waist ( belly button), full coverage in the back with plenty of room bending over, doing squats, or home. The length lays relaxed below my ankle complete with elastic to keep pants off the ground. I will order another pair in a small size (just a little more fitted than before) and wear a cute ankle strap or wedge slide shoe, with a solid color fitted top or crop top, when I finally go out with Bae for fun!***Please note*** I opened this package and they smelled horrible. I could clearly see they were perfect and new, but the chemical processing must be gassy or sulphur maybe? Idk.... The instruction says to hand wash in cold. I machined washed cold with like colors, and hung them dry. The material did not shrink, fade, colors did not run and the stitching held perfect.

I feel like I’m not wearing pants! These are probably the most inexpensive pants ever.....and the most comfortable!! They are so comfortable I literally feel like I’m not wearing pants! These are my new favorite lounging pants, and they definitely passed the sleep test. They are great for summer. I’m sure you could wear leggings under them for cooler seasons. I usually wear a size xl in pants...I’m 5’6” and 187 pounds and I carry all my weight in my hips thighs and butt. The XL was perfect! They should fit baggy like pictured. Otherwise I’m sure you might get an embarrassing butt rip while moving around in public. Honestly if these pants don’t last long, I wouldn’t be too bent about it. I mean I would be out $17.00...but they seem sturdy enough that if you follow care instructions they should hold up. Minus 1 star because they have to be hand washed and line dry otherwise they will shrink and fall apart. Kind of a double edge sword there, because if they weren’t so delicate, they wouldn’t be nearly as comfortable...so hand wash needed for this level of comfort. Did I mention that I feel like I’m not wearing pants?!

Getting ready to order another pair!!! I love, love, LOVE!! These pants!! I pretty much always have these boho, breezy pants as pjs because they are also perfect for running errands! I am 5'3, 126lbs and ordered the medium. It fit perfect; maybe even a little big depending on how tight or loose you like your clothing. I like mine a little baggy and comfy and the medium was perfect for that. As you can see the elastic band is very forgiving and doesn't really cut into your skin like some. I was also hesitant about the bands on the ankle but they are super loose so you barely even feel them against your skin, but they also keep the pant hem from rising up. Keep in mind that there are care instructions included with the package. Since these pants are handmade you should only hand wash them. I did throw mine in the washer and hung them to dry and they were fine, just may wear faster that way. And my favorite part of these pants- the pocket!! Deep enough to fit my hand, and roomy enough for a cell phone or such.

Finally got around to ordered a Barco brand scrub pants and OH MY GOSH - I don’t think I will ever go back to Greys brand scrubs even though they are made by Barco. These are SO comfortable. They feel like a dri-fit type of yoga pants. So much room to stretch when bending down. I love how comfortable these are for being scrub pants! I will probably cut the strings shorter on these though because they strings are stretchy so when you pull the strings right you have a huge amount of string leftover that just hangs there - that is the only part that I didn’t care for but that is something very small - not a big deal in my book at all. Don’t think twice about getting these - just buy them already! They fit as expected. I am 5’8” and about 150lbs give or take some pounds. I normally buy the talls because I wear clogs which are really tall so the length is perfect. I wear a small in all my other scrubs and a small in these as well and they fit perfect.

Super comfy! A little long but love them! Bought 3 pairs and they are SO comfy. I originally ordered XS (I'm usually an XS to S and other barco smalls were too big on me) but the XS were tight. Exchanged for a S and they are perfect (pictures are from the XS order, not S.) My only issue with my first order is that I ordered 2 pairs of blue and 1 pair of black bottoms. I posted pictures so you can see, but one pair of blue drags on the ground and the other pair is too short?? I compared the tags and they were identical. Both regulars. The shorter pair had to have been mis-marked and actually a petite because the black pair is the same size/fit as the longer blue ones. Thought this was a little odd. I’d assume this isn’t a common occurrence, but I’d suggest definitely trying on ALL of them before you cut tags and wash them. Once I exchanged for a small they are awesome and all the same length! They do run a bit long so if you’re in between petite and regular, I’d go with petite. I’m 5’6’’ and a solid regular and found them to be a little long. With tennis shoes on they are okay though. Overall, I’d recommend these pants!

5’6 Height 210-215 pounds Update: Came back to order another pair in XL and they too fit wonderful with a little extra room. If you are large to XL either size will fit, if your weight and height is the same as mine. Did I mention very slimming and flattering on the body. **************************************************** These scrub pants were made especially for me. I’m 5’6 height and between 210-215 pounds with curves everywhere. Did I mention stretch, flattering and comfort… yes these pants have it all. These pant legs are tapered to slim/straight at the bottom with side slits. The length of these scrubs are just about 31 inches long in the large. The fabric is so nice and smooth wearing them out on a casual day lunch or night time comfort early dinner would be quite acceptable. Curvy girls these scrub pants will be your best work uniform, running errands and casual lunch meet ups. I can’t say enough except more colors are needed like hunter green and espresso (chocolate). Giving a 5 star review on anything is a big deal….so don’t hesitate…you really need to buy these… you will be very happy you did.

Super Sporty, Silky, & Long-Enuf in Petite Size I'm short - 5' - and while I greatly appreciate that companies are making petite-length pants, some of them are going a lil' bit overboard with the shortness. When pants are too short for me, they are not going to please the legions of shorties out there... Anyhow, these Sketcher pants are perfect with shoes on & off. The waistband is nice & snug, but doesn't cut into my "softness" - that's what I'm calling my back-fat and love handles, lol. I'd prefer to have cargo pockets on both legs, but the one is a double pocket, not that I'm going to stuff it full, that would look too bulky. I bought the Vitality Charge top, but it had weird, gathered pockets that didn't thrill me. The Vitality Spirit top looks great with these, as well as Koi's Becca - both are good for shorties - not too long!

So flattering!!! These jeans are gorgeous! And they have enough cotton in them to feel super soft, and enough stretch to feel comfortable sitting and walking in them. They are high waisted and have a nice flare that hits the shoe, just like I wanted it to (I’m 5’6”). They wash fine on medium but I let air dry because I don’t want to risk shrinkage in the dryer. I went with my “regular” size, but could have probably fit in the one below, but less comfortably. So I would suggest go with your regular size unless you are hesitating between two, and in that case go with the lower one. Also: the color is gorgeous!!!

Faux leather, actual comfort I'm fairly certain this sort of garment is ordered for looks, not comfort. I'm weird that I want both.After taking my measurements, I ordered a size up to ensure I'd still be able to breathe and not just be an uncomfortable sausage in a black pleather casing. This was a good call as they fit well without being suffocating.I'm about 5'8", and the length and hip to waist ratio are both appropriate - that's often a challenge, so I love when it works. The fabric is slick looking, which means it may not hide a variety of imperfections. (I saw imperfections, my partner drooled. Take from that what you will.)They're soft, pliable, and flexible enough that moving and sitting in them was easy without suffocating. The fit is close enough to look good, and not to where it feels like it will fall off. Overall, a good purchase that will be happily used.I initially rated these as a 4 after a quick try on - I'm raising it to a 5 after an evening's wear as they're more comfortable than I realized from a short time. They are quite warm. For me that's a plus, but it may not be in warmer climes and times.

So cute & comfy!!! I was honestly shocked that these arrived in as good of condition and were absolutely wearable! I am 4'11" approximately 130 lb and I bought a size medium and they fit perfectly. I did have to put on a decent size heel but with the heel I didn't walk on them and they didn't drag the ground at all. The inside lining is super comfy too! The only reason I can't give these five stars is because I wanted them a little bit more elasticy around the waist to give a little more suck in power and because the sequins did rub a little and get caught on each other where my thighs rub together on the inside. I think the rubbing is a fix that you could probably craft with some sealant or maybe fabric adhesive. I got compliments on these all night long and I could have stayed in them for hours! I would say these are a must purchase for any party!!

One the black pants, the white lining shows significantly at the top of the waist (which I would have covered with a belt), but also at the bottom of each leg (which I had no option to cover so this is the primary reason for returning them). 3. They're short... I'm 5'8" with a high waist. I was so excited that these actually did hit my waist (which is uncommon) but disappointed when I looked down and noticed how short the legs were on me... which means when I put shoes on they would be even shorter.... Whole these didn't work great for me (I opted to return them), I think they could be a hood option for someone shorter. They are comfortable and not itchy

Fun, good looking 1-time use! These had a LOT of stretch and were SHORT with a saggy crotch area. Fun & Frilly Pants! The - Blooming Jelly Women Linen Pants Summer Vacation Outfits Wide Leg High Waisted Pants Palazzo Flowy Pants Trouser 2024 – are fun and frilly pants that make me happy when wearing them. I love the way the fabric feels on my skin and moves when I walk. They’re not the most flattering style of pants for my fuller figure but I don’t care - I like them. The only somewhat negative comment I have is the sizing chart lists the measurements of the pants themselves and not the body. And they don’t seem to be that accurate in my opinion. I ordered the size XL which according to the chart the hip measurement is 42”. When I measured the pants I received while flat, I measured 52 inches at the hip. The X-Large does fit me but if I would like the pants to look on me as they do on the model, more roomy and gathered like a skirt under the elastic yoke area, then I should have ordered a larger size or two, if it were available. Regardless of all that, these pants are lovely and I’m very glad I chose to purchase them. For sizing reference, my Bust Measures 44”, my waist measures 37”, and my hips measure 47”. I’m 5’4” tall. I purchased a size XL.

These pants are just as cute as depicted in the description photos. The material is a thin but opaque, lightweight fabric with a little texture that drapes beautifully and doesn't wrinkle easily. They are NOT linen, as some of the lines in the item description suggest. The bow in front is sewn to either side, and is not a drawstring, while the rest of the waist is smocked and very stretchy. As for sizing, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 170 pounds, have a 36C bust with narrow shoulders, a short torso, and a wide but flat derriere, carry most of my weight in the middle, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 pants (medium size pants and joggers at Old Navy and H&M), or a woman's 10 or 12 in dresses. I ordered these pants in the "large" size, which I think was the right fit for me (see my pics). While I don't think these are the most flattering pants for my apple shaped flabby stomach, they are super comfortable to wear with no pinching at the waist, and I can see myself wearing them often as soon as the weather warms up. Overall, these are cute, comfortable summer pants, and I'm happy with the quality. I would order from this brand again.

Super comfortable and nicely made. These pants are as described and do not disappoint. My pics are very poor quality but it does show the approximate fit on my 5'3" 128 lb body. Cute and comfy! I love these pants!!! I ordered them for a trip to Amsterdam next month and I wanted pants other than jeans. I figured I'd give these a try because they seemed to get really great reviews and I found a pattern I love so why not give them a shot. When opened the package, the leg part looked a bit big and I thought they would look like Aladdin pants (LOL) but they don't seem so big when they're actually on. They're roomy but not too big and the material is light but comfy and soft-ish.I love how these come in a variety of prints/colors and are super easy to coordinate an outfit! I plan to wear these with a black tank or black tee and sandals (like I'm wearing in the photo) but I could totally see myself wearing a pair of flats or even heels to dress them up if I wanted to. I also think these would be comfy enough to wear all day while I'm sight-seeing, visiting museums, strolling around town, or going to a flea market.I typically dress in darker clothes but wanted something different, something funky for my trip to Amsterdam. I love bohemian, though I never thought I'd actually like these so much! It might be hard to tell in my photo but they are a perfect dark blue and black floral/mandala type pattern (the color is actually called "Mandala Blue"). The pattern looks exactly how I thought it would be (or is even better!) in person!For reference, I am 5'9" and about 180 pounds. The length of the pants is just right, a little above my ankles. Not too long and not too short even for a taller person. My only beef with these is that I think they look better high waisted however doing so gives me a little bit of camel toe which isn't ideal so I have to pull them down a bit more to my hips for them to be more comfortable (or maybe with a different tee or tank they could work better for me high waisted). I wish the waist part was a bit stretchier or longer, which is the only reason why I gave them 4 stars, but this is definitely not a deal breaker. I was more concerned about these being long enough for my liking and roomy in the leg/thigh area which they very much are.Aside from the high waisted fit thing (which is probably more on my body than the pants themselves), I am absolutely obsessed with these! I didn't expect to love them so much but I really do. They are effortless, super cute and versatile! I can't wait to wear these! I'm so glad I took a chance and tried something I might not have if it weren't for my trip. I will definitely be wearing these into the summer well after my trip to Europe! Thanks so much for a great item!Update: washed these once and I am noticing a few lines on these where the print has faded or the black bled through the fabric? Not sure whether I'm explaining right but they are definitely not wet spots from water as they're a straight line. I did not dry these in the dryer, I let them hang up to dry so I'm not sure why there is a blemish in the fabric. My OCD is killing me knowing these spots exist, but I don't think they would be noticeable to anyone else and I still love these pants.

These are my new favorite pants and as soon as I am done writing this review I am promptly ordering more in different colors. Easy to dress up or lounge around in. Fabric is perfect thickness for summer but it does wrinkle easy; nothing a trip through the dryer won’t fix. Says handwash (who has time for that?)but I threw it in a cold wash with the rest of my clothes and they fared well. I am 5’5” and 190lbs (and losing) and the large (10-16) fit perfect and I think they will still fit well as I lose more weight.

Diggin' my I Dream of Jeannie Pants! I just got them, so too soon to tell for the long term, but right off the bat: the material and fit is super cute, comfortable, and flattering. (For reference- I tend to be a size 8/medium and am about 5'4" tall and I got the 2-10 size) The material does seem a bit cheap so I can see how they would tear like in other reviews. Good thing I am not a gymnast or flexible because I could see them tearing right open if you did the splits. Would buy again if they keep the price low/affordable since they are obviously not meant for long term use. Will likely launder in a bag and on the gentle cycle in attempt to keep them in tact for awhile (fingers crossed!) Update: even being very careful with washing- got a tear right after the first use. Such a bummer since they seemed great otherwise. Would only buy again if priced under $20. Wouldn't pay more since you cannot guarantee more than one use.

I always bought direct and even included them in my clothing line before I sold. The original pj pants durable and LONG. I am 5'11" and I hate short pants. I was super happy to see these on Amazon for faster delivery. When I received the new ones they are just off. It is as if someone took 2 inches off the bottom! Way short and how the took it off leaves the pants with oddly wide ankles. The black and white buffalo check is the old, the blue plaid is the new. Boxercraft you can do better...you HAVE done better.

I’m a dude, and these are the perfect 90’s jeans. I just wanna preface this written review and say that yes, I’m a dude, and believe that this is a great 90’s look jeans purchase regardless of your gender. The men’s ones out there are just horrendous and I’m tired of buying and then returning (Amazon and in-store like Levi’s or GAP), and the ones from Temu never fits me because of the horrible sizing. I’m 5’9 with a size 30 waist and an athletic build (I wear size L or XL depending on brand for men’s shirt). After numerous research, I find that there some dudes out there still that still swears by women’s jeans only. Since why not? I used to wear tight skinny women’s jeans back in my emo-phase days in the early 2000’s anyways, so I decided to check out this baggy version that is in-style nowadays as I really like the style. I ordered a large and they fit okay, I could’ve sized down to a medium though, but the large is still good with a belt on. These are stretchy, lightweight, and comfortable. However, just like the women’s jeans back in the day when I used to wear the skinny ones, I’m still disappointed that women’s jeans doesn’t have a big pocket like the men’s jeans do. Why is that? Oof. I wish that would change as some of the ladies out there have these mega tablet-like size phone, and with my iPhone Pro Max, it was sticking out and when I sit down, or shift to a weird angle, they slide off. Same thing for my wallet. Overall though, these are the exact jeans I wanted and highly recommend.

Versatile jeans, loose legs without being frumpy Great jeans! I’m 5’1” 145lbs so a little stocky, and bought a medium. I thought the waist fit was a tad small but once I wore them a while, it relaxed a little and now fits well - though I probably won’t run them through the drier in case they shrink. I love the high waisted style - the loops are large enough to fit a nice belt and ALL pockets are actually pockets ☺️ I have to hem them a little since jeans are usually long on me which I don’t mind. I like how you can get a more loose flaired end at the bottom or cuff them for a more tapered look (see images). I appreciate the looseness of the legs while still making my behind look good and not frumpy. Just know that these don’t have any stretch to them, and if you have lower belly fat like me, this won’t do a great job of flattening/hiding it.

sizing might be dependent on which color you choose i am 5'11", 145lbs. I wear size 29 pants ~9 in US sizing. if you're tall you know shopping for pants online is always a gamble where more than half might end up as capris or high-waters. I am pleasantly surprised with these. these are the first pair jeans not labeled as TALL that actually make it past my ankles. I think the sizing discrepancies might be dependent on the colour you get. I got large and they are a bit loose instead of being tight like I was expecting from the reviews. material is okay. it's really thin denim so it's very comfortable but I have my doubts if it'll hold up over time. button feels secure, large pockets w really smooth fabric on the inside.
