Baggiest jeans I've worn in a long time, but FUN, received compliments, fairly comfy! I've been wearing skinny jeans and straight leg jeans for a long time, so I decided to 'step out' with these Ripped Baggy Wide Leg Jeans! They are regular denim, non-stretchy. I ordered my usual size 4 and they fit pretty well. For reference, I am 5'7" tall, 130#. The length is perfect, the waist is pretty good (very high-waisted) although a little snug, and the butt fits okay (a little loose). They are baggy and wide from the upper thighs all the way down to the wide bottoms. I'd say the legs are super loose and comfy, but the waist is what is snug since it's so high-waisted. The amount of rips is perfect, and I think necessary to off-set the bagginess and amount (lots) of material that these jeans have. I was able to stay in them all day, although would have been more comfortable if the waist was not so snug. I love the way these jeans look in the front, but do not love the way my butt looks. I guess since there is no design on the back pockets and since the butt is loose, it makes my big butt look big :(. I'm giving these jeans 4 stars as I wish the waist was slightly looser to be more comfortable, but I wish the butt was tighter to look better. Glad to own these fun new jeans though! My teenage daughter saw me wearing these jeans and said, "What are those? You are turning into me!" (And she approved!) Thank you!

Teen daughter likes these My teen daughter is 5’4”, 118 lbs, and has 27.5” waist. She wears size small or 4 pants. She got these jeans in size medium and she’s happy with the fit. The waist is snug but it is not too tight. The hips and legs have a wide relaxed cut. The pants are a bit too long for her to wear with flat shoes so she usually folds them up unless she’s wearing heels, platforms, or bulkier sneakers. There is no fabric content tag. The fabric feels like a quality heavier type of denim with a very small spandex addition. These jeans have a little give but they are not stretchy. The stitching is nice on the exterior and has some small issues on the interior but none are bothersome and don’t compromise the durability of stitching. There was also a piece of plastic foil stitched under the exterior waistband, perhaps a leftover of some kind of a tag but I was able to rip it out.

For curvy girls - these only need minor adjustment for great fit Due to my shape, I struggle a great deal to find pants that fit. Usually my only options are granny styles or looks that are too risque for work. I checked the size chart and ordered a 12 to fit my hips, knowing it would be big in the waist. Of course, the stats help when reading reviews, so: 29.5" waist and 45" hips, 5'5" tall and 160 lbs. You can see in the first photo how loose the waist was. A quick adjustment with my sewing machine fixed the fit, and overall I'm pleased with how these pants look. Eventually I may have a go at letting down the hem to get a little more length out of them. For us ladies with significant curves, these pants require very minimal tailoring to achieve a lovely fit. At this price point, I'm thrilled to get such a professional look. One star off because they're extremely plastic feeling inside (though not outside). Overall, I'd highly recommend for curvy girls with basic sewing skills.

Curvy girl approved! These slacks are curvy girl approved. I usually wear a size 12. I ordered a 14 because you just never know when shopping online. I wish I had ordered the 12. These fit pretty true to size. They have a good amount of stretch in them as well so you could probably even go down a size if you like a tighter fit. They have a pretty high rise so no butt cracks will be on display. There are no belt loops so if you’re like me and order them too big, you will likely be pulling them up through-out the day. Finally! Not too wide, not too skinny 5”6, 205ish, size 18 plus. Have been looking for black pants to wear to work that don’t have ridiculously wide legs or super skinny legging types that emphasize my chunky upper legs if my top doesn’t go down to my knees. Found them, yay! Great price, too. Length is perfect. I’m buying several pairs because after 2 -3wearings the knees seem to get a bit baggier than other (more expensive) pants I’ve previously bought. Probably the one “con” that I have. However, 2 pairs of these cost me what I’ve paid for 1 so I’ll rotate them more. As with all of my work pants I prefer to cold water wash & hang to dry. They seem a bit wrinkled when dry but the wrinkles are gone by the time I get to work. Overall very pleased and I may even buy some in the different colors!

Very comfy, but the fabric is a fuzz magnet! These pants are incredibly comfortable. I'm currently fitting in a size 16 jean and the XXL size fit me exactly as I would hope. The tie on the front is just sewn on as decoration and is not actually a drawstring. I don't find that I would need a drawstring because the waistband fits nice and snugly without being tight or restricting.I'm not a big fan of the pleat down the front of the legs. Comodísimo - Fresco (un poco largo-5’2”) Debo decir que este pantalón ha superado mis expectativas (tanto que ya pedí otro color). Es increíblemente versátil, ideal para usar durante todo el día, en el trabajo, y también para salidas nocturnas. La tela es ligera y transpirable (aunque está forrada) perfecta para los días calurosos, pero también se ve lo suficientemente elegante para un entorno profesional. Lo que más me gusta es su comodidad; el ajuste es perfecto, permitiendo libertad de movimiento sin perder el estilo. Además, el diseño es moderno y atemporal, lo que facilita combinarlo con diferentes prendas y accesorios. Este pantalón se ha convertido en una pieza esencial en mi guardarropa. Es práctico, cómodo y adecuado para cualquier ocasión. Lo recomiendo encarecidamente a quienes buscan una prenda versátil y de alta calidad. Tomen en cuenta que mido 5’2 y me queda un poco largo-nada que unas sandalias altas no puedan resolver.

Super cute wide leg pants These pants are super cute pants and true to size (I'm a size 4 and ordered a small). The color pictured here is the black. They have an elastic waist with ties which is for those that usually need tightening at the waist. I like that they have pockets on each side that are pretty deep. The legs are super wide (bigger than bell bottoms) and flowy and the perfect long length (I'm 5'6" and the bottoms land right above the floor). I would suggest wearing these with a tighter top to offset the width of the pants, unless you are going for super casual look. The material is a bit thin (not see throughy) so these are better suited for warmer environment. The only down side I had so far was these are a bit static, I guess because they are polyester, so I need to find a way to keep them from sticking to my body as I walked (see picture three around the top). I could definitely see myself wearing these pants in white or a fun pattern at a beachy place. Would be perfect for that!

Size up!! Update: washing them did not lengthen them which is a good thing, technically. They kept their shape pretty well and did not shrink. They actually feel much softer after washing, too. But if you are 5’2, I don’t recommend the smalls unless you like your ankles being exposed when you walk and sit. . I just recieved my pair of MEDIUMS and they fit sooo much better. No twisting of the seams and they hang perfectly. I am actually changing my rating to 5 stars now because having them in the right size is a game changer. They hang beautifully. They are the perfect length, the material is cozy and definitely warm enough for winter. Ok, so my pictures. If the black color seems odd, it’s because I altered the lighting to show details. The black pants are a nice rich black color..a pure black with no blue/brown or grey undertones. I have one shot of the inside crotch area. I always want to know if there’s a big lump there that will irritate me. These are nice. The seams of the crotch/gusset are barely noticeable when I’m wearing them. The next pic shows the waist. There’s exposed stitching hanging out of the seam that if I were to leave it there, the pants would start to lose strength there. Next shot, I brightened the pic so you can see…the seams that run down the outside of the legs are totally lopsided. One pant leg is normal (my right side) and the other is twisted. This is moderately noticeable when I’m wearing them. I feel like I constantly have to tug that right leg fabric back towards my rear. Next pictures are of the fit. I am 5’2, ~126lbs with short legs. These pants are a size small. The waist fits nicely. There’s no need to even tie them as they stay up just fine. I do notice that the pants don’t come up as high on my back as they do on my stomach and this is kind of annoying to me. I prefer my pants to be even all around on my waist. The pants fit well on my thighs and flare out nicely at the knee but this is where I notice the twisted seam the most. And as for length? I feel they are about a half inch too short for me. They look ok when I stand still but when I move or walk, the pants ride up and show my ugly socks. I don’t like being able to see my socks when I’m wearing long pants. Lastly, the fabric is not really buttery. It’s a mostly polyester mixed with spandex fabric which means it’s similiar to a sporty type material. When I run my hands on the fabric, it feels kind of like the little microfiber rags you get with your new glasses or cell phone protector. It feels rough in a silky way…if that makes sense. Now, I don’t not like these pants. I just feel that the listing specifically calls them buttery. I don’t find them to be buttery..but they do have a luxurious feel to them. I just hope I don’t have a reaction to them, as I have had major issues with spandex causing my allergic rashes lately. I actually just ordered a medium size to see if maybe they feel so twisty and weird because I ordered the wrong size? I guess we’ll see. I also wonder if maybe washing them will bring out some softness. I plan to wash on cold water and hang to dry. I’m hoping that doing that may lengthen them a bit, too. And even out the stitching. Sometimes fabric is weird like that if it hasn’t been washed yet. Lol I will update after washing and also plan to review the medium size once I get them. As it is, I would happily pay $7-10 for pants like this particular pair to wear around the house. Also, if washing them makes them hang better, they would be perfect to wear out, as well. With this length, I would wear them with sandals or flats but not heels or boots.

I have already bought three pairs of these... Most. Comfortable. Pants. EVER. I am 5'10", 165 lbs, and ordered a large. I do wish they were a little bit longer, but that's my burden for having a 33" inseam. I bought the kitty face pairs first, and wore them for about a week (washed them twice) before ordering two more pairs. I've been in quarantine for the past 2.5 months, and pretty much all I've been wearing is pajamas -- these have replaced all of my previous lounge pants now. I removed the drawstring because it seems to be mostly decorative anyway (and my cat likes to attack it) and they still stay up fine without. They don't stretch out and get baggy, even after wearing them for several days in a row, and so far they'e held up really well in the wash. The best part is that the waist is non-binding -- I can't stand elastic waistbands that cut into me -- and these are comfortable whether they are hiked all the way up on my waist or pulled all the way down around my hips, and they don't "migrate" when I move around. BUY THEM -- you won't regret it!

Great value for a set of three casual pants! I ordered this set of three BVNSOZ Joggers for Women for myself. I definitely could use these pants to round out my casual, around the house wardrobe, and give me more pants for just hanging out or doing yoga in. In the description, it says these are sweatpants, they're definitely not sweatpants. These are fairly thin and are made of a lightweight material, but they're still very useable and very comfortable pants. Because these are fairly thin I don't expect them to last for years and years but because if the higher spandex content maybe they'll surprise me! I can be definitely see getting a couple of years wear out of them at any rate. These are soft, comfortable, and great for just hanging around the house. For reference, I normally wear a size XXL out women's size 18 these pants fit very comfortably. These pants are made of 92% polyester and 8% spandex, which is a higher spandex content than most pants of this style. The high spandex content makes them stretchy, lightweight, and resistant to perspiration, which makes these a great choice for my daily yoga sessions. Unfortunately, the spandex content also tends to make them more apt to make you sweat and tends to make the fabric a little less breathable. doesn't allow your skin to breathe easily, and is sensitive to heat. They're perfect for warm spring and autumn days, and cooler summer days when you're at home. Unfortunately, they're really too thin to wear out unless you have a top that comes down below. These pants have an elastic waist with long drawstring. They're true to size, long enough without being too long for people who are probably 5'6 or taller. They also have pockets on both sides and taper to a short loose cuff at the ankle. I've washed these pants a couple of times since getting them. These pants wash up nicely in cold water without much bleeding, no shrinkage and, so far, no pilling. Is best to dry on low heat or hang to dry especially if you want to ensure there's no shrinkage. This set of three women's joggers for under $30 is an incredible value! These are very soft, very comfortable pants for light workouts or just hanging out doing your daily household activities. This set of three pants would be perfect for anyone who wears these kinds of pants because they're a great value. Highly Recommended!

If I wear them inside of boots or something though they would be okay so you don't see how short they are.

Good for the price I got this pair first, and then also the white one. First you see what they look like with straight bottom. They become longer after being ironed, out of the package. I'm 5'10", so it is an acceptable length. However, It's still a little short for me, when I walk and move around and I found them not very flattering. I had this idea. Purchased a pair of shoelaces in similar color; cut a small hole on the 'wrong' side of the bottom of each pant leg so that it is between the very bottom and the horizontal seam, and then I threaded a shoelace in each, to tie them around the ankle. And these pants become what you see on the last picture, with a tie on the outer side of the leg. This style is more attractive to me, and works better with this garment for my height. I did the same with the white pair. And I wear both frequently. I haven't had a chance to wash them yet, since I wear them gently and mostly for walks. I heard from other reviews that these pants shrink and pill. Shrinkage is the bigger worry. I have other linen pants that shrink and stretch with washes and wear. My plan with THESE pants is to iron them while wet after the wash, and stretch them out, then hang-dry. In my experience, this works with clothes that are prone to shrinking. At least for a while. For pilling/fuzz, I keep a fabric-shaving machine. It's been a great help for many years, and there's a great selection of them right here on Amazon. Hope this helps.

I'm 5'10 and a size 8. And the navy ones fit perfect and needed no tailoring. My old black slacks got lost so I purchased these in black and I hoped they'd fit like the Navy ones. If you are used to teen type sizes like H&M or Forever 21 than yes go down a size but if you are used to woman's brands like CK, Kenneth Cole, or Jones New York, than stay to your true size. At first I was nervous because they were a little tighter in the thighs than my navy ones but I wore them around the house for a day and they stretched to fit perfectly. These slacks do have lots of stretch which is great for comfort but know that they will give slightly once you're wearing them so a little tight when you first wear them is good. Otherwise they'll be baggy and nobody wants saggy slacks butt. They are long. The cut is perfect for women with a little hip. Not boot cut but wide enough at the bottom to give you a little balance. You can see in my photo that at 5'10 they go all the way to floor and at 39in hips fit pretty perfect. There's a little pull in photo from way I was standing but that's it. Good luck!

Great for “pretend” business attire for pandemic era These pants from CAMBIVO are very comfortable. The material is not see through, but you can very much see outlines of everything, including the pockets, contents of the pockets, and underwear lines depending on the thickness of the underwear. I still love having the pockets, as at least I can put stuff temporarily when I need hands, but the pocket lines are also very thin/stretchy and isn’t meant for holding phones and wallets all day. The shape is like professional pants but the material is very much of a yoga pants, and there’s stuff like their logo on the waist on the back center that makes it give away that it’s not real office pants. I actually works very well for me at the moment, where I usually had to dress full professional before the pandemic but as we are not really “back” and not having clients come into the office much yet, it’s sort of business casual / more flexible dressing. So I can look like in dresses for the office but really be sitting in yoga pants 😄. I’m 5’1, wearing 3 inch heels in the photos, which fits the length without having to cut, and 135 lbs. I’m wearing Medium, which fits well except strangely, I have to pull the waist up now and then a little bit. I say strangely because I have a bit of a belly so this must be generously cut on the waist. I fit size 6~8 in Express/Banana Republic. I machine washed and dried in warm settings and it came out good. Also, it doesn’t attract dog fur like crazy. It still sticks, especially when my dog rubs against me causing static, but it’s not like cotton where it’s a fur magnet.

Very Comfortable This is a pair of wide-leg pants. The waist is elastic and provides a snug fit. I liked the ribbon around the waist. The fabric is very loose and flowy, but it has no sheerness to the look. I found the inseam to be a bit long for me. I am 5'2, so I need to wear these with shoes that offer a heel or platform to not walk on the bottom of these pants. I recommend this for someone a bit taller than me. Look-wise, I find these to be suitable for the office or a casual outing. I don't think these pants flatter me. I think these would look better on someone who wore a medium or small as opposed to someone who wears larger sizes like me.

So Comfy, Almost Feel Like Corduroy, Great Lounge Pants! These pants are so cute, they feel amazing (almost like corduroy), and I am very happy with them! - Great quality, thicker but not too warm, great lounge pants - Look as pictured, knitted texture, wide leg - Plenty of stretch in the waist & some in the legs - Crotch hangs a little lower than I would prefer, but that isn't a deal breaker for me in lounge pants - Nice, deep functional side pockets - Size seems true. The small fits me perfectly, and I wear smalls in everything. For reference, I am 5'3", 125 lbs, 28" waist, 36" hips - Machine washable, but lay flat or hang to dry, hit with the steamer to remove wrinkles The design at the bottom is very unique, and I wasn't sure I would like these pants, but they are too cute, elevated lounge pants, and the quality is fabulous! I will be needing more colors. Definitely recommend!!

Tight on the thighs and major waist gap This doesn’t fit like my other Carhartt pants. I have the same pants in a denim blue and they are perfect. These, in contrast, are a terrible fit. So these scrubs have gone kind of viral in the ER I work at, I was influenced and finally bought a pair. Let me tell you, they are sooo comfy and soft which I didn’t expect! The fabric is like butter and super lightweight,l which is perfect for running around a busy ER. They also look more flattering on than I expected - somewhat form fitting on top but more relaxed fit in the legs and thighs, my butt doesn’t look saggy which I was worried about. The elastic waistband is perfect, looks clean from the front but scrunches in the back; can adjust with drawcord as needed. Lots of pockets which I don’t really need in the ER but would have loved as a floor nurse. Very durable. I’ve washed multiple times and air dried so haven’t had any issues with shrinkage; no pilling either. I will say I sized down to a small/tall bc I didn’t want them super baggy and for the length I like my scrubs to basically touch the floor and am always worried they’ll look like high waters on me so if you want a more fitted look I’d size down. If you don’t mind them being more loose stay TTS. I’m about 5’5 and 145lbs for reference; smaller on top with bigger booty and thighs. Great value for money highly recommend!

Insanely comfortable, a little long. These are my new favorite pants. I did have to hem them a good 3.5” on the legs because I’m 5’2” and very clearly not that tall. Quality is amazing, you really stretch your dollar then you get these. I’ve seen people pay twice as much for this quality and still be satisfied, so seeing that this quality for this price is amazing. I got an army green color and unfortunately, the closest thread I had to it was Hunter green so the hem is a little noticeable. Color and fabric are just like the description.

My favorte scrubs ever I've held several nursing jobs and have had to order several sets of scrubs throughout the years and these are now the only scrubs I buy!! They are so comfortable, flattering, quick drying impossible to stain, and the color does not fade over time, neither does the quality of the fabric. The only thing issue is that the sizing can be inconsistent with ordering online. Im 5ft2in and 155lbs and I initially ordered a small pant because the smalls fit me perfectly in the Caribbean blue color that I had been wearing for the last 2+ years, however, in the navy which I just ordered for a new job, the smalls were tiny so I returned for a medium (which is my typical size) and they fit great. I also had to get them hemmed because they were a tad too long however I am petite. I read reviews that they run on the short side so I did not want to risk getting the petite sizing however I probably could have to avoid having to get them altered. These are great for women with full thighs because they do not wear down in between the legs like my old Grey's Anatomy scrubs did. They stay looking new for at least a couple years and feel like you are wearing pajamas. As long as they continue making these scrubs, I will only buy these, they are my absolute favorite ♥️

Comfy! The pants itself are very comfortable. I got an XL and I’m 5’9 and they are a little big on me. This is my second day at work using them and I went to tighten the string and I felt a pop. Trial and error I own 2 pairs of the tan Carhartt Original Fit straight leg pants, purchased from Buchheits. One size 14 and one size 16. They sit at / above my belly button which was a selling point because I had a csection and I still have pain. I attempted to purchase another pair from Carhartts website but they're sold out. First, finding the exact pant is hard because what is says on the tag is different than the website. I found the grey color on Amazon and ordered a 14 since that size fits me better. Received them and I couldn't even button them. No where close. Also, the labeling said "loose fit" not original fit. I requested the size 16 which came promptly in the mail. They sit lower than my other pairs, which okay, I could deal with, but the legs are HUGE compared to my tan ones (pictured). I looked at the tag and I was sent 16 Tall. I'm 5'4 and these looked ridiculous on me. They also seemed more bootcut than straight leg. I double checked my order and made sure I ordered the standard 16 regular. I'm disappointed because Carhartts sizing isn't consistent, my boyfriend has the same issues with their clothes. We both work construction and its nice having womens durable clothing but it's a pain in the ass to find a pair that works. Returning and will continue to look for the right pair.

I love flannel pants because they are so soft and comfortable to lounge around in. These pants are no exception - they are a soft flannel with a gentle elastic waistband (it is not binding around my waist) and they have a drawstring to get the perfect fit. The blue color is darker and less bright than the image that shows on my monitor, which I am happy about. The image online looked a little too bright to me - these are more of deep blue that has some brightness to it.I normally wear a size large in clothes (I have a 32"-33" waist and 40" hips), but ordered these in a size XL because I like my loungewear to be oversize and super comfortable. I would have fit in a size L and the XL is oversized on me, so I would say these are true to size. These pants are several inches too long on me (I am 5'-3" and normally wear a 29" inseam), so I need to roll them up. I will hem them at some point. Other than the length (which was noted in the listing, so I am not deducting any stars), these flannel pants are exactly what I was looking for.Casual Nights Women's Flannel Pajama Sleep Pants, Soft Plaid Pjs Bottoms (Blue Green Plaid, XL)

She's about 125 lbs. & I ordered her a "medium" in these hoping they'd be a little longer. The fit is just what she was looking for; baggy & cute without looking sloppy & most importantly- they pass the sit down test where they don't ride halfway up your shin once you sit lol (pictured). Seeing as how these were made of 100% cotton flannel I've been washing & letting them air dry, just didn't want to risk any shrinkage & they come out great.

The Summer Alternative to Yoga Pants! In the winter, I'm always wearing yoga pants when I'm at home because they are so comfortable. I wanted something similar but for warmer weather since I wanted other options besides shorts. After some searching, I found these capri pants and decided to try them because I've owned and liked a number of Champion items. I initially purchased one pair to start and see how I liked them. These pants are capri pants so they go down below your knees. They have an elastic waist, a drawstring, but no pockets. There is a Champion logo embroidered on the bottom of the left leg. I ordered the black color and they are made in El Salvador of 100% cotton. Washing instructions say to machine wash cold with like colors and tumble dry low. I've washed and dried these a lot of times already and never had a problem with shrinking. For size reference, I'm 5'6", 170 pounds with a bit of a tummy left over from my twin pregnancy. I usually wear a 12 in pants and ordered a large which fits perfectly! They are so comfortable that I ended up ordering two more pairs so I now have three black pairs of these! They are soft and lightweight enough for summer. I feel comfortable wearing these at home, while out running errands, and even when going out to a casual dinner. I've paired them with tanks and t-shirts and feel these capris are so versatile. My thighs rub together so there is some pilling in the thigh area but not enough to make holes. These capris aren't thin enough to be see-through which makes me feel more comfortable. I'm so happy I found these capris because I wear them pretty much every day! They are soft, comfortable, and the summer alternative to yoga pants. I feel they are priced very reasonably for the quality you get and I would recommend them to anyone looking for capri pants!

I ordered the black color and they are made in El Salvador of 100% cotton. Washing instructions say to machine wash cold with like colors and tumble dry low. I've washed and dried these a lot of times already and never had a problem with shrinking. For size reference, I'm 5'6", 170 pounds with a bit of a tummy left over from my twin pregnancy. I usually wear a 12 in pants and ordered a large which fits perfectly! They are so comfortable that I ended up ordering two more pairs so I now have three black pairs of these! They are soft and lightweight enough for summer. I feel comfortable wearing these at home, while out running errands, and even when going out to a casual dinner. I've paired them with tanks and t-shirts and feel these capris are so versatile. My thighs rub together so there is some pilling in the thigh area but not enough to make holes. These capris aren't thin enough to be see-through which makes me feel more comfortable. I'm so happy I found these capris because I wear them pretty much every day! They are soft, comfortable, and the summer alternative to yoga pants. I feel they are priced very reasonably for the quality you get and I would recommend them to anyone looking for capri pants!

Comfortable and flattering cotton joggers I was somewhat skeptical when I saw the low price on these Champion 100% cotton jersey joggers, but I took a chance and ordered a pair in size medium. I am 5'3', 127 lbs (or so), and typically wear size 8-10 in bottoms and these run true to size. These cotton joggers have a slim-cut that doesn't bag out at the seat or crotch, and the pockets do not add bulk. The ribbed cuffs cover my ankles without much pooling at the bottom and could easily accommodate taller gals up to about 5'7' The 2" wide elasticized waistband match the cuffs with a comfortable and flattering fit that doesn't bunch or roll. The drawstring it just a cute design detail. The material is a heavier (sweatshirt) weight cotton, that isn't fleece or terry lined, which makes it ideal for warmer climates year-round and suitable for crisp autumn weather in cooler climates. Fabric Content: 100% cotton; granite: 60% cotton/40% polyester; oxford: 90% cotton/10% polyester. These truly exceeded my expectations and you can't go wrong for $13. I plan on stocking up while they are still available at this price. Highly recommended!

Color/Style A+, Not Longlasting/Durable, Thin, Long 140 5'5' - I ordered a S, M, and L. Large was too big, I returned it. Style is nice. Color is as shown. Quality is ok, material is thin. Durability is less than a year. Medium fit a bit loose but comfortable, good fit after washed, looser with wear. The waist is still too stretchy, so I have to wear a belt all the time or it will fall down and my belly will stick out. It is long on me, about 2.5 inches below my ankle, so I have to fold it over or wear platform shoes for the material not to drag on the floor. I really like the flare and I have meaty thighs, so the stretch is good. I wear the medium most, i have had it for 8 months and worn it liberally, the denim on the inner thighs is already wearing down like shown in the pic, and will have a holes very soon and won't be wearable anymore. So the quality and durability of these is only about 8-10 months if you wear them often and your thighs touch. So that is diappointing. The small I also kept. Small was still large at the waist that it will fall down without a belt & can create a muffin top of you are curvy in the belly. The small fit snug, material stretches, so fits my thighs, but is a bit snug which is not my favorite look. Still long, so I fold it up when wearing flats and leave it long when wearing platforms.

Really Wrinkled At first I was excited about these pants but after sitting in them for a minute I think I might have to return. They were very wrinkled right out of the bag but I figured that was normal and that those would come out after wearing them for a bit. However, I noticed that they became much more wrinkled after sitting in them. I tried adjusting them in different ways and changing my posture but I wasn't able to figure out a way to sit without wrinkling them. I know the pants are a linen mix so some wrinkling is to be expected but these just made me look disheveled. Extra details: I ordered the khaki color and I would say that the actual product appears significantly darker and a little more yellow in person. My waist is 26 inches and I'm often in-between XS and a S, so I ordered a Small just to be on the safe side. They were definitely a little big but still wearable, but if you're unsure I would consider sizing down. The length is great (I'm 5'6" and wear a 'Tall' in jeans) and I love the pockets! I tried them on while wearing bright red underwear and they weren't even remotely sheer which was great!

Not flattering for me Oh man. I don’t know why I thought these might work for me. I have a protruding belly like I’m still pregnant (I’m not, lol) and these types of pants usually don’t work. I ordered an XL, which technically is the right size for me, but they’re just not flattering on me. I feel like these pants are great if you have a perfectly flat stomach, but I don’t, and it caused weird creases to form in the front where my legs meet my torso. I feel like they just emphasized my width in relation to my height, and looked bulky while making my legs appear shorter at the same time. As for length, I’m 5ft 4.5 inches tall and the hem just touched the floor. The fabric was a little stiffer than I expected, but it seems like it would soften a bit after the first wash.

Moms .. you want these I have been in the healthcare field for 12 years I bought these pants because I’m finally in nursing school and the scrubs that they gave us at the school are terrible they’re just so uncomfortable they are the drawstring unisex scrubs, like the black sheep of all scrubs. I bought these pants and tried them on and in the 12 years that I’ve worked in hospitals these are the MOST COMFORTABLE, MOVABLE, STRETCHY scrub pants I have EVER worn. I have three children, my twins and my baby girl all via C-sections so my scar is very uncomfortable with different pants and I still have a little mom pooch from my last one I hate when scrub pants roll down or when you do find pants that are “high waisted” but are so tight it’s just uncomfortable however these pants stayed in place all 12 hours I wore them and they are extremely stretchy but they stay where they need to be. They definitely are made for the curvy women. I can’t say enough that these pants are the most comfortable pants I have ever worn moving around pulling on patients bending over and squatting down you can move so freely in these pants if I could give these pants 10 stars I would I added pictures to show you how they look and on the pant legs as well. You can see that there’s a drawstring so if you buy them Long they’re definitely long enough to look professional with Dansko‘s or clogs but also if you like joggers you could probably buy them short and pull on the drawstring and they’ll kind of be against your ankle rather than hanging over your shoes (check out my pictures) either way these things are the bomb they are so comfortable I cannot get over how comfortable they are I only ordered one pair for clinical and after wearing them for one day I ended up ordering more and I ended up ordering a bunch for my actual job because I don’t think I’m gonna wear any other pants for work & school! THE ONLY SLIGHT DOWNFALL that some people may find is the material it’s like the polyester material it’s not cotton and I don’t think there’s any cotton except for the waistband I know some people sweat while these pants are good for comfort they might not be the best option if you tend to sweat let’s be honest I’m just gonna say it, if you tend to easily get “swamp booty” then I would recommend wearing 100% cotton panties with these bc honestly the material is so clingy and soft it’s won’t ride up your butt. I’m just keeping it real ladies.All in all I love these pants I’m so glad I ordered definitely 100% worth every penny!!SD: I am 5”10 200Ibs “slim-thick”

Petite Small vs. Petite Medium I found the sizing chart to be somewhat confusing, so I have notes on both Petitie Small and Petite Medium! I posted a picture of me wearing the Petite Small and the other picture is me in the Petite Medium. It's difficult to tell by the photo but the Medium is looser. Petite Small (actual pants measurements): Waist 30" circumference Hips 38.5" Calf 13.5 Hip to crotch: 11.5" approx Petite Medium (actual pants measurement): Waist 31" Hips: 40" Calf: 16" Me: waist 28" Hips 35" Calf 13" The small fits on me, but snug in the calf. The medium also fits. The waist band is thick 2.5" but does have stretch. The actual material of the pants does not have stretch. Overall, the medium is looser like sweat pants but looks fine with a fitted top! I wear both the small and medium! A lot of my coworkers wear these in a tighter fit especially if they are pear-shaped and these pants look like leggings on them. :)

These are flattering, comfortable, have some stretch, and the material feels very durable compared to other thinner scrubs. The pockets have a weird zipper design that goes all the way to the hip but I kind of like how it looks. (I also got the cherokee longsleeve underscrub top and absolutely love it! great flattering scrub pants I am obsessed with these scrubs. I am 5'4" with a 30" waist and 39" hips. I am usually a medium in pants and generally prefer a looser fit, but with the waist not being adjustible I decided to order both a Small regular and Medium petite. The mediums were just too loose in the waist and the moment I bent down the waistline fell with it. The length on the petites look better to me with my height, but I have a very athletic build and the cuff was too tight over my muscular calves. On the other hand, the size smalls fit PERFECTLY in the waist. I was worried they'd be too tight in the hips/rear but the fabric has a surprising amount of stretch and I've been very comfortable with them at work (I work in a veterinary clinic so I spend a lot of time squatting and wrangling animals). They did shrink ever so slightly with my covid-compliant hot wash/hot dry cycles (the photos of the small size pants were taken post-wash) but again, still an amazing fit, highly recommend

Pant cuff wasn’t a jogger fit 👎🏼 I’m 5’2” and weigh 149 lbs, ordered a Medium Petite. The pant fit wasn’t too tight or too lose, length was fine. It’s as comfortable as any regular scrub pant. The material is much thicker kinda like workout pants. I agree with other reviews that said that the zipper digs in you’re skin if you zip all the way up. Makes sense for fashion but not convenient or comfortable in that aspect. I am returning them because unlike in the display pictures, the pant cuff wasn’t snug around my ankle. Seemed like they were stretched out or defective from the company. If you look at the picture I uploaded, the cuff if almost the same width as the rest of the pant leg so what’s the point of ordering a jogger pant if it doesn’t look like one. Cherokee is a good brand but not sure if I’d order this type of pant from them again.

My FAVORITE scrubs! The scrubs are super flattering and comfortable... when you finally get the size right. I had to go through the hassle of ordering a couple pairs and returning the sizes that didn’t work for me. However, I found that and XS top and S Petite bottoms fit best. Gorgeous color! I love the fabric and how comfortable these are! I am 5'7", 140 lbs, and small was a touch tight. (That's more on me than the pants.) Worth it, size correctly I got a small, petite. I was hesitant based on reviews. However, I’m 5’1 & thought it would be fine.. While it will work, it’s short if you’re bending down and doing anything. I prefer my ankles to be covered and the length is pushing it. I also measured my size but the sizing doesn’t factor in the ankles riding up. Unless you’re under 5 ft / have shorter legs than I wouldn’t recommend getting the petite. If you have a bigger bottom, definitely size up because, I have a small bottom and it fits good. Overall pants are super cute next time I’ll just get the small regular length.

They’re just okay, but convenient Received these today so I haven’t worn them to work yet but from what I can tell so far, I like the material. It’s kind of like windbreaker, no swishy sounds, but light weight & thin. As a healthcare worker that moves between buildings I don’t know how they’ll do in the Indiana winter with these but they’re light enough to wear later leggings if necessary I’m 5’5, 170lbs, 31 weeks pregnant (all butt & belly) and got a large, could fit medium but I work with SMI males and don’t want to accentuate anything. The elastic waist is so nice for maternity but I feel I can wear these comfortably after without wasting money on maternity scrubs. The ankles do not truly fit like joggers, the ankle part is actually really wide and not as fitting as I’d like. Again, this suits me because I need loose fitting, but not baggy workwear. Overall, they’re okay, I plan to keep them for the convenience but strongly feel they’re overpriced with regard to quality.

My 'Life' Pants! These pants are AMAZING! So confession... I do not work in the medical field (yet! Based on how comfy these are, I might switch careers to one where I can wear them all week!). I was looking for a pair of flattering joggers that weren't sweatpants. I hit the jackpot with these! I am 5'8", 123lbs and the XS Tall fit perfectly = not too tight, not too loose, not too long, not too short. I am going to buy more in different colors!

5’10”, 160lbs - S top, M pants I really wanted to like these. The top is great but the bottoms. What a disappointment. I ordered a small pant to go with the small top that fits great but the small pants were too tight on my butt which was not ok for work in the event of bending down. So I swap for a M bottom: the first bottoms I got, I assumed the ankle cuffs were defective as they seemed very stretched out and loose. So I replace with another M bottom only to find this has to be a design flaw. I don’t have cankles but even if I did, these would be ill fitting. The length, the waist, buttock and thigh region all fit just fine but due to the TERRIBLE ankle cuffs, the pants flop as I walk and make the pants feel dangly. The small size had a perfect ankle cuff fit but going to a M the ankle cuff seems to jump 4+ inches in circumference which affects the aesthetics as well we the overall leg fit.

Runs way too large and no communication from company As embarrassing the photos are of these scrubs on me I am posting to show how large they run as I wish someone had shown or told me :) I made an order of several pair of scrub bottoms (including these red ones) in different colours and styles trying to find the right fit. The colour is a vibrant red and after washing in hot water and drying the colour remains but the size did not change. I ordered a XXS Petite and the pictures are taken 1, as they arrived in a plastic bag 2, the side view with nothing in my pockets but the back looks like it is full :( 3, the extra length and 4 I tied them as tight as possible and am showing that they still have several inches where they slide up and down. I had no communication with the company despite several attempts on this pair, a green pair that arrived without any tags and no bag and note the white pair is EXTREMELY see through! Since I needed something to wear immediately I'm keeping these and taking the loss. I give an average 3 star review because the colour is good and they will fit many people...plus I hate leaving negative reviews even when the company ignores my questions about refunds, returns or exchanges. For reference I am about 5'1", 102lbs and waist is abt 24"and again I ordered the XXS Petite, washed in hot water and machine dried and they are still embarrassingly large.

Could be worse, but could be A LOT better! Just starting nursing I'm finding it so hard to find nice but affordable cute scrubs. Not only that but looking for ones that fit me right is even more of a struggle. These pants however are not the greatest I do like the fit on me though. The fabric is not stretchy it's stiff and not soft and the elastic waist comes up past my belly button. There is also a lot of extra fabric in the hip area that's not needed for me. With that said a small fits me relatively nicely I like my scrubs to fit me more on the snugger side with a little bit of looseness but definitely not baggy everywhere. I don't have to hem the bottom the length hits a little below my ankle hovering above the ground. My measurements are hip: 40, waist: 30, wt: 152 ht: 5'4. I hope this helps some else see if these work for them. UPDATE: I wore them to work once and they were the most uncomfortable scrubs pants I own. I tried to make it work but they were not staying up, kept falling down at the waist. They were super tight in my thighs it felt like it was almost gonna rip when I sat down. I ended up returning them 😑 disappointed....

Long, long, long,but ok I'm 5'5", 140ish lbs, and the small petite fit as expected: fitted, but not skintight... but they were SOOOOO LONG. They still are just barely off the ground with a petite. The material on these is a thick-threaded, stiff fabric, it almost reminds me of the fabric tents are made of, or work coveralls (but thinner), or low thread count sheets... For the fabric being so stiff, i was super surprised with how comfortable these scrubs actually were. Not like your fave pair of pjs or anything, but not bad at all. I ordered both the pants and top on Amazon, and they match perfectly in style, material, and color. (I've included both tags in picture so you can compare item #s). For a $30 set of scrubs, these aren't bad... but they're not as amazing as some of the reviews state. I guess i was expecting more.

Good but runs large I find Cherokee tends to run large. For example, these are a medium. According to the size chart a medium should fit hips 39-41. You can't really see the tape measure but that is measuring 23" across, which would be a 46" hip. Nice fabric, different fit I've worn scrubs for the past 40 years of my life -Old school scrubs, stiff material, drawstring closure, unisex fit. The newer material is nice. It's softer. I like a little bit of stretch in it. I do not, however like a tight fit to scrubs, these are work clothes, for the operating room and not fashion per se. I found a pair of Dickie scrubs that I really liked and of course after 6 months they no longer exist. This is my attempt to find something similar. The waistband is similar, albeit without a drawstring, that is a downer for me because I tend to carry my phone and other items in pockets. Without a drawstring these do tend to be less secure at the waist and they slide a little bit with weightier things in the pocket. I am 5'7 and 190 lb. An extra large slid down without anything in the pockets, but I liked how the legs fit. I returned those for a large and the waistband sits just below my navel and the legs are okay, not tight but not loose fit scrubs. The stitching that comes right across the knee is just weird. When you walk you have that feeling of having long shorts on because the band is not stretchy. The length is good and I like the flare at the ankle. I guess we'll see how the color holds up. Not a fan of the waistband if you were going to tuck your scrubs in, but I've conceded to ordering more Missy fit scrub tops that will be worn outside of my scrub pants.

Ridiculous vanity size Zero stars!!!! I am 5'7 and 158lbs. I am a large on most scrubs as I have to wear white at work so don't like my scrubs to be too tight. I ordered these twice. If people followed the size charts there wouldn't be so many 1 star reviews If you are reading the reviews and saying "oh no! so many negative reviews are saying these pants are huge!" and are wondering what size to order or if you should order them at all... Well I have attached photos of the Cherokee brand size charts so you don't have to wonder. As you can see, the LETTER sizes on Cherokee's size charts don't match up with a standard US size chart. For example, I wear a US size 18 which would typically translate to letter sizes XXL or 2x. However, if I go by my number size, an 18 on this Cherokee brand size chart would have me order a letter size XL. In addition to that, my normal inseam is 33" so I would need to order the XL Tall, because Cherokee size chart says Tall inseam is 33-34" length. It appears that Cherokee's Petite inseam length is 27.5 - 28.5", and Regular inseam length is 30 - 31" for reference. As someone who reads reviews to help me decide if I want to order an item or not, this listing is one that I will not rely on the negative reviews. Most of the negative reviews are people who ordered pants based on their standard letter size, even though this Cherokee brand size chart is sized differently. If you go by the Cherokee size chart, you might have better luck finding a good fit for these pants. I hope this helps a little bit. As for my personal review of this style of Cherokee pants... I ordered the size XL Tall, and it fits just fine. These give a little extra wiggle room which I kind of like. For reference I'm number size US 18, my height is 5'10", and I weigh somewhere around 235 lbs. I have a large butt, and thicc thighs. My inseam is 33". The Cherokee brand size XL Tall in this style of pants is a little bit too tall for me, so I could probably hike these pants up a bit or get them hemmed. These pants cover my bum and are loose enough on my thighs for comfort to move around in. If you want a more fitted scrub pants, these are probably not the style for you. These are comfortably loose all around. I don't work in a field that requires these for work. I actually got them because I needed some comfortable cotton blend pants due to a severe eczema flare up. My legs are covered in itchy skin and unfortunately even some scratch wounds. If you've ever dealt with eczema, the struggle is real. I don't want to live in cotton pajama pants all the time, and would like to leave the house looking like a normal person at some point. These pants allow me to be comfortable and feel normal again. I have to say goodbye to my polyester pants and replace them with cotton blends. I appreciate that these are affordable so I can buy a few of them for my wardrobe update. I hope this review helps someone with their decision.

My waist 37.5" Hips 45" Height 5.4ft. I read all the reviewers comments but not much help to make a good decision on what size should I get. Following the size chart I took XL. But actual XL pants hips was 52" instead of max 49" as stated in size chart. I paid SGD43.34 for the 1st scrub pants. If I chose to return I had to bear SGD20 return shipping fees. So I had it altered and paid SGD15. Size Large can fit but still on the loose side which I dislike, I sent back 3 of them for refund, rather lost SGD20 than get them altered. Finally I bought Medium, It fits nicely except the length still too long. I had them shortened 2.5" Material is soft and comfortable but non stretch. Color similar as advertised. If seller has provided accurate size chart, I wouldn't have to bear SGD47 in total for return shipping fees/alteration. With so many reviews complaining size chart inaccurate, I sincerely hope seller be responsible enough to update and provide an accurate size chart as per actual items sold. Usually 1-3cm difference in actual items is acceptable. For this I gave 3 stars.

Advice for Ladies that are ~5’4” and weigh around 65 kg The product is exactly what you expect. It’s slightly tighter around the waist, it goes above the navel, slightly long and baggy at the bottom. I am 5’4” and weigh 65kg. If you have a small butt then your butt will look pretty flat, it’s not ugly flat tho. If you are a female around my height I would recommend a small short. A medium for a hip size above 35inches would be too large. As for material, the best I can compare it to would be a shower curtain. It’s a pretty thin material.So if you wear a women’s size 7 or 8 in regular jeans I highly recommend these because they are pretty cheap compared to the others.

I Like These Pants I've been looking for pants like these for quite awhile. I wanted lightweight pull-on pants with pockets big enough for a cell phone that I can wear during the summer months. For me, the hardest part was determining the right size. The online size chart indicated that I should wear a size 18P, which is larger than anything I own. Nevertheless, I bought a pair and they turned out to be too big. I knew as soon as I tried them on, though, that I wanted one in every color. So, I ordered two more pairs in both a 16P and a 14P. The 16P turned out to be the best fit, even though it is still a little loose; but I definitely didn't want pants that were too tight. These are good every-day pants for a retired woman in her 70's. I am 5'2" and weigh about 160. I had no problems with making multiple returns.

Great, size down, good quality pants So I just had surgery so I am quite below my normal weight, but I'm 5 foot one and around 105 pounds at the moment and got extra small and they fit perfectly. Quality surpassed expectations LOVE these pants and can’t wait to continue styling them. They’re the perfect height for us petite girlies (I’m 4’11). The material is thicker than expected and the waist band is comfortable. Only reason I’m leaving 4 stars is because sometimes the material bunches up in the back and I found myself pulling/fixing it a few times.

Bought these to take on vacation and seriously they are becoming my favorite pants, what’s not to love, super comfortable and classic styling that will transition to any season, add a sweater & booties, tennis shoes, graphic tee & jean jacket, throw on a blazer & loafers, tank & sandals. The Fabric breathes and although the fabric looks like linen, it’s a wonderful blend that doesn’t wrinkle even after wearing all day and hangs beautifully, looks like your wearing a skirt but we know they are pants with pockets, love that! I bought the small for ref (5’4” 120ish size 2) and the fit was perfect

Size down I took a chance after seeing these pants on some blogs. They are so cute & comfortable. I call them my “clown pants”. I am 5’4” with a 30 inch waist. I followed the size chart & ordered a medium. Way too big. Exchanged for an XS. They fit great. Minus 1 star for cheap, non breathable fabric & misleading size. But you get what you oay for.

Super cute! LOVE! They are super cute on and figure flattering. They are trendy so it’s nice not to overspend as this style won’t last! Materials not the greatest but comfortable enough. True to size. 5’3 - 110 lbs - small was perfect for more. The look is oversized!

Super cute! LOVE! They are super cute on and figure flattering. They are trendy so it’s nice not to overspend as this style won’t last! Materials not the greatest but comfortable enough. True to size. 5’3 - 110 lbs - small was perfect for more. The look is oversized!

Mid Waist, natural waist, Leopard print, size medium, color wine. I love these pants. These jeans are printed, in a blend of rich earthy tones, ruddy and rusty. I love the style. They are a wide leg, cropped length, trouser. The fit is up at the natural waist about navel level. They are not mom jeans and in no way are disfiguring or horrible looking in the bum area. They allow for curves, and allow the curves to show, and are also loose fit and wide down the leg. There is no stretch, 100% cotton, which I love. However they are unforgiving. I used both size charts and they are accurate for me. The inseam is 71.3 cm, which is about 28 inches, HOWEVER, these jeans have a 25" inseam crotch to hem. That is too short on me, but with the trouser, cropped style being stylish right now, worn with boots or funky pumps or sandals, socks, they are fabulously hip in the shorter, ankle revealing length. I love them. I didn't want to have a tight fit with these because I like the loose look and perhaps the options to let them ride low slung. These fit just below the natural waist for me. I am 5'8" 140 lbs, 29 inch to 30 inch waist at the belly button. 38 inch hips, and a 34" mid rise. My inseam is more like 31" in reality and most jeans are 30" regular. I order long length when available. I weight 140 now, but usually weigh 130, so these are more fitted than loose and roomy. I would like these styles to offer longer length, but the baggy, wide and Barrel look seems to come with super short inseams with a crotch that hangs down. Not my favorite. These fit comfortable, however. The colors and the fit shown in the ads is accurate. I did have to be considerate when deciding on size. I have had to be more thoughtful with most jean ads lately to be aware of materials, stretch or no, inseams and size charts. 5 stars!!!!

RAWR!!! I ordered these CIDER Denim Mid Waist Leopard Wide Leg Pants in Wine Red in a Size Small for my 10 year old daughter... and it's giving all the RAWR vibes! For reference, she is 5'1" tall and 110ish pounds. She wears size 14 girls clothing from a Big Box store. These pants fit her great! The quality is EXCELLENT! They are a thick denim material that isn't super stretchy. It is more structured. They are fashionably cut with a barrel leg and mid-waist. The colors are bright and vibrant. The print is clear and crisp. They wash up and dry nicely, without shrinking too much. These pants are perfect to wear to a holiday party, on a fun occasion like a birthday party, or just around town-- every time my daughter wears them she gets lots of compliments on them! Mom and daughter approved... We highly recommend them!!!

I would say these pants are fun, fashion and edge all in one. These pants are a combo of a few trends which offers some versatility. They are wide/ flare leg and land a bit ankle length, the color is like a mauve/wine colored animal print, and the material is denim. It is a good quality medium to light weight denim and non stretch. I am a size 24/25 waist so I got a size XS and they fit great, they are also comfortable to wear. While they are a wide leg pant the waist is fitted. I am 5'5" and the length of the pant is just at the ankle on me but I do have long legs so that is something I used to style these pants, I went with a knee high boot which created an oversized/ baggy look that I loved. These are really statement jeansthat work day or night.

Perfect fit These pants are great. There's a lining like shorts inside and it doesn't ride up. You notice it a little bit when you're walking, like the hem of the shorts, but it's not very noticeable. It might be more visible if you're pretty dark skinned, though. They're flowy and the wrinkles steamed out easily--however, they wrinkle when you're sitting for a bit (I was at my desk half of the day) and those new wrinkles didn't go away when you get up. Not a deal breaker for me, but worth noting. I love that the waist fit me perfectly. There's no elastic, no stretch, and I used the measurements in the at size chart. These will prob be too long if you're short. I'm 5'8" and had on 2.5" heels and they were just above the floor maybe an inch. For size reference, I ordered XL and it's perfect. I'm 5'8", 173#, about a size 10. My mid waist is 34 and my hips are 40 inches.

Cute pants with some flaws I was really excited for these pants and love the way they look online. In person I was disappointed a little with some aspects. For reference I am 5‘,5“ tall and 115 pounds. My measurements are 26 inch waist and 38 inch hips. I ordered the size extra small and it fit perfectly. It was even slightly snug in the waist and hips on me, but not a dealbreaker. I was disappointed online the color is a very pretty light sky blue. However in person it’s a darker, dusty blue color. I already have pants this color and really wanted a light pale, blue pant. The other thing I didn’t care for are the front pleats they’re not very flattering and add a little bit of bulk to me. Finally they were also a little bit short. I’m only 5‘5“ tall and these fell right at my ankles to where I really need to wear flats with them in order for them to not look like high waters. I typically like my pants longer, and to cover most of my shoe. All in all they are an OK pair of pants. I wasn’t thrilled at the color in person is not the same and didn’t particularly like the front pleats but I will still wear them. Hope that helps!

Very big & very unflattering! I am pear shaped, 5’4” 150lbs. I saw reviews that said these pants ran small, and since I am bottom heavy, I sized up to an XL. Big mistake, these pants are huge! Not for me, they make me look huge as well! In the photos, I am shown wearing the A-Brown color option. The pants themselves are well made and the weight of the fabric drapes well, they are unlined but not see though in the darker color. I wish I went with my usual size large, usually in pants I am a size 10/12. Check the size charts and take note of the pant length. If I decide to keep these, I will need to alter the length, waist, and seat. Made in China from 97% Polyester, 3% Spandex the manufacturer recommends machine washing, line drying.

Meh These slacks are ok. They come in a plastic bag to protect them yet mine arrived with a big stain & there’s no tears on the packaging. The sizing is off. I ordered an XL which should fit based on the measurements they have (I’m a 33.01” waist & 41.4” hip) yet I struggled to get them buttoned. I bought these because I would be traveling in a car for several days and wanted to be comfortable. I was very comfortable. I wore them for 4 straight days. I know that's a long time, but no one pointed to me and said " lady do you ever change your pants?". Very comfy walking on the Appalachian trail ( gotta brag as I'm 62yrs. young). As you see in photo the thread is a little loose. I'll just put a drop off clear nail polish or frey check on it for now instead of returning them. I did wash them when I got home inside out and air dried. They look fine. Size was good. I'm five' 5" feet and 130 pounds. I wore them with sandles as I was traveling from FL. to CO. Savannah, GE was wonderful!! If you go stay on the river in the Marriott with the balcony! Any way, these jeggings were great for travel. My daughter said they are good for old people like me. I took her out of my Will. Try these, you won't be disappointed. Update! These jeggings hold up to falls!! I was running on loose rocks when my daughter's dog ( who is no longer in my Will ) cut me off and I fell. I was wearing my jeggings at the time. I landed on one knee and wrist very hard while landing on my side. My knee did not tear the jegging material yet my knee was cut and bruised. I fell hard, broke three toes, and injured my shoulder, but the jeggings are fine! There is just a little place where my knee hit the rocks that made the fabric a little lighter blue but not noticeable to not wear it again. I'm wearing an orthopedic boot for my broken toes for a few months, but I think the jeggings will look cute with the boot when I go to Maine to hike a little bit on the Appalachian Trail. I have good insurance. So again, I would recommend these, although I wouldn't recommend running on rocks with a large dog thinking it's playtime. And I question why would a husband ask "Are you O.K." when finding you bleeding and laying down on rocks?

Runs a little small, but great jeggings 2023 UPDATE - when I say that I made these nearly an everyday staple for the past 2 years, I'm not joking. I wore these almost everyday, washed and dried them in the dryer endlessly. I finally purchased another pair (identical size and color as previously) and I am AMAZED at how well these held up! The wash slightly faded and there is one small spot where a thread loop (decorative only) came out of one back pocket.. that's IT. The fit hasn't shrunk in the slightest. So DO NOT HESITATE to buy these extremely high quality for the price and comfy jeggings!!! I'm always looking for inexpensive comfy jeggings for around the house when I want to feel like I'm still in pajamas but not necessarily *look* like I'm wearing pajamas. Grabbed these in a size Small. FIT I would say these run small. Not insanely small, but in particular the elastic waistband is too small to be comfortable. I'm a 28" waist and generally order everything in XS but this waistband is really tight on my waist. Other than the waistband I like the fit of these leggings. I am 5'3 and they hit just below my ankle which I prefer. QUALITY These are super stretchy but still look like authentic Jean fabric and stitching so it looks good and feels easy to move around in. LOOK/STYLE I like the wash of these jeggings, they're exactly as I hoped for. I don't think that the overly small waistband makes these flattering on me so I will only be wearing these with longer shirts that cover most of that area. The stitching and look of the fabric is an authentic Jean look and not cheap looking at all. FINAL THOUGHTS I do think these run small and if you dry clothes in the dryer like I do, I expect these will eventually shrink and become even smaller. I recommend sizing up at least one size, maybe 2 if you really want to be comfy. I would likely purchase more of these in more colors and medium size next time

This is the first pair of jeggings I've purchased, and I LOVE them!!!!!! They look and feel like real denim jeans! I'm not a fan of 'skinny' jeans, but I decided to give them a go (because I can't find anything similar in boot cut or flare without a hefty price tag). I've seen pairs of jeggings at Walmart that looked.. well... not good lol. I've also seen other people wearing jeggings and you can totally tell that they are, in fact, jeggings. They look kinda cheap? But not these, they look amazing. I've never felt more confident in any other pair of pants (is it technically 'bottoms'?). I'm very happy with my purchase. I will give you a break down of my body shape and weight. Something that I absolutely hate!!! I have fluctuated up in down weight wise my whole life. I have some extra skin as a reminder. But, here goes... I am roughly 5'8" and weigh 240. I am sort of awkwardly proportioned. According to me lol. I carry most of my fat in my belly. I have a hard time finding real jeans that actually fit me properly, because my legs aren't that big. My natural waist is 44, my hips are 54, and my lower hip measurement is 34. So when I try on jeans in a size I can get up high enough with my tum tum, the legs always look baggy and weird. Anyway, I think it's important to remember that I could be standing next to someone that is the same height and weight, and we will not necessarily be the same size. My weight is in my belly, but some are fortunate enough to have there's evenly distributed. I ordered an XL, because I fit in to a XL in yoga pants, and leggings. They fit me perfectly. The length, feel, and fit was amazing. They aren't as thin as leggings, but they aren't as thick as some denim jeans. They structure to them, so they don't highlight every bulge. I hope this helps someone.

True to Size. Nice 4 way stretch Jean Fabric. Comfortable & Flattering to Wear. So Happy these Just Arrived Today!! Came Quickly in The Mail, only took 3 days! I Like the Denim Light Blue Color and the Generous Sizing of the 4-Way Stretch Fabric. I Purchased the XL and its an Extremely Comfortable and generous flattering Fit for my Frame: Height 5'4" Weight 150 LBS. The only thing I dont like about it is the Smell of the Fabric, very odd, almost a slight musty smell but I beleive it will come out in the wash. I saw where some other customers had mentioned it too. I will be ordering more of these. I paid around $20 with Tax. These are the Indigo Classic Denim Leggings made by No Nonsense. They did a darn good job on these, If I had to change anything I would ask for REAL Front Pockets insted of Faux ones, and I would ask for a Powder Blue Color too. Frankly, I am real happy with what I do have and I do recommend them.

Love them!! I love these pants! At 5’5” and 220 it was hard to find cute jeans/pants in the “skinny” style. I needed something much more fitted at the ankle than my big girl jeans! Thought I’d give these a try and I’m glad I did! They are thick enough they don’t look like leggings, just nice enough to wear to work, and stretchy enough to be comfortable! I have now lost 30 lbs and I’m still wearing them! No need to buy every size as I’m losing weight. XL fit then and fits now. I might not have to buy again until I get into a medium!! Those reviewers whose pants split right away were likely wearing them waaay too small. If you’re on the fence, go for it!

Not For My Body Type I thought because these were leggings they would fit tighter in the thighs, hips, behind & ankles. I also thought they would be longer in length & rise. But they are much too large, in those areas & too short for my body type. Because the rise is too short, they fit below the belly button, unless you want to pull them up & have a camel toe. As a point of reference, I’m more on the tall & thin side at 5’9”, 128lbs, 28” waist, 36” hips with thin thighs & not much behind. The fabric is soft & comfortable, but just not a good fit for me. They were so inexpensive that it doesn’t make sense to send them back. So I will donate them instead.

Had several tiny holes- fabric too thin They came with several tiny holes in fabric. I will say this fabric is extremely thin. I tried them on after noticing the holes in order to determine if I should replace or outright return them. They fit really well, true to size (I'm 5'8, 250 & bought 2x), however given the type of fabric - which was Not really denim, but rather somewhere between a fabric stretch jegging and tights - it seemed that if they hadn't already been damaged upon arrival, they wouldn't have lasted more than a couple a couple wears without damage anyway. Opted for the refund. If these had been a slightly more durable or even reasonably decent fabric, I would've kept them because the fit seemed nice.

Save your money and buy from trusted brands! I am nearly 5'11, 200 lbs. and am a size 14, which is a Large, but I ordered an XL based off reviews: It just literally arrived and I will be returning them after I send this review. I was disappointed upon seeing and feeling them, but to be certain I briefly tried them on- being extremely careful not to snag any of the overexagerated cuts with my toes. It fit well and had a good stretch too them, however the cuts aren't reinforced properly and on the right leg knee cut it was already dangling- no thank you! In my opinion these are way overpriced! If you are curvy and have thighs that rub- I can predict they will leave additional holes in the inner thigh area with normal use in minimal time- just my assumption based off the material. For the price- buy yourself some trusted Levi's, Lee's, Wranglers, Old Nay, Gap- even Lucky Brand (on clearance😜). If thinner, more slender, then, yeah, these can work if you're wanting extremely ripped up Jean's, and don't mind paying too much for them... your preference is your own.

Full. Size. Pockets!!! These destroyed jeans are amazing! The front pockets are full size so you can fit your entire cell phone in them. They're very on trend especially for about $35, the stretch is perfect and holds up. I'm 5'6" and 150 lbs and the medium fit perfectly.

defective... The pants have an inner lining, that is comfortable and doesn't ride so that it flows as a long skirt instead of pants. The pockets are also lined, however a "pro" iPhone does not fit in the side pocket as it sticks out a lot - regular size iPhone barely fits but I wouldn't trust it to stay safe in the pocket. They're 100% Polyester and they say "Machine washable" but no iron, no tumble dry, no dry clean so I seriously question how these will look as coming out of the packaged bag, the chiffon is already wrinkled. I have yet to wash them, but I wanted to review first because it is important to check these as mine came snagged and I fear these will tear when I wash them. Overall, they are long (I ordered a size Medium, I am 5'3 and will need to wear them with at least, 3.5in heels to avoid them from dragging on the floor) but they flow like a long skirt so when you see they are pants, they look elegant. With that in mind, the chiffon is snagged (see picture) and it is so delicate that I want to put it in a clothes bag while in the wash but it says no iron or dryer so the wrinkles will be a factor! If they are not too wrinkled or ripped after the wash, I will update... 4/5 stars because they are snagged but they look well made, pockets are for hands only but at right place and it flows beautifully.

SO FLATTERING I absolutely adore these pants. They are so beautiful and make me look immediately put together even if all I pair with it is a tank top. They're light weight enough to wear in the summer, but pair well with a blazer or shacket so I plan to wear well into fall. Also they are SO LONG (I’m 5’10” and pants are rarely long enough but these are- I couldn’t wear with heels but they’re perfect with sandals). Couple of things to note: 1. these are very thin (even with the second layer of liner), so if you are wearing colorful or thicker undies, do a double check to make sure they aren't visible. 2. The stitching isn't consistent, and there were some areas where it came undone pretty quickly (this is the reason for 4/5 stars) - you can see in my picture where the area right under the pocket pulled apart (and this was after 1 wash on declicate+air drying and 1 wear). I was able to sew it back up so that you can't really tell, but it was really disappointing.

Decent pants Although these may not be for me, I do think these pants are decent overall. They arrived looking as pictured in the product photos, and the quality seems good. They held up well in the washer and dryer with no shrinking or other issues. They are lightweight as well, which makes them comfortable in warmer weather. The price point seems decent for the quality. I am 5’4”, 125lbs and ordered a size small. These pants were pretty long on me, and the waistband is somewhat tight, which makes them uncomfortable to wear overall. Unfortunately I know these would be huge on me if I sized up. Although I don’t think I’ll be wearing these, they are definitely worth a try.

Very cute and comfy i love these! I am a fan of palazzo pants because I like flared legs and these are the types of pants you can dress up or down. Pretty comfortable pants and they are lined so no issues with seeing through them. I will say they were a bit more fitted at the top than I expected and the elastic was tighter than other items I have like this, but for some people that might be better. I am 5'8, about a size 8-10/medium for reference. I took a picture with a pretty baggy shirt tucked in and you can see where it showed my bit of bloat, lol, so maybe go up a size if you want a more comfortable experience in the upper section. I washed them on a cold permanent press cycle and dried them on delicate and everything came out great, no issues with snagging or color bleed. Definitely recommend!

Capri S vs Regular XS I am 5' 3/4" and about 118 lbs. I usually wear around size 2 in jeans and size xs or s in shirts. Amazon recommended that I get size S based on my shopping histroy. At first I ordered these joggers in size S, but they were too long for me. I was torn between trying capris in S or getting the XS in order to get basically a full-length jogger that fits right on my short legs. I got both, and here are the results. I hope this helps a fellow short gal out. The green pants are the size S capris. The grey are the XS regular length. I decided to return the green and keep the grey. The green capris are definitely tighter on my legs and they may look ok, but they were just not comfortable being that tight. The XS regular were surprisingly not too tight, and I think they are a perfect full-length jogger for me. After having these a few months, I love these joggers and recommend them. They are very comfortable and look good too.
