Very lightweight pant not made for taller people. I ordered this in a small regular as there is no tall in this item but it is advertised as a 32 inch inseam. I am 5'8", slim build, size 4. My measuring tape says the inseam is 31 inches. The model in the photo shows the pant actually bagging at the ankle, so beware, she must be petite. The pant fits perfectly elsewhere. The fabric is very lightweight. The waist band is wide and comfortable, but the fabric is a little bit "noisy". It rustles as you move and when your thighs touch. Please Columbia, if you can't provide pants in a tall, at the very least advertise the correct dimensions.

Best pants for travel light, if style fits you but don't recommend Black and Stone color. I bought a light grey color Anytime Casual Pull On Pants at a sports shop at $49.99 and it's really perfect for me. I loved them so much so got extra ones on Amazon (also 49.99). I was looking for a pair of pants for my next travel where I'm planning to pack everything in one carry-on luggage only. Pros: - super light weight - super comfortable - breathable in warmer weather - sun protection - less outdoor looking for town/city use - easy to handwash - easy to dry - comfortable material for sensitive skin - not super expensive (not that cheap though) - water repellent - winkle free, to some extent - easy to pull on and off Notes: - tightly made so not suitable for well build person - length on shorter side (and there is no long version available) - very thin material that would show the lines of undergarments - Black color pants gets so much static! (Other color doesn't) - White (Stone color) pants are rather see-through... - as it says "Anytime Casual" pants I don't think it's meant to be for hiking in harsh condition but perfect for light nature events and daily use. I'm around 5'4" and I'm not well build at all. I usually wear S size or US size 2. For me, size S was perfect. Looser at the waist, nice fit around thigh, looser at carf. Super comfortable that I feel like I could wear them forever! I really love the Light grey color, which isn't super light and with tint of warmer color, which gives you brighter feeling but yet it is dark enough not to show color of your undergarments. Color wise, I liked Stone (almost white, slightly beige or yellowish) as well but you can sort of see what you wear underneath. I put them against window and took photo. Stone color pants pass through light and can see the pockets while Light grey did not. Also very interesting but the Black seem to have different material and for some reason it gets so much static. I was trying them on in the department store and it was unbelievable how much static it gets. I had no issue with Light grey, Stone, Nocturnal but Black was disaster. I saw someone posted the photo in the review of black pants accumulated pet's hair and everything and I was wondering why because the Light grey I didn't seem to. After I tried on Black color pants, I noticed it's clearly the issue of Black only. I felt like it even get stuck to my own legs and I didn't feel comfortable at all. I don't recommend black unless you are well-build and don't have excess material and don't mind statics. There are some other Columbia "Casual Anytime" pants and because I loved "Pull On" so much, I tried others too. They were all equally super super comfortable and I loved all but these "Pull On" is the best for me for the ease to put on and off. On Amazon there are also boot cut, capri, short pants and even some dresses too. I depending on where you live but I thought local shops would carry some more variety but it did not. Amazon has more variety. The material is super lightweight, not as noisy or hard as typical GoreTex type of waterproof pants but yet has nice water repellent feature. I'm not much outdoor person and I'm not using these pants for hiking so can't tell from the point of view but the fact that they don't come with obvious outdoor feature like hoops, zipper pockets, reflection line etc, it makes them look somewhat acceptable to wear in even in city and definitely good to wear in town. I have highly sensitive skin but this pants I feel very comfortable. I get irritated even by some natural material such as wool and linen so it's difficult for me to find some cloth with comfortable material. I cut most of the tags at the back of my neck else I get rushes. Same for seams and to any rough material and even some merino wool product irritates my skin. Those pants are like wearing comfortable cotton pajama pants or something. I got more expensive North Face pants at the same time which I love the color and design but I feel irritated by seams. These lower price Columbia Anytime Casual Pull On pants are much much better. I feel like I could wear them forever. I am not planning to hand wash on travel but just in case I would, I tried at home and it's so so so easy. The repellent function is so good that hand washing feels bit funny because it does not absolve water and seems to float on the surface of water. Best pants for travel light, if style fits you but don't recommend Black and Stone color. I bought a light grey color Anytime Casual Pull On Pants at a sports shop at $49.99 and it's really perfect for me. I loved them so much so got extra ones on Amazon (also 49.99). I was looking for a pair of pants for my next travel where I'm planning to pack everything in one carry-on luggage only. Pros: - super light weight - super comfortable - breathable in warmer weather - sun protection - less outdoor looking for town/city use - easy to handwash - easy to dry - comfortable material for sensitive skin - not super expensive (not that cheap though) - water repellent - winkle free, to some extent - easy to pull on and off Notes: - tightly made so not suitable for well build person - length on shorter side (and there is no long version available) - very thin material that would show the lines of undergarments - Black color pants gets so much static! (Other color doesn't) - White (Stone color) pants are rather see-through... - as it says "Anytime Casual" pants I don't think it's meant to be for hiking in harsh condition but perfect for light nature events and daily use. I'm around 5'4" and I'm not well build at all. I usually wear S size or US size 2. For me, size S was perfect. Looser at the waist, nice fit around thigh, looser at carf. Super comfortable that I feel like I could wear them forever! I really love the Light grey color, which isn't super light and with tint of warmer color, which gives you brighter feeling but yet it is dark enough not to show color of your undergarments. Color wise, I liked Stone (almost white, slightly beige or yellowish) as well but you can sort of see what you wear underneath. I put them against window and took photo. Stone color pants pass through light and can see the pockets while Light grey did not. Also very interesting but the Black seem to have different material and for some reason it gets so much static. I was trying them on in the department store and it was unbelievable how much static it gets. I had no issue with Light grey, Stone, Nocturnal but Black was disaster. I saw someone posted the photo in the review of black pants accumulated pet's hair and everything and I was wondering why because the Light grey I didn't seem to. After I tried on Black color pants, I noticed it's clearly the issue of Black only. I felt like it even get stuck to my own legs and I didn't feel comfortable at all. I don't recommend black unless you are well-build and don't have excess material and don't mind statics. There are some other Columbia "Casual Anytime" pants and because I loved "Pull On" so much, I tried others too. They were all equally super super comfortable and I loved all but these "Pull On" is the best for me for the ease to put on and off. Amazon has more variety. The material is super lightweight, not as noisy or hard as typical GoreTex type of waterproof pants but yet has nice water repellent feature. I'm not much outdoor person and I'm not using these pants for hiking so can't tell from the point of view but the fact that they don't come with obvious outdoor feature like hoops, zipper pockets, reflection line etc, it makes them look somewhat acceptable to wear in even in city and definitely good to wear in town. I get irritated even by some natural material such as wool and linen so it's difficult for me to find some cloth with comfortable material. I cut most of the tags at the back of my neck else I get rushes. Same for seams and to any rough material and even some merino wool product irritates my skin. Those pants are like wearing comfortable cotton pajama pants or something. I got more expensive North Face pants at the same time which I love the color and design but I feel irritated by seams. Excellent travel pants! Long length, needed hem, water resistant, stylish, comfortable and verrrry flexible. Love. I've been calling these my airplane pants. We just recently took a lovely trip back home to Nova Scotia. It's summer, I don't really need the heavy warmth of regular jeans but I thought that something thin and flexible for wearing on the lonnng plane flight would be good. I wanted something lightweight, dark to hide spills, flexible and something that could withstand hiking about the woods in rural Canada. These were perfect! Mind you that was after I hemmed them. I am not sure who these pants were made for length wise, lady giants? I had to cut off a good 4 inches of length and re-hem them before they were the perfect length for my leg. Not a big deal but if you are short on time, want pants like these for a specific travel purchase as I did, keep the long length in mind. I am 5'6", so what I consider average height and found these reallllly long. I'l post pics of what I cut off and what the final length was. The material is indeed lightweight and has a beautiful stretch to it. That stretch means when you hem you'll need an appropriate stretch stitch so your stitches don't bust. I spent some quality time googling my hem options and familiarizing myself with my sewing machine's stretch stitch option before beginning. I was a bit nervous cutting my new pants but I went slow and ironed them, pinned them, took lots of precautions and they turned out perfect. Can't even tell they are not the factory hem unless you get down at ankle level and stare hard at the stitches... The pants are so lightweight and rolled into such a tiny little ball of fabric that I, quite geekily, weighed them! They weigh the same amount as a single pair of my most worn/favorite cotton cargo shorts. Suuuhhhweet. As for space in my luggage they hardly took up any. As I mentioned though I wanted these first for wearing on the air plane, it was an overnight flight and wanted maximum comfort. I was indeed verrrrry comfortable. My one complaint...WHY DO MANUFACTURERS NOT GIVE WOMEN DECENT POCKETS????? Seriously. It's stupid. Put the deep pockets in and let us decide if we want to stuff them and add pack mule like bulges to our thighs. If I am traveling I don't care if my cell phone bulges my front pockets or a wad of tissue or cough drops...I'd just like the option of carrying those things. Women's pant's pockets are notoriously short and I find it insulting. I purchased a pair of Columbia brand hiking pants for my husband at the same time as mine and they of course had wonderful, usable pockets. I won't ding them a star because it didn't affect my opinion of the over all feel, use and comfort and also it seems to just be an overall annoying clothing trend. But Columbia.....you'd impress me if you changed this antiquated habit of short, unusable pockets for future pants. I read up on these pants before committing to the purchase so I knew they were water resistant but WOW. They are VERY water resistant. Like if you take a handful of water and toss on your leg (there's that geek testing again) the water just rolls off. Of course if you submerge the material they will get wet but for spills, splashes, light rain the water literally rolls off. When we were outside in some light showers I just gave them a flick on the way inside and knocked the water beads off. They dry pretty quickly as well. We had access to a washer and dryer on our trip but I suspect these would be the sort of clothing you could hand wash in a sink and wring and dry in the air. I will try that out some time. I was careful the first time I dried them, keeping my eye on them and checking every few minutes. I find with these modern fabrics they either go nutso and shrink or do something weird or they are super star space age modern marvels, these are modern marvels. I followed the instructions, cool water, gentle cycle for drying (no high heat). They washed well. They can wrinkle a little, so if you fold or hang them right out of the dryer they look best. However I do find the wrinkles relax away after wearing or hanging so again, no big deal. We were in a humid environment, East Coast summer so quite moist, we didn't need long pants like these the whole time and I did find they got a bit stuffy after the sun was up. Not a biggy, just letting you know they're not as cool as shorts, despite their light weight etc. They look like dress pants. This is actually really awesome. They look like I'm "dressing up" instead of heading out to hike a trail or wander around the woods. I received many compliments on them when I was wearing a nice white t-shirt with them and kept having an internal mental giggle because I was wearing hiking clothes...nothing "fancy". I am a size 18 usually for most average department store pants. Considered "plus size" These pants fit my hips and legs etc really great. In fact for the first time in a while I wondered if I should have went a size smaller... These were actually a little loose on me but again I wanted maximum comfort not maximum style so loose sounded good for long airplane travel. I may try the size 16 in the future because for one, you save moolah in the smaller sizes and also it might be nice to have that slimmer option.. I did find after an all day wear the pants got even looser, stretched a bit...but they tighten back up after a wash. They have a VERY HANDY draw string cord as well as the snap and zip. I saw some one else mention the zipper falling down. THANK-YOU to who ever commented on that. I took the precaution of black undies for travel and halfway through the airport I realized things were feeling a bit breezy and lo and behold my zip was down..er....not cool...well it was cooling but not "cool". Anyways, be aware of that as well. It only happened once but once was enough for me to check every once in a while ;) I did find cat hair really clung to these, just remembered that...but I didn't have any dryer sheets at the place we were staying so maybe that's not an issue if they've been dryed with those..hmm, I'll look in to that further. I was visiting my Mom who had a verrrrry cat-hairy spot to sit and after I stood up I realized I'd taken half the cat hair with me haahaha. Let's see...That's all I can think of for now. These are really awesome pants and my minor complaints are just that, minor and don't feel they warrant dinging a star. Their supreme comfort and stretch that let me do anything from deep squats, hiking up a hill, sit for long periods in a plane seat are what I wanted and these excelled there. They satisfied all my needs and I hope they are long lasting in their wear. I'll do a longer term update in 6 months or a year or something.

Nice pants AFTER I'd tailored them to fit Bought size 20 in the blue. COLOR: a somewhat odd blue, definitely has a gray undertone - still works as a neutral color for pants. It's a chameleon - if you wear a navy blue top the pants will appear to be gray; wear a gray top and the pants will appear to be blue-ish. MATERIAL: nice and stretchy in ALL directions! Extremely comfortable to wear due to all the stretch. Appearance is a tight 'woven' fabric, silky-feeling but has a matte finish (not shiny). All pockets are mesh, so not bulky under the outer material, and are fairly deep. Though the material wrinkles after sitting a while, they gradually disappear after standing or hanging. HARDWARE: Snap is metal and heavy-duty (won't easily pop open like some plastic snaps do when I bend over). Zipper is nylon. SIZING: LENGTH measured 31.5 inches after washing and drying once (no shrinkage in length or width). I have a 27.5" inseam so needed to hem them - I have a sewing machine and am used to doing this unless I can buy pants with a 'short' inseam. PANT LEG WIDTH was perfect for me (thigh 29", calf 16"). I think the size 18 legs would fit also, but would be tighter than I want (more like a 'skinny-leg' pant). I wanted a looser-fitting leg. WAIST was too large for me (my waist=41") The drawstring was the width of a shoestring (!) inside the 1.5" wide waistband, which made it difficult to spread the gathers out evenly when I pulled it tight enough to keep the pants from slipping down. Instead, I removed the drawstring and inserted "waistband elastic" through 2 slits I cut in the inside material of the waistband at the back near the left and right hip seams, zigzag stitching closed the slit I cut to insert the elastic (elastic measured 1.25" wide x 3" shorter than the hip-to-hip seams). This nicely shrank the waistband so it fits me comfortably; I can pull the pants on, zip-and-snap and I'm ready to go! See attached photos. I plan on wearing these pants for hiking here in Colorado, & will be taking them while touring Java & Bali for 3 weeks this fall, and on a tour to Chile & Argentina (including Patagonia) next fall. They should be very comfortable to wear on these long flights thanks to the stretch & fluid-resistance of the fabric.

The Perfect Everywhere, Everyday AND Hiking Pants After trying a couple of other brands of outdoor pants and being disappointed, I found these on sale on Amazon 5 years ago and I have not looked back since. I am 5'5" 125 lb. with a straight build, and the size 6 is perfect. I also have the size 8 for when I am 5-10 pounds heavier. I own 5 pairs in black and khaki colors, and have worn them on miles of hiking in Utah, Nevada, Colorado, and Iceland. They are light weight, wrinkle resistant, water resistant and offer plenty of give for serious hiking. I like the drawstring waist feature to tighten them during long hikes if needed. Not to mention, they have a very flattering look to wear out and about, not just for hiking/trekking. I have not experienced "pilling" on any of the pairs that I have; however one of them did snag on the leg but that is to be expected when hiking through vegetation or rough terrain. I hope Columbia never, ever stops making these pants!

Love these pants but... Love these pants and fit. I am glad I chose a size 6R for 125-128lbs weight and 5' 4" ht. The size 4 fit but looked like legging tight in the thigh. Size 6 is more sporty look for my weight. The pants are perfect for travel and hot weather. They have good stretch so movement is easy and they are light weight and quickdry, all the features they are desirable for travel to a tropical place. But the only negative is that even after following the washing and drying instructions, the laces got tangled in the pants and the result was a very wired and tangled lace that I was fortunately able straighten by ironing. But when I put it back in I noticed some grey stuff/ material come out which I cannot put back in ( see pics).

Comfy outdoor pants I bought these originally to wear for a trip around Yosemite, but have worn them for hiking a good deal since then. Overall, they are lightweight, breathable, stretchy, and fairly flattering. For colder temps I layered thermal underpants underneath, and they still looked good from the outside. My complaints are that after a few hours of activity, they stretch out and lose some of their shape. Also, the drawstring at the waist adds a bit of bulk in an area where none of us needs any extra. Finally, I'm getting a bit of pilling/fuzziness in a few spots after three months of use. I was hoping they would last longer. For sizing reference, I am 147 lbs and 5'6". Tried the 8 and 10 but ultimately went with the 8.

Crazy Static! I was looking for a pair of utility pants for camping/hiking and was hoping to avoid the pricey ‘outdoor brands’ that charge $90-100 per pair. These pants got good reviews and the price couldn’t be beat. They are definitely soft and comfortable. I’m 5’-2” and 130 lb and the 4 fit nicely- a little more flared at the bottom than I typically like, but the boot cut would definitely be ok with chunky hiking boots. I was ready to call the search over until I noticed how much static cling was happening after only wearing them for 1 minute! The pants picked up every single piece of lint, dust, dog hair within a 5 foot radius. Since I plan on wearing these in mostly dusty, dirty, rocky places, I would be annoyed if these picked up every little piece of dust and looked dirty constantly. There’s a chance I tried them on on a dryer than average day, but I’d rather not take the chance. Unfortunately ended up returning them.

Comfortable hiking pants I am 5' 5" and 150lbs and purchased the size 8 regular and the fit great. The pockets may gap a little so a 10 would probably have been sufficient as well. I wore these all weekend on a backpacking trip. Comfortable and lightweight even in 90 degree weather. The only concern was that the fabric was easier for ticks to hold on to than my husband's more standard hiking pants. Additionally, the area that sits tight against the skin (thighs), mosquitos were able to bite through. I walked through a stream and they dried very quickly!

Truly waterproof I am 5 ft 5 in and 135 pounds, usually wear womens pants size 6. I got a medium based on reviews and thinking I will wear layers under them. They are a little baggy, maybe should have gotten a small. I wore these in the shower and stayed completely dry! I only dislike that the cuff is quite wide, more like boot cut instead of tighter fitting. I have to wear these on the outside of rubber boots. It will only bother me I suppose when biking when you want no extra pant cuff. There is velcro on the cuff, but you can't make it skin tight, there will ways be a little flare. I got these for $41.50, which is nice. I will use them for biking, coxing, hiking, snowshoeing and farming in the rain. There is a drawstring and an inside id/key pocket. The mesh layer inside keeps pants from sticking to you. Why didn't I think of getting these sooner!? So many uses.

"Used-Like New" should include cut in the butt Needed waterproof pants for a camping trip that predicted rain. And being a plus size girl finding anything in store is like asking for 3miracles. So in comes Amazon. I hate paying so much for something i probably will only use once so purchasing used made sense. And the description of "Used-Like New" assumes that it's in great condition. Well the pants still had the tag and all, but there was a 2inch cut right in the left cheek. Annoying. But they served their purpose given that we ended up having little to no rain. The fit otherwise was as expected. I ordered a size up to be able to comfortably layer and was able to squat and bend with no problem. I'm 5'3, 250lbs and i carry my weight in my belly- for reference.

Waterproof and Fit Nicely These are an awesome alternative to gaiters. The ankle is wide enough so I can take them on/off without removing my hiking boots, but the Velcro sinches at ankle. They fit nicely at hips and thighs (waist is not obnoxiously high). I am 5’5 125 pounds. They are def waterproof. I wear these in the snow and rain for long hikes. I wish the leg was a little narrower but I would definitely not say the pants are too wide. These are great pants. Also wind resistant. Can wear fitted layers (leggings/soccer pants) underneath.

Buying a back-up pair!! I don't write reviews, but these pants are amazing. I'm buying a back-up pair today. I hiked 26 miles of the Appalachian Trail in Georgia in rain and thunderstorms. Not once did I get wet, never felt the wind and I stayed warm. I'm a size 6/8, 5'8 at 145 lb and the medium fit perfect. Plenty of room to go over my running pants and the length was spot on. Columbia rain jacket gets 4 stars too! Highly recommend.

Great foul weather gear for fishing. Bought these for my daughter. We are big-time fishermen in S. Florida. I and my son also own a pair of these, and they are great in wet weather. Completely rainproof and breathable. When we are on the ocean in the winter with chop and swells running 5 to 6 feet, the waves crash over the side of the boat, and th cold rain is pelting you, these pants keep you nice and dry and warm. That way we can stay focused on the fishing and not hypothermia.

Great for snow sports in Norway These kept me so dry even on heavy snow days. I also wore these when chasing the northern lights in the middle of the night. I stayed dry and warm all night with good base layers underneath. I highly recommend these pants! I'm 5'1" and with some rolling at the waistline, they were the perfect length.

Nice, cool pants but longer than advertised My husband and I have some upcoming travel plans that will take us to some areas that will have temps in the upper 90’s… too hot! I’ve been looking for clothing that is not only cool but lightweight and wrinkle-resistant. These pants are perfect!!I’m a former ICU/ER nurse and had to wear scrubs daily at work. They were always comfortable, but it never occurred to me, now that I’m retired, to purchase scrubs for daily wear. These were really a revelation for me! Very neat, no ironing required, lightweight, and multiple pockets, what more could a girl ask for?These have an elastic waist that is very forgiving, as well as a drawstring for reinforcement. The fabric is not see through, but is rather thin, so although there are multiple pockets, I wouldn’t try to stuff them with bulky items. A really nice detail is the small slit in the outer seam at the bottom edges, it really makes the pants look nice. These are available in petite, regular and tall. I have a 32” inseam, so ordered the Tall size. Although the “Tall” states it is 32.5” inseam but measures 33.25”. I have no problem hemming them up an inch but just an FYI. I’m just delighted that I can find pants long enough for me.I am thrilled to have found these but must still deduct one star for the length.I hope you found this review to be . Thanks for reading!!

Just the right amount of baggy comfort I ordered these in the medium (unisex) size––I’m 5’5”, 140lbs and I found the medium to be the perfect amount of baggy without swimming in them so much that I feel like I have no shape. The elastic on both the ankle and waist is nice and not at all tight; the drawstring is just a simple cotton shoe-lace style rope; and there are no grommets to keep the string from disappearing into the elastic waistband. For me, the backside is a little saggy, but that’ll be different for everyone. Plus it will shrink over time because it’s cotton. The fabric is medium to lightweight, it’s slightly fleeced on the inside and a mostly soft outside with some texture to it, which is similar to a fine, corduroy stripe. Pepper is a great, dusty dark grey that goes with everything. I’m happy enough with them that I’m thinking of also buying them in blue.

Comfortable, No flaws, Nice sized pockets Shipping was quick for these blue-ish sweats. They also came with a tag "Comfort Colors" that warns they might fade in the wash. They did fade a little but it wasn't much - still good to know so you don't ruin something light/white. I did put a white washcloth in to test the colorfastness. They also got softer after being washed, making them my new favorite sweats! The color of these isn't a true blue, but more of a dusty, greyish, blue. Very nice color though. The weave on the fabric is interesting. Another review akinned it to corduroy. You can see a slight wale in it but it isn't stiff or really noticeable unless you look closely. The inseam was about 29" which fits me perfectly at 190# and 5'8". The drawstring in these is a solid material similar to a shoelace - no stretch - but long enough to sinch in if you need it. Good sized pockets which I recommend turning inside out for the first was at least as my pockets had quite a fluff collection going on in there lol Overall I love them. Super comfortable. Just the right length.

I sized up because these are 100% cotton. Very comfortable, relaxed fit I recently got a pair of these grey (“pepper”) 100% cotton sweatpants. I decided to size up to an XL in case they shrink. Ordinarily, I wear a M or L depending on the brand, and for reference, I am 6’ tall and weigh 150 lbs. These sweatpants are medium weight, seem well made, and have a relaxed fit that is very comfortable. The waist is slightly large on me, but the rest of the fit is perfect, and there is a drawstring to keep them in place. I usually wear a 32-34” waist, and these are a bit bigger than that, which makes sense because I sized up to an XL. The pockets are medium to small in size, the length is good, and I expect these will get a lot of use.

Cute capri. I’m 5’6” 135# I bought medium and they fit great.

Couldn't decide what size to order so I ordered 3 sizes! My size info: 180lbs 5'6 hips 41" waist 35" I was having a hard time figuring out what size to order from other reviews stating these are compression pants order up. So i did. I usually wear an xl in yoga type pants but can fit in large as well. So i ordered these in xxl. I got them and they weren't compressing anything didn't have any of the popping sounds people talked about. So I ordered an xl and a large to try on. I will be keeping all three. They all fit. Attaching photos to show fit and I wrote the size on the mirror. I wear them just barely covering my belly button to suck in the spare tire between my hips and belly button. The white pattern ones do show thru if you are wearing bright undies. I put on some bright striped full coverage underwear to test it. I will definately wear nude colored panties with these in public. The xl made a couple popping noises while pulling them on. The L had several popping noises while pulling them on but they pulled right up no fighting or struggling to pull them on. They all were slightly baggy around calf area but were easy to pull up to a position where they fit snuggly and they didn't bunch up. So far they seem to stay in place but I have only worn a pair once so I will update my review after I have worn them more. The smaller the size the more compression there was without feeling like I was being crushed. These are my first pair of compression pants so cannot say how they compare to other compression pants. They feel comfortable and feel durable. I will update when there is something to update.

Please don’t ever stop making these! If you’re looking for a pair of leggings that are high waisted, compression (read no cellulite or jiggle), don’t sag or lose their shape, and can be worn sporty, dressy, or casual, these are your leggings! Also a plus they aren’t see through. They aren’t super shiny so they look great with dressy tops for work. I bought three black pair in size medium for school (cosmetology school so all black is required). They look just as good lounging around the house as they do paired with a long top, flats, vans, or boots for when I wear them for school. For reference I’m 5 feet tall and roughly 155 lbs and shrinking (losing baby weight) and ordered a medium so I’d get more time out of them before needing to go down a size. They are perfect. Even though I sized down they aren’t see through. They are a longer Capri length on me than shown in the listing photo. They land just slightly above my ankles but look good at that length.

If the sizing was more accurate and the pants weren't so oversized I would have given this product a much higher review because of the nice fabric, but they just look so dumpy on me.

Comfortable, well made joggers! I ordered these joggers in the wine color. I am 5'9, 265 lbs and I ordered the 3X. I love the fabric, it is lightweight and the color is deep and even. They fit loosely and I love the jogger style ankle. The ankle closure isn't too tight for me, which can sometimes be a problem. I also really like that they are plenty long for my height, but even if you're not so tall you can use the elastic ankle to make sure that they fit you well. They have a drawstring waist, which I enjoy because then I can adjust them a little bit. I probably could have sized down one and still had a nice comfortable fit. They're exactly the type of lightweight pant I was looking for for a trip I'm taking next week. The mornings and evenings will be cool enough to need longer pants but they won't be too warm during the day because they're nice and light. Love them!

Thick and soft These sweatpants are thick, warm and comfortable, making them the perfect lounging pants for cool winter days. They are a regular jersey knit on the outside, but have a plush velvety lining. The lining makes them thick, but feels very plush and warm against the skin. They also have deep functional pockets. These sweatpants are thick, and heavy, so they are not the best for sports or gym because I find they tend to fall down. They do have a drawstring to help keep them in place, the aglets on the drawstring seem flimsy and will probably not last long, but they are metal. I ordered these in a 2xl, I was going based off of my waist measurement of 37, and found these fit about right. I might suggest sizing up if you have a larger rear than your average person, as the pants don’t really account for extra “junk in the trunk”, I probably could have done the 3xl for more space, but worry all the extra fabric on a 3xl would make these much heavier. These pants are great if you get cold easily and want a pair of THICK warm fuzzy pants to lounge around the house in.

Super comfy jogger pants If you want to win your wife over, just get her some super comfortable jogger pants. lol My wife has been looking for some jogger pants and when I got her these, she fell over. She tells me (and everyone else) about how super soft these are and how they have a "buttery feel" to them on the inside and outside. When she saw they had pockets in the back and front, she was so happy. They did fit a little big on her but she likes her clothes that she wears around the house a little baggy. There have drawstrings as well and that is a big help for when she wants to tighten them She is 5’0 and 120lbs so I got her to large because I know she likes a baggier fit and I thought that if she was going to be wearing them all day, to get them bigger just in case. I'm glad I did and she is too. lol She said that she is not going to order a medium pair so they won't be as baggy so she can wear them out. Order the size that you want so that they will fit the way you want them too. She said that the large is about 2 sizes too big for her but since she is wearing them only home, they are perfect and that they are still super comfortable. I will end this by saying, she told her parents that she has never worn pants or joggers that had such a luxurious feeling! As happy as she was, I am definitely giving these a 5 ***** Star rating.

Not a fan of the material These scrub pants are cute and have a lot of pockets which I appreciate. The waistband is comfortable. They fit fairly well. They are slightly large on me but I intentionally sized up from my usual scrub bottom size of medium to a size large since I sometimes find jogger-style scrub bottoms fit a little tighter than I like. I could probably have worn the medium but I am happy with the extra space in the large, especially since the material is not very stretchy. For reference I am 5'9" around 160lb.My big complaint about these is the material. Right away I realized the material is sort of "plasticky" feeling, much stiffer and scratchier than other scrubs. This is especially problematic for the pants, since I wear an undershirt with the top but the pants are right against my skin. The material also makes a quiet "swishing" noise with every movement, which I don't like especially in a work environment. For context, I wear scrubs almost every day, I have 5 other brands of premium scrubs in my closet along with countless hospital-issue scrubs and these are the most uncomfortable ones I own. I wouldn't get these again.

Comfy but baggy These pants are comfy! I like wearing them, they feel lightweight and look neutral. The waistband fits well and I like the pockets. However, as someone on the shorter side at 5’1”, they are too long, which like any jogger that is too long makes them pool at my ankles. I wish they came in a size short.

Not Stretchy Enough I don't really like these scrubs/hiking pants myself. The waistband elastic is really firm and not actually all that stretchy or comfortable at all. Size Medium runs just a bit smaller than the chart at just under a 30" waist, and I would recommend you actually are at a 30" (or less) which I definitely am not. 11.5" rise, which is pretty close to a true mid-rise on me, I'm unable to pull it up to a high waist. 31.5" inseam. The fabric is pretty thin and I'm not sure it would hold up to hard wear. The pockets are nice and it does seem breathable. Might be okay if you are small-waisted and your job doesn't cause much wear and tear.

Perfect! I love CRZ Yoga's butterluxe line. The quality is great. It is the only brand I wear. I have about 10 pairs of the 6" shorts and several of the leggings in the 28". (I need a few more!) I really like that I can choose the length that I want. The standard size is the 25". I am not tall, only 5'4", but I don't like it when the leggings are up above my ankles. If I buy the 28" they hit where I like them to. They also have an even longer 30 inch size, as well as capris length and several lengths in their shorts line. The material is super soft. They are nice and thick, but not so thick so that they make you hot. They stay in place nicely and don't ride up, fold over or bunch up anywhere. I work from home, so in the summer I throw on the shorts and the leggings in the winter and wear them all day. When it is time to workout, I don't need to change, I'm ready to go! I also love that they come in so many great colors. I can mix and match with other tops.

cute, comfy and long enough for me! These seem like very high quality for the price, are very stretchy, cute and comfortable. They're soft on the inside and nice and long -- I'm almost 6 feet tall and these fit perfectly. These aren't super warm so just be careful. You could run in them in like 35 or 40 degree weather probably, but they wouldn't be warm enough to walk in temps like that. But that's not why I bought them and not what they're intended for. Buy the fleece lined leggings if you want something very warm for going outside in the cold. (Or just buy them because they're super comfy and cute too!)I really like this brand and own a lot of clothing of theirs now (like 15 pairs of leggings, 4 tops, some shorts etc). I wanted these to be a little loose honestly so I went up a size. All of my other pants are Larges but I bought an XL and like it. I think a Large would have fit fine but would have been tighter.I don't get anything for writing this review btw, I'm a real person with a real job who likes writing reviews to try and be and counter the trend of fake reviews. I want to preface by saying I love this brand and have a bunch of clothes from them. They are durable, great quality, and look so dang cute on the days I want to relax without looking like a total scrub. I am 5'5", about 105lbs, waist size 25 inches, hips 36.5-37 inches. I purchased both XS and XXS. XXS is more form fitting but it is a tad too tight around the ankles. Waist is a bit loose, but nothing too noticeable. Still is very cute regardless. XS is more of a relax fit on my frame. It is big around the waist unless you cinch it up really tight, which makes it look a somewhat weird. I would recommend XS for waist size 26 or 27 inches. Length is perfect on XS. I will keep both, especially for the days when I'm bloated.

Soooo comfy and very functional! **UPDATED** Here are the cognac brown ones in a medium and the mountain green ones in a small Size at photo time: 5'6", 140lbs, 29.5" waist, 36" stomach, 35.5" hip bones, 38" butt, 21" thighs, 32" inseam The mediums are definitely "too big", but I wouldn't be able to wear the smalls when I'm bloated. The smalls are definitely the right size for me, so size down (I usually wear size 8). I do a lot of hiking, biking, scrambling, climbing, and some bushwhacking. I have climbed in both pairs at the gym and even though the small is fitted, the fabric is plenty of stretchy to keep range of motion climbing. We'll see how the durability is, but the seams look good and the fabric feels fairly sturdy. They definitely do dry fast. I wore them to the climbing gym twice and they are very abrasion resistant (I have repeatedly scraped them in the climbing gym). Pretty easy to knock the chalk off of them. At this point, I'm in love with these and would like to live in them daily.

Great pants with stretch, bit short in the legs Six month update: I wear these once a week at most and wash them... infrequently. Even so, my friend noticed that a tear is appearing on the back around the waistband. I love the fabric, feel, and taper, but I think I’ll stick with Columbia from now on (for $0-$15 more and better stitching). I’m ~115 lbs and 5’7” and leggy, and the XS fit perfectly in the waist, but are a bit short (like a 7/8 pant) in the legs for me. Great fabric for hiking and a bit more stylish than my Columbia pants (though not as breathable). The waistband is stretchy and comfy, and I wear these pants casually too! A few months in, they’re holding up well through hikes (no bushwhacking though) and machine washing (air dry). I will note that I ordered these in mountain green, and everything but the waist tie looks gray in every light (see picture). I didn’t mind the color so much so kept the pants, but do beware of ordering (at least the mountain green) if you’re deadset on the color.

Great fabric for hiking and a bit more stylish than my Columbia pants (though not as breathable). The waistband is stretchy and comfy, and I wear these pants casually too! A few months in, they’re holding up well through hikes (no bushwhacking though) and machine washing (air dry). I will note that I ordered these in mountain green, and everything but the waist tie looks gray in every light (see picture). I didn’t mind the color so much so kept the pants, but do beware of ordering (at least the mountain green) if you’re deadset on the color.

All around 5 stars. Perfect fit. I am 5’6” 140lbs I am wearing the Mountain Green size medium joggers. I just ordered two pair of CRZ joggers because I have been so pleased with these. This time I want to try different fabrics and styles.

Comfy, long enough, but not as thick as I hoped For reference: 5'7" 185lbs pear-shape and I ordered size XL. I bought these hoping they'd be a comfortable and casual option for colder days/nights this winter. (I live in a climate where the temp drops below 0°F often throughout the winter) The length was great because I'm 5'7" and I can wear them high waisted and the pant legs still reach my ankles. I've tried some joggers from crz yoga that leaves my ankles exposed, so that's obviously not ideal for the cold temps and I just prefer the look of longer pants on myself. The material of these pants is not exactly what I expected. It's very comfy and soft. I'm not sure how it will hold up for washing... But the inside of the pants are not fuzzy/fleece like I expected. They're almost a heavier legging material inside. I purchased the matching mock neck hoodie and the interior of the hoodie is soft and fleece inside, so I just wish that the pants were the same. That would lend to them being warmer and in my opinion generally more cozy. The other thing I'd like to note with this material is that it does show more texture than I'd like. The front pocket seams show through the pants which takes away from the elevated leisure look these are going for and when the fabric is flush with my skin, I can see the texture of my cellulite and I imagine you'd be able to see panty lines as well. I did get a light color, so perhaps the darker colors wouldn't have this issue, but an interior fleece lining would have solved this problem as well. I'm honestly not sure if I'll keep them or return them. I do like the hoodie and will be keeping that.

I ordered these as work pants because I wanted something not as form-fitting as my leggings, but still stretchy and soft. I always get a little nervous with different pants because I have sensitive skin (might be a sensory thing idk) and some material feels like little pinches on my legs all day and is unbearable. Anyway, while I do get the little pinchy sensation here and there, it's very tolerable and I love these pants! They're stretchy, soft, easy to walk in, and I love how they're fitted from the top and down my legs but loose at the ankle! I'm between a 14/16 and ordered XL and they're totally perfect. I will say though, I'm 5'6" and they stop slightly before my ankle bone, which I like, but thought it was worth mentioning. I'm ordering another pair but in navy! I HIGHLY recommend, even for the girlies with sensory issues😉

Nice sweats I really like these sweats. The material is smooth with a terry-like texture inside. They're lighter weight and perfect for fall. The teal color is exactly as pictured and looks really nice. For sizing, I think they fit true to size, but if you're wanting a little extra room for comfort or if you're in between sizes, you can size up. I'm 5'9, 170lbs, size 10/12 or large, but I ordered an extra large because I don't like the tight sweats that almost look like leggings. They have a nice, relaxed fit in a size up. One thing to note is that these have a really straight fit and not much extra room in the rear if you're curvy. The length was a bit short on me so I scrunched them up. The elastic bands keep them in place on my calves. The quality seems really good and they washed up nicely. I washed them on cold and air dryed them.

Almost 5 Stars I really like these sweatpants but can’t give them 5 stars because the waist sizing is disproportionately big :/ The length is great - even for shorter women (I’m 5’0” and roll the ankle cuff up once); the leg width is not too baggy or too tight giving a neat & structured look and allowing to wear out of the house without looking sloppy; the waist is a true high-waisted fit, hitting just barely above my bellybutton (when drawstring is tied); and the material is soft inside and out and thick enough to be warm in cold weather. Despite all this, the waist is circumference is too big by several inches. If it weren’t for the drawstring being cinched in as much as possible, the pants wouldn’t be that flattering IMO - they would slide down onto the hips and defeat the purpose of being high-waisted. I still like them but this bugs me (especially at the price point) and am on the fence on ordering another pair. I ordered the XXS and have a 22-23” waist 32” hip.

not "butterluxe", XXS too big, 33.5 inseam too short still 5'8" with 34 inch inseam to ankles, 25 inch waist, 37 inch hip, size XXS in CRZ Yoga butterluxe and size XXS is too big in these flares. These are not the same material as the CRZ Yoga butterluxe leggings that I've loved for the past year. These are shinier than the leggings and they are not as soft or thin as the leggings are. I would say the leggings are like a second skin in the butterluxe material. These are closer to the "naked feel" material- which is slightly thicker and slightly shiny and just has a slippery feel to them. They don't breathe as well as the butterluxe leggings. The XXS in these are bigger at the waist and hip than the same size in the leggings. The XXS in the butterluxe leggings (same brand and rise) fit me PERFECTLY. In these flares, the waist is loose and shifts around when I move and the hips are bigger. It just feels overall like a size too large. The inseam length at 33.5 inches, is really great and i really appreciate the attempt. However, I really hope we can do some even longer. There is an entire population of women who have legs even longer than that. Like me. I'm 34 inches from crotch to ankle. If I'm wearing flares, then I need 36 inches so they go to my heel. Just a thought. I swear, if you would just OFFER 36 inch inseam on flares and leggings, you would INSTANTLY get ALL of us long legged ladies buying MULTIPLE colors of every style you offer. And please don't forget that some of us long legged women come in many shapes and sizes, and some are also thin, so XXS with 36 inch inseam would be EXCELLENT. Thank you.

Pants can be washed! Heat up nicely once I figured it out I ordered these and intend to wear them to cold weather Green Bay Packer games (on the "Frozen Tundra of Lambeau Field..."). I ordered the XL size so I could comfortably wear either leggings or even my old-but-fav skinny jeans under them. I am exactly 5'2" but these are not too long on me and will be fine with shoes on. There is both an inner fleece lining and an outer layer of fabric that is sweatshirt-type material. At first they did not seem to be heating up but I think that was user error. You attach the included battery pack to the USB-A connector in the front left pocket. Then you press the Power button that is visible on the front of the pants (lighted up in my photo in the mirror). I believe that is just ON and was red. Then you short-press to cycle through the temperatures of Low (white), Medium (blue) and High (red). As soon as I did that and got to red again, then I immediately felt the pants warming up. I included a photo from the instructions called Warm Tips. Mostly they are cautions. You should not spray any liquid on the heated pants and avoid sharp items, or heavy objects that apply excessive pressure on the heating elements. That does give me pause - what if some two-fisted-slopper spills his beer on me at the game?! Well I guess this is why is plan to have something on underneath, so that I can strip the pants off in a hurry, if needed. And these can be washed! The avoiding of liquid is only when they are connected to the battery pack and are heated. I included a photo of the label inside of the pants that say they can be washed on cold and then hung to dry. The cord that is permanently inside of the pocket (USB-A connection) has a cap which I'm sure you want to put in place before putting in the washing machine. Go, Pack, go!

High Waisted and Comfy They are high waisted which I love because I have a long torso and they’re soft. I got a medium and they fit great. The ankle parts could be a little stretchier because when they get caught on my lower calves they’re a little too tight. (Not a huge deal) I got a medium and I’m 5’6” and 155lbs. Giving 4 stars because the material is pretty thin and I would prefer joggers to be a normal to thicker material but it’s not the worst. Overall I do like them.

Defective pair, but overall solid. Consider a size up! I wanted to love these, but there seems to be a factory error maybe in mine. The left leg is slightly shorter than the right - it isn’t super noticeable if you’re not looking for it, but it drives me crazy when I’m wearing them. I took off two stars for that. Other than that: The pants are polyester, not super soft like cotton but still comfy. I think the bands are super think which might annoy some people but I personally like it. I wish I had ordered a size up, however. The baggies is good, but they are a little short for me if I want them to be truly high waisted...which is surprising because I’m more on the short size. I’m 5’3” and 117lbs and I got the Small, but I wish I had gotten a Medium. Unfortunately I can’t exchange them because I threw away the packaging. I would maybe order another pair, but I’m wary too because of the defect. Maybe I just got a bad pair, though. Overall, a solid pair of sweats. I just wish the quality was slightly more consistent for a $25 pair of sweatpants. Maybe order a size up if you are tall or want them extra baggy.

Buy 10 of these! UPDATE: these did shrink a bit in the wash. Enough that I wouldn’t mind a size up. They still fit comfortably around the waist, but the hem hangs at my ankle now, and the pant leg is not quite as loose as it was before. The fabric does pill slightly, but not bad, and it doesn’t bother me. I am 5’6” and 125 lbs and I ordered a small. They fit absolutely perfectly. The hens just barely touch the floor, but don’t drag (and they aren’t obnoxiously high on my ankles). The waist band is well fitted, with elastic and pull strings. The elastic doesn’t feel too snug or tight, but I also don’t have to tie the strings to keep the pjs up. The flannel fabric is incredibly soft. It feels warm and cozy without being too hot. The leg is comfortably loose and not excessively baggy. My only recommendation would be to buy these in every color. If you’re like me, you’ll want to wear these all day, every day.

However, I recently bought a size medium and it fits me waist-wise well, but I’m nearly 5’ 11” with long legs (33”-34” jeans) and these new pants also shrunk (after one wash) to 29” and that’s just too short for me even wearing them low on my waist. Fantastic looking..REALLY warm These looked fantastic! Sadly, my butt is not large by any means and I have hip dips, yet these pants still made it look phenomenal (I think). I say these are pants because when I think of leggings, I think of light/spandex-y material—which these are not. For me, these are winter time only pants. They’re lined with fleece-like material and really keep your body heat in. It was about 70 degrees inside my home when I tried them on and I was sweating inside of the pants within 10mins—my skin was damp from sweat when I took them. I think the best thing I can compare how they keep heat in is like how a scuba diving wet suit keeps heat in. The pleather-like material can get a little bunched up on the outside if you don’t pull the material tight enough down the leg after putting them on. The way the stitching is across the butt is meant to fall under where your butt cheek is—which gives your butt the illusion of poking out further than it actually does. Again, I think these are super sexy, but only something I’ll, personally, wear in the winter/cold weather. I’m 5’4 135lbs with 32 inch waist, 37 inch hips for those who want size reference and I purchased a size large. I’d have loved to have tried on a size lower to see if they fit any tighter as I feel like there was a little bit of room.

Save your Money My legginings arrived on time and did not smell. I read some reviews that stated they had a smell. I wish I would have read the reviews prior to purchasing though. The back of the leggings has a crossed beltloop in the back, dead center of your butt that looks like an X, however, ONE of the strips that makes the x is WHITE! When you peek on the other side it's black 🤦🏽‍♀️ A few of the good reviews mention this major DEFECT. I was going to return the leggings for a larger size but there's no point if they are all DEFECTIVE. I am 5,7" & 125 lbs & blessed with a natural, high, round booty. I usually wear a size 3/4 and in jeggings I usually size up to a medium so I got these in a medium also. They weren't long enough for my taste. They hit above my ankle. It was hard getting them on and adjusted properly. Note: I did try them on directly after I got out of the shower so my legs may have had some unseen moisture that prevented them from sliding on. For the brief moment I had them on, they seemed hot ... like eventually you'll get crotch & butt sweat hot aka not practical for the club, a concert or summertime. Once I removed the pants I could see that there was some type of scrunch butt going on down the booty seam. Bottom line, if they didn't have the white belt loop on the back, I would have returned them & tried a bigger size. Instead I will try another brand for more money.

Practically Perfect in Every Way So like a lot of the reviews on here I bought these as a replacement for the old cotton danskin yoga pants I loved so much. Except I still had mine so I could do a side by side comparison. Now my old ones are like 10-15 years old (probably closer to 15), and I've gotten bigger in this time (surprise surprise), so the mediums I had in the original have stretched to fit me over time, and the fabric is wearing out some so it's stretched and almost pilling a bit. But for old yoga pants that have served me well all this time I can't complain. Now I loved the original fit, with the little bit of a relaxed flair at the end just below the knee, but since I got bigger the mediums lost that trait. I got these new ones and even though they're slightly different in some small ways, they're very much the same. Same quality, comfortable and stretchy fabric that breathes, same slight relaxed flair at the end below the knee (without being an actual flair). Fits well in all the right ways, very flattering. The only real difference I could find was that the waist band was slightly different? But honestly it doesn't wear any different on my waist. It's still around the same width to give that tummy coverage, and fits comfortably without too much give or being too tight. The extra stitching was just kind of decorative and I think it has been updated for the times or something so it's not a huge deal (though I did love the stitching). Overall 10/10. I've worn them like half a dozen times and washed them once and no change, still as comfy as ever.

Practically Perfect in Every Way So like a lot of the reviews on here I bought these as a replacement for the old cotton danskin yoga pants I loved so much. Except I still had mine so I could do a side by side comparison. Now my old ones are like 10-15 years old (probably closer to 15), and I've gotten bigger in this time (surprise surprise), so the mediums I had in the original have stretched to fit me over time, and the fabric is wearing out some so it's stretched and almost pilling a bit. But for old yoga pants that have served me well all this time I can't complain. Now I loved the original fit, with the little bit of a relaxed flair at the end just below the knee, but since I got bigger the mediums lost that trait. I got these new ones and even though they're slightly different in some small ways, they're very much the same. Same quality, comfortable and stretchy fabric that breathes, same slight relaxed flair at the end below the knee (without being an actual flair). Fits well in all the right ways, very flattering. The only real difference I could find was that the waist band was slightly different? But honestly it doesn't wear any different on my waist. It's still around the same width to give that tummy coverage, and fits comfortably without too much give or being too tight. The extra stitching was just kind of decorative and I think it has been updated for the times or something so it's not a huge deal (though I did love the stitching). Overall 10/10. I've worn them like half a dozen times and washed them once and no change, still as comfy as ever.

Because they are so thick, I didn't expect them to be flattering, but they're actually not too bad. They lie nicely and disguise anything unflattering with their interesting texture. Overall, a decent pair of drawstring pants.

Flattering Fit and Comfy Stretch I ordered these Desol Women's Wide Leg Palazzo Pants for the office, and they’re great! They’re lightweight, stretchy, and super comfortable. The high waist and palazzo style flatters my figure and make my legs look longer. I usually wear a medium or large but went with XL based on the sizing chart (my waist is 37 inches, and the XL was listed as 30.71 inches). Turns out, the XL has plenty of stretch— I actually could have gone with a large. These pants are for tall people. I am 5'4 and even with heels on, the seam touches the floor. The sizing chart seems off, so I recommend ordering your usual size or one up if you want a looser fit.

I usually wear a 12 (41” hip) and the medium fits with a little room left over, so I would say they run large. Very comfortable, but I will probably add a drawstring or another piece of elastic so that my big phone and other things in the pockets don’t pull them down.

Spandex/slacks material I am 170lbs, 5ft 1in, 10-11 size in Jean's, and I carry my weight in my thighs and butt. The band is very comfortable. The downside is that it can't be tightened. I got a large petite. They are a little tiny bit big. I prefer a more fitted look, but I dont want to take the risk of a medium being too snug. So I'll keep them. The material is very spandex like, kind of like slack material that isn't made of cotton, the feel to them is fresh and cool, they're very flowy on the calves. I'll have to get use to that. They feel like they could slide down and I'd have to be pulling them up every now and then. I will give it a go at work and see how they work out.

These are PERFECT in every way! I needed scrub pants for the embalming lab. I am short and have some hips with a smaller waist. It is near impossible to find scrub pants that fit properly. These are PERFECT in every way. They hit just to the floor (i ordered a small petite and am 5'3" almost, size 4 in pants). They sit comfortably on my waist, not too high and not too low. They aren't tight at all but stay up without me having to hike them up. The comfort band is wonderful, gives me added support in the tummy area. The material is breathable and cooling, great for when those warmer months move in. I'm already placing another order for a few more pairs.

Whatever workhorse pants. I am 5.4 tall, 110 lb, hips 35, waist 25. Size 2 was a right guess. Slightly long shoeless which is compensated when worn with work slip resistant shoes. There is an extra inch at the waist line (? They are if rose for me) for comfort squatting. Much nicer with leather belt. I do not plan to iron them, only use a steamer because fabric has syntetic content so would not get iron gloss on them. Stay strong. Save $$. Update. I successfully graduated working at the grocery store. This was my first item to trhow away before even walking away (jk). The pants would loose the shape color and visual appeal in just couple washes. In fact they look like a garbage bag after the first wash. In order to look presentable I had to iron them with the corn stratch sprayer. If you for at the front end and don't get exposed to foods, cleaning supplies - they might last. If not - well they are thick and synthetic and protect your legs from hitting randon corners and boxes. Other then that, they are not the best. Those pantalones did last me a year at albertsons but I they also ugly and barely grey after all the washes.

Great if you’re really tall. Ordered these for work. I’m 5’11 and 157lbs, usually wear a (snug) size 8 long, closest size was an 8 tall so that’s what I ordered. These were too big and too long, didn’t have time to wait (left for job out of town same day) for new size so wore a belt and taped the hem. Material is pretty stiff. When I got home I washed and dried them, the length shrunk a little, no noticeable shrinkage in the waist area though. I’ll order a 6 next time. I think the tall size makes it bigger everywhere. They washed up nice, all in all I like these pants.

Pay Attention When Ordering I wear a 24 in the uniform company from my last job, but a 20 in my everyday jeans so I went for a 22 with Dickies. My new job told me to get Original 774 and with Amazon Prime I'd get them in time for my first day. They ended up being a little looser which means this company doesn't run small (that's good!) The waist band is high quality and the belt loops fit my 1.1" belt fine. They were really long though and that was MY FAULT. I didn't read the description on the bottom of the listing about the different seams the petites and regulars had. The petite inseam it says is 28.5" and my inseam is 29" so I definitely shouldn't have gotten the regular 32" inseam. You can see the repercussions of them being too long as a bunch of dirt got on the bottoms of the pant legs. Other than that, they are a great pair of pants!

The pants fit me great! I ordered a 10 Petite, I am 5'2" and the inseam is perfect for a change! I am a little concerned about the color of the pants. I teach at a school where we must wear khaki pants as part of our uniform and to me, these are more of a light sage green. I have attached a photo of the fabric. My current uniform pants are lying on top of my new Dickies and there is quite a difference in color. I'm keeping them and hoping my principal accepts them as khaki colored. Great Product, not built for my bodytype. Material is great, looks a little bit dressy. I work in the office at a manufacturing plant, so I walk around the plant but need to sit in meetings too. These would be perfect, tough fabric but professional. Unfortunately, just not cut for me. I ordered these in a 16Short and an 18 Short. In both sizes the thighs fit but the rise over the backside was too short. Like there was not enough material to come up from the crotch and cover my butt, I think if I had a smaller backside these would be great pants! The 18S also gapped a lot at the waist. I am 5'3" and the length was a bit long if I had on flats but would be just fine if I was in work boots

Not to bad 8/6/2022 Update: so I am closing in on a year or so of have these same pants, I think now I have 4 or 5 of them and these particular relaxed fit are still going strong with my daily work abuse to them. Honest the color has slightly faded as in they look like I wear them regularly and I just have a little friction discoloration between the thigh area. Aside from that they are very comfortable there are no tares or rips or anything coming undone. Good investment for my line of work. Only real bad thing is I work in the Florida heat a little more than I like these days and when it's hot I sweat and sometimes the pant legs stick to me. But that just started happening this week just because I am more out side. There is more then enough leg room it's must muggy here.11/12/21 so I am not a big dickies fan when it comes to pants. Well these Relaxed fits are better then any pair I have had in the past. I must say that it's all about body type on how anything can fits. I am 5'5'' About 230 and I am between sizes from 16 to 20 depending on manufacturer. So these pants fit me as a 20. I can bend, breathe, squat, break dance and what ever else in these pants. For me they work fine for my business center look or Shipping and receiving job.

Pockets stick out It's so hard to find good quality dickies in stores these days (always find the juniors shiny crap) so when I searched on amazon I made sure I ordered a pair that I (hopefully) wouldn't have to return. As far as fit I ordered a size 4 and these were a little loose. At 5'5" and in flats the pant is just a tad too long but that is to be expected. The twill is comfortable and stretchy. I work in a sports bar restaurant so the first time wearing the pant I noticed that they were really high rise which made me look like a grandma with a polo shirt tucked in. The pockets also flared out as if they were too tight on me, however, upon closer review the pockets were sewn in a tulip or petal like shape thereby causing the top portion of the pocket to "stick out". The outer seam running down the pant leg on one side was not sewn straight as it is off by almost 2" towards the front from top to bottom. I noticed this when wearing them as I kept twisting the front panel of the leg forward before I realized it was a sewing defect. Overall I feel very disappointed with this purchase and although I have worn and washed it I am hoping I can still return it as these things would not have been noticed until after putting them on.

Fits differently from the Dickeys I purchased from their website. I'm 5'6 and 175 lb and Amazon recommended the size 16 and i went with it. I did get one they're a bit loose but after I wash them first time they they fit around my waist. The pants legs are loose and roomy which are nice. I usually purchase cargo dickeys from their website and wear a size 12. Not sure what the jump is since these are so much looser than the cargo pants I purchase, so maybe they run small. I do like them though. Really comfortable work pants. Update: I still like the pants however the bottom hem came undone.

Slightly too big, great quality The quality was great although a bit stiffer then I was expecting but it's Dickies so I think that's the point, I'd expect these to be quite durable. I was going to use these as casual black pants. A bit on the big side. I'm 5'4 140lbs waist is 31 and hip is almost 39. I recently had a baby and don't know my new size yet, (plus every company is a little different) I used the size chart and got an 8. It was definitely fit too long by maybe 3/4 inches or More, but I could've gotten it hemmed, for the quality of these pants it would have been worth it. At the waist it was way too loose. The butt area was ok, it could've been just a bit tighter. And at the crotch area it was visibly baggy (as much as stiff pants allow) and creasy. I will be returning them because of the fit only. I will not be exchanging to a smaller size because I prefer a lighter material for the summer for casual wear. I may get these a size down in the future. -1 star for not fitting, everything else was good.

definitely size up! update! after washing and drying completely they feel a little snug on the first wear but definitely loosen up during the day. they don’t bag out too badly, i was able to wear them about 3-4 times before washing and they still fit well. based on the other reviews i ordered 2 sizes bigger than what i wear in jeans and they fit pretty well! a little gaping at the back waist and the crotch scrunches up a little, as noted in other reviews. they almost come up to my belly button which is perfect for me —i can’t stand low rise pants! haven’t washed them yet so not sure if they’ll shrink or not, but if they do they’ll probably fit perfectly. the length is perfect for me! legs are pretty wide for bootcut. for reference i’m 5’9” and 160 pounds and i ordered a 10 reg. just needed them for work so i think they’ll work just fine.

Good quality, very comfortable, runs large (purchased: 4 short) Personally I do not like these for fit reasons but I am rating them higher because: 1) the material does feel very sturdy 2) the relaxed fit is relaxed and comfortable 3) nice pockets! I thought this would fit since I'm 5'2" and the size 4 measurements were only a little larger than I am, but there is still a gap at the back (my photo makes it look manageable but in reality I can loosely fit my fist between the band and my back). Fit and feel amazing! These fit perfectly and feel absolutely amazing! I'm just under 5' 3"/weight 110 and the size 4 was perfect. I can wear them with my boots, Romeos or Danner shoes and they don't touch the ground. I tried size 2 originally, but I new once they shrunk, they'd be a tad too small. I've been looking for a great pair of pants for at deer/elk camp and these are it. I was setting up camp, gathering and splitting wood all weekend and they felt great. I really like that they aren't too high waisted, as I personally don't like pants sitting at or above my belly button (but I also want to be decent when I bend over). They aren't stretchy, but they aren't stiff like other canvas work pants and don't bunch up when working. They aren't baggy or skinny like skinny jeans, they're right in the middle and also have lots of practical pockets. Finding quality, well fitting womens work pants hasn't been easy. Can't say enough good things, I will be buying several more. One year later, I've ordered 3 pairs in different colors. The tan color is a really nice color, not a super vibrant/bright, it's a bit subtle and perfect. The green and gray colors are just as beautiful. I just love these pants and I get so many compliments every time I wear them. Highly recommend them!

I'm 67, 5'7" and 118 pounds. Size 2 fit ok with a belt. At first, I thought they'd be fine, and was going to get another pair. We have a farm animal sanctuary and good work pants are essential. Then I washed them twice (according to instructions) and they are so badly pilled that they look like I got them from the bargain bin at a thrift store. Everything (like hay) already stuck to them but now it's horrendous. The animals don't care, but I do give tours and would rather not look quite so disheveled.

On the fence.. I need khaki pants for a new job.. scoured Amazon for something with actual pockets and decided to try my high school go-to pants, Dickies. A late-30something now, I’m used to higher rise pants and these seem obscenely low, definitely bringing me back to the late 90s/early 2000s. Things I love: pockets. Real front and back pockets that many women’s pants deny us. The legs are a bit tight, but not uncomfortably so—there’s room for muscular thighs. I also like that they’re a slim straight leg and not a skin tight skinny. Things I don’t care for as much: that rise. It’s okay in front, though it’ll take some getting used to, but the rear rise leaves a bit to be desired… my butt is covered fully when I sit, but barely and my underwear comes out the top. The waist is a bit too big, which usually happens if I’m not buying pants made specifically for weight lifters. Also too long, but that was already anticipated since all dickies have a 32” inseam. They might work because I haven’t found anything better fitting yet, but still not sure if these will get returned or if I’ll get used to them and keep. For size reference, I am 5’3”, 125-130 lbs, athletic (slight) hourglass shape with 26.5” waist and 39” hip and ordered a size 4. I’ve also included some photos for reference.

Inconsistent quality & size 1 pair good & 1 pair bad. Neither are thick/tough workwear, but not super thin nor too stretchy. Good pair has a few loose threads, placket over zipper is kind of small, so does that wonky thing of showing gold zip near button, but not too bad. I'm 8 or 10, & 10 gives me a bit of drape instead of skin tight thigh/calf. Does make flat rear look flatter, but got for physical labor, not spanx substitute. Both pairs 10R, labels all match, but lt. grey is 1-1.5" longer than navy. Unwashed, grey is long side of 32" inseam & navy short side of 31. Both inseams could work for me in other uses, but didn't want ankle-showing for these. Plus, the navy is poorly made - not just a ton of loose threads, but missed & messed up stiches that will come apart eventually. Worst, navy pair has really messed up zip placket, way small, off kilter, & permanently pressed all wrong. So, most of very bright gold zipper would show - from like a mile away - against the dark fabric. Grey pair will be annoying around the top, but navy is terrible - that full zipper display looks like too-tight, cheap pants, even though bit loose on me. Torn tag & human hairs indicate someone else also found this navy pair unwearable.

Professional and Comfortable I needed nice khaki pants for work. I didn't want to go for any brand other than dickies. Quality is ok. I've worn them a couple of weeks. So far, they've held up. I probably could use a belt, but its fine for now. I don't like the front part. Makes me look like I have a little fupa. Doesn't matter though, purchased them for comfort. I am 5'2 and a couple inches too long. With my boots they are fine, I don't need to hem them. The pants wash well. I wash them in cold water and throw in the dryer. I don't need to iron really. They don't get wrinkly too much. There are only the 2 pockets up front and the 2 back pockets. Back pocket fits my phone.

Size 4R works well for 5’8” 120lbs I ordered the Dickies relaxed fit cargo in rinsed grape leaf green in a size 4R (although Amazon said I would fit a 6- I tried the 6 and it was way too loose everywhere). I’m 5’8”, ~120 lbs, usually a size 4 (My measurements from 2 years ago: Bra: 32A/B - 34 A/B, Waist: 65cm, Hips 92cm). The 4R fit me well. Even though when I stand there is a little room at the waist, it isn’t noticeable unless I pull it out. It would not be comfortable if they were tighter there, especially when you sit, you need a little space around the waist. When I kneel (which I do in fieldwork), these are a bit tight in the thighs and cut in a bit behind the knees- but that is to be expected with this thickness of fabric. The length fits me perfectly. I’m 5’8” and I have a shorter torso and longer legs and these are at my heel. The pockets are large so you can fit lots of things, although when you sit that might not be very comfortable. My iPhone 13 Pro Max fits in my thigh pockets when standing, but when sitting, it jabs into my pelvis. It is a little too long for the thigh pockets; It sticks out such that you can’t Velcro it shut. These seem well made and Dickies is a brand I trust.

Great Pant but need smaller original update 5’10 / 180lbs / ordered a size 14 long. These pants seem like they are going to work for my occupation as a freight train conduct however I ordered a couple sizes to large. I have a long torso and short legs and the length was perfect for me. The fabric are typical dickies feel and appear to be durable with great pockets. Amazing makes exchanges super easy so I just did an even exchange and am awaiting my replacement pair. I figured a 10 long would work better. I’ll do an update when i get them done comparison. Update… Went for a a size 10R and they are great. Washed them, HUNG TO DRY and wore them to work today. I would oursire in the heat of Texas summers and these pant were AMAZING. Light material yet durable. Snug around the waste but flexible. Pockets were perfect and the bottoms fit around my work boots and the length was just right. Overall, I am very happy with these and will be ordering many, many more. Better than the hot jeans I am used to swearing in. Hope this helps ya.

Size Charts are Full of Lies Overall, I like these pants. Downside, size charts are a nightmare. These pants do run smaller than an average pair of jeans. I normally wear size 12 jeans. Waist 34" and Hips 46". Based on the size chart I decided to buy a size 16 figuring that if the hips were a little tight they would stretch over time. The hips fit great. I'm comfortable and I can move my legs enough for hiking. The waist is huge. I could store a few bottles of water just down the back gap. I measured it with a yard stick and the waist is 40 inches. For my fellow shoppers, buy up a size or two from what you normally wear and buy a belt to go with it. Also, you can thank my fiance for the Vitamin bottle photo.
