They are perfect to me... I did have to order a size up (after reading reviews, I decided to start big). I normally wear around a 12-14, so i figured 16 would be a great start. They do fit a little snug around the butt, and they are just a tad loose around the waste (nothing a belt can't fix). Overall, i absolutley love them. The material is soft and not all scratchy like most dickies. They are more of a bell bottom style, not so much straight leg, but i'm fine with that because I prefer some flare. And ladies, if you are short, these will be extra long on you. Lol. I'm 5'8" and they touch the ground. I love the length, but some may find them a bit too long. Aside from trying to figure out what size to order, I absolutely love them.

Comparing 4 Scrub Pants I bought 4 different brand scrib pants at the same time before deciding which to keep. Short story – I’m keeping the Dickies Scrub Pants Long story… 1.SIVVAN Scrubs for Women - Drawstring Cargo Scrub Pants (Small) -size Small fits as expected at waist based on sizing chart -stiffer material/thicker than Dickies -material bubbles up/poofy in front/hips and legs so less flattering if you are very slim -cargo pocket left side; phone completely hidden/deep pocket 7.5” -right cargo phone pocket deep 7.75” plus 4.25” deep phone pocket– wide enough for my phone in its case which sticks out ~2.25” at top -no front slash pockets or back pockets -legs – rather wide for slim legs; 29” inseam; total outside length 40.5”; 7.75” cuff width at the very bottom -returned this pant 2.WonderWink Women's Wonderwork Pull-On Cargo Scrub Pant -liked better than SIVVAN -size Small fits as expected at waist -noticeably softer than SIVVAN material, still puffy in front hips and legs so less flattering if you are very slim -cargo pocket left side; phone completely hidden/deep pocket 7.5” -right cargo phone pocket deep 7.50” plus 5” deep phone pocket– wide enough for my phone in its case which sticks out ~1.75” at top -nice front slash pockets -no left cargo pocket or back pockets -legs – rather wide for slim legs; 8.75” cuff width at the very bottom so very wide for skinny legs -returned this pant 3.Trend Uniforms Tailored Fit Scrub Pants for Women - Soft Stretch with 8 Pockets & Drawstring -overall more flattering than previous 2 -size X-Small fits as expected at waist based on sizing chart - 94% polyester and 6% spandex so more drapey and less stiff/puffy than previous 2 which are 65% Polyester, 35% Cotton), more flattering for me (very slim hips/legs); stretchy fabric that lays nicely -left and right cargo pockets both 6” deep outer and 5.75” deep inner phone pocket, BUT these cargo pockets sit lower 2-3” on the pant legs then previous 2 pants – I feel like a little too low. The previous two feel natural while I feel like I have to bend sideways too much to reach into the cargo pockets of these pants -nice front slash pockets and back pockets -legs – rather wide for slim legs; 30” inseam; total outside length 40.5”; 8.5” cuff width at the very bottom so very wide for skinny legs but less noticeable due to how the fabric drapes -may keep even though would prefer cargo pockets were located 2-3” higher on thighs 4.Dickies EDS Signature Women Scrubs Pant Natural Rise Tapered Leg Pull-on 86106 -liked best for fit/flattering and tapered legs -size XS fits well at the waist -softest material 55% Cotton, 45% Polyester and not puffy around front hips/legs BUT biggest lint magnet (much better after 1st washing); not stretchy -left cargo pocket 7” deep; right cargo pocket – mixed feelings. The 7” deep pocket has a stretchy red knit pocket inside 4.25” deep (not visible from outside). My phone fits VERY snugly here widthwise, maybe too snugly but if not using this pocket you tend to snag items on this knit pocket when trying to use the larger pocket. If I try to drop my phone into the 7” pocket it often catches on the inner knit pocket. Outside/below this large pocket is the outer cargo pocket 5.25” deep. What I’m surprised reviews don’t mention often is the numerous pen loops located inside the pocket. I took a photo (only posting on my Dickies review). I find these loops annoying and they keep preventing me from easily sliding items into the right cargo pocket. But if you carry lots of pens/small tools you find the numerous pen loops a plus.

Great! Runs large by 2 sizes. Generous Length (but I'm short). I'm 5' 3.25" and around 130 lbs. I usually wear a size 8 in women's pants. I have worn and loved Carhartt work pants which I bought in a size 8, but they ran a size larger and give after wear. These Dickies are a great, more reasonably priced work pants very similar to Carhartts. Lots of pockets and a little stretch with the durable material. The size 6 are good but big. I feel like I could have went to a size 4 for a "beltless," fit. I plan on wearing a belt so they don't fall down too much especially when it's been a couple wears before washing/drying. Depending on how much room you like in your work pants, I highly recommend going a size down for some wiggle room, 2 sizes down to go beltless and have it fit more like a glove at the waist. I have a 30" inseam and they are long! I'll have to hem them especially since they'll sink down after some wear and pocket weight.

Bad pockets, Needs some design improvements Edit #2. I took another chance and ordered a second pair in green and received another with skimpy fabric at the pocket and showing white pocket liner. I’m so disappointed in Dickies. I really like these pants and the pair that is made correctly is great but the stingy fabric around the pockets of the others is a deal killer for me. The difference between the fabric depth into the pocket on the “good” pair is an extra half inch. So congrats Dickies, you saved 5 cents on fabric and completely lost a customer. Edit: The second pair of pants I received in green do not have the pocket lining issue. Both pairs stretch out as you wear them and are a bit too big and long despite sizing down. I am 5’5.” Definitely going to need a belt with these but at least they give good freedom of movement. Fit is large, I went with a size 4 reg thinking they might be a touch snug but they are pretty loose and feel like they will loosen up more than I would like with wear. I’m a little “fluffier” than normal right now and if I drop any weight they are definitely going to be too loose. What I really don’t appreciate is the stinginess on the pocket fabric. They did not put enough of the heavy weight twill in the pocket opening so despite being a little on the big side as you wear the pants the lighter bright white fabric shows. It’s looks terrible and only happens on one side. I ordered two pairs and might send the second pair back. The first pair unfortunately I have already worn.

Solid, sturdy work pants sized for women So far, so good. I'm in between an 8 and a 10 so I ordered both. The 10s were gigantic and even the 8s are big. I probably could have gone down to a 6, but I washed these once and they shrank a little, maybe more next time as they're like 97% cotton. Even though they're big, with a belt they hold up comfortably and don't get the chafe-y, baggy crotch you get with some pants. They are also wide leg and very long. I cuffed them, but I'll probably have to hem them so the cuffs don't get full of sand. That being said, they're still GREAT solid, sturdy work pants. They're nice and thick duck with a double front and just enough stretch to be comfy. It's so hard to find quality work wear for women, so I would definitely recommend these pants and would buy them again, but in a smaller size. For reference, I'm 5'7", 145 lbs.

Extremely comfy I selected the purple color in a size large and I absolutely love them! First off they are extremely soft. I wouldn’t consider them fleece or jersey as the material feels like nothing I’ve worn before. The material isn’t thin but isn’t too thick either, to me it is just right. I have been able to wear them when it is cool out and wear them on warmer sunny days. They have been great to work out in as well. The elastic waistline fits perfectly on me but there is also a drawstring in the waist if you need to adjust them. For size reference I am 175-180 pounds and my normal size is large in similar bottoms. I feel they run true to size as I even have a little wiggle room but, if you want them baggy then I would consider sizing up. These do run very long though for my height of 5’4” (and on a side note they do not roll up well either as when rolled they fall out within a few steps so I had to hem them). I as well am thrilled that they wash and dry well too. I have washed them numerous times and they are still so soft and have had no shrinkage. I wash them in cold water and air dry them. They accidentally made it into the dryer once but even then the were just like new coming out of the dryer. I definitely recommend these a million times over!

Great cross-waist pants These are very soft, but with a slight sheen. Not too thick or thin. I hate yoga pants that dig into my stomach, so I only wear cross waisted ones. They give a little bit of tummy support and they lay nice and flat. I'm 5'3", 128 lbs, and I usually wear a medium. But I hate pants that pinch so I ordered a large. I could have gone with a medium for a better fit and a bit more compression. So, they're true to size. I'm petite, with a 28/29 inseam, and these drag a little bit under my heels. I cut an inch off so I can wear them to yoga in bare feet. They won't fray. The pockets on each side are nice and deep for a phone and keys. They have all the best features of great yoga pants. I could live in these all day, every day.

Not enough pockets I generally wear a size 14 and the Large fit perfectly. I like that these pants are pull-on. The waist band is stretchy and comfortable. However, the material feels a bit plastic-y and makes a good bit of noise when you move. I am taller (5'7") and pleased that these pants are actually long enough in the inseam, and drawstring around the ankles is a nice feature. That said, they are also quite long-waisted... almost too long from crotch to waist. And, at this price point, I would expect more than two open pockets in hiking pants, but they are just as advertised, so just be sure to read the description.

A must have in the every color! I own these cargo pants in just about every color, and I was shocked by the quality of the material. The fabric is smooth and gives the pants a tailored look, with an elastic band in the back that prevents gapping. There isn’t much stretch, but they are true to size. I typically wear a size 16 or 17 depending on the brand, and I purchased these in a size 18—they fit perfectly. The pants have functioning pockets and a real zipper, unlike some that just have decorative details. In my opinion, they are tall-friendly. At 5’6”, they look great with heels. These can easily be dressed up for an elevated casual look or paired with sneakers or flats and a tank top for a neat, casual style.

Must haves No joke the perfect pants. I had to write a review. The photos show freshly washed not steamed yet and you can tell the material doesn’t hold wrinkles so a steaming will make them smooth. They are quality made durable but still feel light and flowy. There’s a little sheen which makes them dressier than a regular khaki green pant. I’m 5’3 and for size 4 and will wear a platform or heel so they don’t need hemmed. The back is elastic band so no gap back there and they fit my waist and are loose on my bottom which is a bit hard to do in most pants. Four real pockets! 95% poly. 5% elastane so flowy, but a cotton-like feeling too. This pair is really quality made. The pocket pick is a better representation of the shade of olive green. Will machine wash well and steam to perfection!

Very nice pants for Fall/Winter These are such nice, corduroy pants! I got them for my granddaughter, age 15, who really liked the look of them on Amazon. She’s wearing an adult size 8-10, and is very picky about what she likes to wear for school. Meaning, they had to be black. Haha. Seriously, though, black is her signature color, as she proudly proclaims. She loves that these are cargo pants, and also that they’re corduroy, rather than plain old denim. The corduroy is thin-wale, and the fabric is very soft, making it a perfect, cozy feel for Fall/Winter. The pants fit her perfectly, too. The fabric has a nice bit of stretch in the waist, but not obviously so - it’s just enough to stretch over the hips, then fit her perfectly in the waist. The length is even great! So many pants are too long on even average-size women/girls. These are a tiny bit long on my 5’4” granddaughter, but she prefers them to be long and slouchy. She’s a tough sell these days, but she is honestly delighted with these pants. The price of $34.99, as of this writing, is very reasonable for a quality-made pair of pants. These are machine wash and dry, perfect for a busy family with 3 kids. I’m very pleased with these pants.

THESE WILL SHRINK AND BREAK YOUR HEART I ADORED these pants when they first arrived. I’m 5ft 4.5in tall, 185 lbs, and ordered the XL. They were the perfect, breezy, floor-length pants of my dreams. They skimmed my problem area (my postpartum belly) gracefully and made my stomach look flatter. I washed them once on the cold delicate cycle and hung them to air dry and they shrank at least 2 or 3 inches. They used to touch the top of my feet, and now they are above my ankles. I’m not sure if I’ll wear them at all now - instead of lengthening my proportions, they make my legs look shorter and stubbier. Extremely disappointing and a waste of money. Save yourself the heartache! Unless you’re exactly 5’2, in which case they’d probably end up being perfect lol. Ladies, know your fabrics. Anything 100% rayon or viscose will shrink. Look for polyester options instead. Check my other reviews if you’d like to see the brand I found that worked way better for me.

The material is silky but also a little like linen in that they wrinkle easily but have that worn relaxed look. A sort of resort, vacation vibe. They fit true to size (I’m 5’10” and 180 lbs and wear a large, which is the size I ordered) and they are long enough for us taller ladies. They have “shorts” under them as a liner which is nice. The problem is, they aren’t cut in a flattering way if you have curves! I’ve worn palazzo pants or wide legged pants before that looked beautiful on me, but these are not them. These have too much fabric up top and make me appear to be 2 sizes larger than I am! My whole family agreed. These might look nice on a tall, slender built woman.

I think these pants are a great value -- really, really nice.

Soft & Comfy, Pricey, But Not A Great Fit For Pear-Shaped Women, I *WILL* Luncle Dry! I'm just under 5'8" and down to 195lbs at the time of my order. I lost over 60 lbs last year, from a size 22-24 to I'm not sure what size exactly. This might be important for others to know. I'm still losing slowly, but based on the measurements I took of myself before ordering (Waist 39", Hips 45", Inseam 32-34"), I optimistically ordered size 14 -- and miraculously, they fit, just a bit snug but not overly tight. I think the slight stretch to the material helped with that. The tag was so amusing that I had to share. I'm too old to deal with hand washing anything, so if it doesn't survive my machine, it's not worth having. You can bet that not only did I wash it in my machine (cold water, normal setting, with the rest of my clothes), I also tumbled it dry -- to be fair, I did follow the instructions to not luncle dry it! They came out just fine, with no noticeable shrinkage. If machine washing & drying makes them wear out faster, that's just too bad. The most I'll go out of my way for something special I rarely wear is to put it in a net bag to wash and hang to dry -- but that's only if I have the time and inclination. :) The only problem I have with them is the same as with many pants: there is a gap at my lower back since my hips & butt are bigger than my waist. I have to wear a belt with these pants to correct that. They are not really made for women with my type of curves. I'm also a notorious penny-pincher, and these pants are nearly $40, which is almost twice as much as I prefer to pay for pants. These are comfy & soft, but the material is also thin, so I don't know how long they'll last before they tear. I've only worn them a few times since I received & washed them. For this price, I hope they last a long time. If they do tear or have any problems prematurely, I will come back to update.

This will be the most HONEST REVIEW you've ever seen..... I bought the Black in size 12 and the Green in size 14, but only bcuz a Green size 12 didn't become available Until AFTER I let the seller know they were running out of sizes. However, I have tried Shrinking the Green size 14 and they did shrink a little bit. I will try to Shrink the Green some more, but I will need to Stretch the Legs while they are hot and/or Use a Steamer to Stretch the Length, bcuz I really don't want the Length to Shrink! Also, as you will see in the photos, the Black pair had Tags etc strung into a little black loop inside the back of the pants, whereas the Green pair didn't have any Tags nor a special little black loop for the Tags, so I almost kinda feel that the Green pair were a Return, but that's ok, bcuz I've since washed them... But truthfully, I'm shocked that these pants/jeans are as Amazing, Soft and Super Comfy as they are! When I first grabbed the package from being downstairs and outside at the door, it didn't feel like a package of jeans. It felt waaaaay too light for jeans and I was almost disappointed! But... I opened the package and decided to try them on anyway! Wow!! I am absolutely blown away! Not only do they Fit according to the Size Chart, (I'm 5' - 6.5" and about 145ish pounds) but they are a really nice, soft and a "comfy around the waist" pair of jeans, that I honestly think will become my Favorite pair of jeans!! Due to stomach issues, I don't usually like anything around or touching my upper tummy, but these are sooo Light and Soft that they honestly Don't bother my tummy 1 bit! I'm even been sitting and laying on the couch to check out their comfortness and they pass with flying colors.. COMFORT Beyond Belief!! And bcuz of how extremely Comfortable they are, I am now going to order a couple more pairs!I've already ordered and received the Green pair, but I'm going to get another Black pair like these ones, and I think I'll get a pair of the Darker Blue ones IF the seller has more of the Blue ones in a size 12?!? I do have 1 "Slight" issue, and it's nothing major, it's a "Me Thing" just bcuz I'm very picky when it comes to a Pants Length, I do prefer my pants to be Longer, and so, I wish these jeans were another Inch, or even preferably 2 inches Longer!! But as they are right now, they almost touch the floor at my 5' - 6.5" of height! And like I said, it's a "Me Thing", I just prefer Extra Long Pants!! However, after washing them and before hanging them up to dry, I'm going to try and Stretch the Legs out in order to Gain a little bit more Length!! And IF this doesn't work, I may have to use Hot Steam to Stretch them out a bit, bcuz Hot Steam always works when wanting to Stretch out clothing!! **I have since washed both pairs in cool water and tumbled dried them for a bit with a little bit of Heat and I was able to stretch the Length back.** Anyway, being an extremely Picky person, I personally and highly Recommend these jeans! You Won't be Disappointed!! I truly hope you Grow to Love them as much as I do, and I've only had them on for 30 minutes just to try them out... (I've now had them for 2 days and have washed both the Black and Green pair and All was good) But I do know already, that they will and have, become my FAVORITE pair of Cargo jeans... And yes, it's true that they are Not a Heavy Cotton jean, but that's why I Love them so much, bcuz with them being a lighter fabric, it makes them Easier to move around in! I can do Squats and whatever else in them, plus Sit for Hours in them and I'm still very comfortable and it's all bcuz of how Flexible they are with being a Lighter fabric/material... --Bottom Line, I have absolutely NO PROBLEM with these Cargo jeans! They are absolutely Amazing and I'm sure you will Love them as much as I do...--

Delivered stained and dirty I really like the fit and overall length, I’m 5’4 140 lbs & I got a size 6. However I will say they look rather beaten up/washed several times because they’re super faded, not a dark black at all. They also appear to have oil/brown residue stains. I’m exchanging and hoping I had just received a flawed pair.

Cute and comfortable I’m 5’6”, 168 athletic build and I got a size 8. I could have sized down to a 6 but I wanted a baggy fit. Color matches as advertised, soft material with some stretch. No weird smell out of package. I got lots of compliments!

Something different Jeans I was looking for black jeans but with a little different look. These are a lighter material than regular denim jeans, has a good stretch to it. I ordered size 8 (I am 5'4, 150lbs) they are very comfortable, a touch snug at the waist but lots of leg room. Front and back pockets are both functional (yes!) These look very cute dressed up with heels but will also look nice dressed down with sandals or sneakers.

Just right for the price really pleased with these. I am 5'7'' and 150 and I got the 10. The flap on the pocket does flip up unless you steam it down after washing and the black ones have a propensity for lint. On the plus side, they are flattering and comfortable as can be. I got the green ones first and ordered these because I like them so much. For the price, I'm very happy with these.

Love these pants!! These are adorable. A little bit of stretch, but not enough to feel cheap. Not super thick. Slimming. Perfect length for today’s style. I ordered two other colors immediately. Not the most flattering look for me, but practical I'm always looking for denim leggings, and appreciate new pairs that I can wear out of the house before they get ripped, banged around, paint splattered, etc. I'm not sure these jeans from Dokotoo are that flattering for me, though. I wear an XL (5'4", 170 pounds) and the legs are way tooo long, and the wide flares are also maybe not that slenderizing. The brand-new stitching looks a bit bright and cheap to me. With that said, I may just hem the legs and wear these at home and about town. They are very comfortable and fit well aside from the leg length. They might be perfect to sew kneepads in, for floor work on my various building projects. For someone who is a taller XL, these might be just perfect. Of note is that the slash pockets at the top are real, and the stitching in the area of where cargo pockets would be, is faux. A big plus is that these are elastic cotton -- 98 percent cotton, 2 percent elastane, which may be why they feel comfortable is a way different to the soft comfort of polyester. Just more natural feeling. Review of Dokotoo Women's Casual Mid Waist Cargo Jeans Stretch Wide Leg Denim Pants with Pockets

Wish these were a *tad* longer I wore these all day and I gotta say they are COMFY! I ordered a 4 and I'm a 26-27" waist and they fit really well, I probably could have sized down for a tighter fit on the waist without issue. Obviously the cut of these pants allows for a lot of movement but the fabric stretches and bounces back with ease. I could squat and goblin walk in these and be totally comfy while doing it (we all know that's the true test). I wasn't sure about the aesthetic of these but I got multiple compliments from coworkers and they really grew on me. I did notice that the white threads started to fuzz up by the time I got home. It seems like they may have used cotton thread for these accents which would account for that. My only hesitation is that I wish they were an inch or two longer, but I'm 5'9 and they're definitely long enough. That's why I removed a star, but I think for *most* people the length will be absolutely perfect. These are honestly really great and the quality exceeds the price. Much better than similar pants I've gotten for more $ from popular retailers

Excellent quality. I ordered these Dokotoo Women’s Casual Mid Waist cargo jeans for my daughter and she loved it. Cargo jeans are sewn with high quality and neatly. The seams are even throughout and the threads do not stick out anywhere. The inner seams are carefully finished. The fabric from which the jeans are sewn stretches well and is pleasant to the touch. Cargo jeans look good and complement the look nicely. Cargo jeans have a beautiful gray color. I ordered a size 12 for my daughter and it fit well. My daughter was deciding between 10 and 12 and chose 12 because she doesn't like jeans that are too tight. The daughter is 5.3" tall and weighs 140 lbs. Her hips are 41.5 inches and her waist is 32 inches. There is a size chart in the description, which is very convenient. The cargo jeans are a bit long for her height, but this can be easily fixed with iron-on adhesive tape.

My favorite pants! These are the softest, most comfortable pants I own. And I own a LOT of pants. Based on the size chart, I ordered up but I needn't have, so I'm hoping they shrink a little. But the length is perfect, so hopefully they don't shrink too much! I'd order my usual size but if you're between sizes you might want to order up. For reference, I'm 5'6" about 145 lb and normally wear a size 6 but ordered size 8. They're a bit loose in the waist and with so much stretch and always carrying my phone in my pocket, I'll need a belt! The fabric is amazingly soft - like peach fuzz - with a lot of stretch. They're light but thick enough to have good structure and quite flattering. So glad they're 98% cotton - they'll be breathable for summer comfort. And speaking of pockets - get a load of these bad boys! Nice, big phone pockets - for those of us who don't carry purses, my wallet phone case has no trouble fitting in here! I'd give these pants 10 stars if I could, they're so comfortable. They're my go-to pants for wearing every day and doing every thing! Highly recommended, A+++++ !!

WHAT COULD BE MORE DISTINCTIVE IN LEGGINGS THAN VELVET?! I live in NYC, which is supposed to be known for fashion, but way too many females are looking like cardboard cutouts of each other, with their boring black leggings, or boring black yoga pants. These VELVET grey leggings switch it up a bit. I don't even really like leggings, but feel compelled to wear them because circumstances often dictate I leave the house very quickly, with no allowance for even getting socks on, so I am able to just wear leggings around the house and slip on these waterproof clogs (also from amazon, the Melissa brand), because it also rains a lot here in NYC. If you value a more unique looking legging, then these should be to your liking. For reference, I am about 120 pounds and 5'6", and these are the MEDIUM size leggings, so they may run a bit small. UPDATE: I also bought them in blue, thinking what could be better, in leggings, than BLUE VELVET?! PHOTOS attached of those as well.

Not Sure About These... I am 5'4", rather athletic build, and not much of a booty.😭 I hover between 145-150lbs. I bought a medium, which was what was suggested. I wear anything from a size 6 to a 10 in bottoms, although 8 is my overall average. These are VERY high- waist on me, but I have a short torso so that might be why. They aren't as loose fitting at I thought they would be, but they aren't tight. The waistband is very stretchy so don't panick when you first pull them out the bag. I thought they were child's pants at first! I bought the army green color and it is much darker than what is usually considered army green. It's almost like what would be called a "forest green". The pockets are fine although they land lower on my butt than I would like. Note that the little front patch is just that--a patch of material that is sewn on, not an actual pocket. The material is similar to a lightweight chino or khakis, good for fall and spring. With some leggings underneath you could wear them in the winter as well. Overall, they are very comfortable, I just am not sure how to style them. They aren't tight at all, so if you like how they look on me, get your regular size, but double check the size chart measurements.

Sizing depends on the color, but soooo worth it!! 4'11", 135lb - typically a medium / size 6 with a 30" inseam. Pictures: black in XL, purlple in XL, and olive in medium. I will say this about the sizing... I originally ordered the vintage purple and olive green in medium and while they do fit, I wanted a more relaxed waist (especially on days when bloating is an issue). The mediums felt like I was cutting off circulation to my lower body by the end of the night. Waited to return them and ordered the same colors and another in black leopard (because I love the fabric) in x-large.... The purple and olive were perfect for what I want but the black seems to ACTUALLY be an XL! Nuts! Will be returning the leopard black for medium, but keeping the olive and purple in XL. Material is soft and warm - too warm for me to work out in, but this is just a personal preference. I love how comfortable they are! Not too hard to dress up with a nice shirt. TL;DR you'll love them, just don't be surprised if different colors have different sizes. Size up for a more relaxed fit.

If you don't like panty lines, do not purchase. I purchased these joggers because reviewers said they were a great dupe for the Lululemon Align Joggers. I was frustrated with how quickly my Align Joggers pilled, so I thought these might be a better alternative. I was wrong in so many ways. 1) The fabric shows every single panty line. The only way to avoid panty lines is not to wear underwear. It doesn't matter if you have seamless panties or even a thong; your underwear lines will show. 2) They pill just as bad and as quickly as the align joggers. 3) They are not as soft as the align joggers, and the fabric is much thinner. 4) They don't hold in your belly as well as the align joggers. I have never worn these outside the house because I hate showing panty lines. Personally, I would recommend just paying more for the real thing. Sizing - I am 5'4" and 130 lbs. My waist is 27 inches, and my hips are 38 inches. I purchased a medium, which fits similar to a lululemon 8. If I ordered a different pair, I'd probably get a small instead.

I took them off and folded them in half, laying them flat, and the legs are definitely not the same length. Ordered replacement and will update rating after 2nd pair comes! UPDATE: Replacement came quickly and the legs are of equal length this time so my review is going from 1 star to 4. If it weren’t for the first pair, I would have given these 5 stars.

Not great, not terrible. After reading previous reviews, I took my chances, hoping if I was careful laundering these they would work for me. They’re so cute! …in theory. I’m 5’4”, 176 lbs. I bought the XL in both the orange and the grey. I washed and dried them on delicate, in cold water and on low heat. The orange didn’t seem to shrink much, if at all. The grey did a little more so. But aside from that, the overall fit is kind of… odd. I think if I ordered the XXL, they’d be too big in the legs and groin. I’ve decided not to take that chance. I should also mention that the material is a little thin but not terribly see through, at least not in these two colors. They’re just eh. UPDATE: I ended up reordering the orange in XXL and they fit much better. So size up would be my recommendation.

Comfy and lightweight! I'm 5'4", weigh 132 pounds with an apple shape - 32" waist, and typically wear size 14/Large. I followed the seller's sizing chart and ordered a Large. When they arrived, I tried them on, and they were HUGE! They looked ridiculous! Since these pants are 100% cotton, I decided to machine wash (cold water) and dry them, hoping they would shrink up. Well, they did and fit much better now! They shrunk quite a bit and now fit great and are relaxed and comfortable feeling. I don't think I'll put them in the dryer again, I don't want them to shrink up anymore. I normally love pockets but the placement of these are too low and look sort of weird. They should've just not bothered with them, they're pretty useless where they are. For about $23, these pants are a good deal! I think I'll be wearing them a lot this summer!

My girlfriend loves them Bought them as gift and paired it with a white cable sweater. She jumped up and down when she saw and wore it out. Looks great and very soft. Her go to pants for a casual outdoor excursion. She is 5'3" and 115 lbs. Small fit her perfectly.

Cozy lounge pants I’ve had these pants for about 2 months, and the only thing I’ve noticed is that the material balls up a bit like in the picture I’ve shared. Sooo soft and cozy! I love these pants, they're so comfy! They are made of super soft sweater material. They are high waisted and fitted at the waist and hips, but wide in the legs. They fit true to size, I'm 5'9, 165lbs, and I ordered my normal size, medium. I sometimes buy larges in comfy pants for extra room, but these have lots of stretch, I didn't need to size up. The length hits me a few inches above my ankles. They have another seam sewn about four inches above the hem, I'm wondering if that is there so you could fold it up inside and sew it in case they are too long for you? If that is the case, it's a great idea. My only complaint about these pants is that they do show every lump and bump I have on my thighs and rear. That's not the pants' fault, though. They're really not that see through for being such a light color, you'll probably just not want to wear your bright red undies with these if you're wearing them in public. They are nice enough to wear out in public in my opinion, I'm just going to wear a long shirt with them to hide my imperfections. I love the "porcupine" color, it's a soft, pretty, light gray.

Flowy & comfortable palazzo pants, but long. The material has a really nice feel to it. Listed as 70% cotton/30% linen. They are flowy & full. Very comfortable. I am 5’4” & they are a bit long on me, but will be fine with a small heel. I mostly wear a size 12, occasionally a size 10 if an item runs large. I ordered an XL, but think a Large may of fit more appropriately, both in length & size. My suggestion would be to size down, if in between sizes like myself. They came in a vacuumed packed bag, so they were quite wrinkly, but steamed out nicely & easily. They are very pretty pants if a palazzo style appeals to you. The pockets are a decent size, but are not overly deep. I think they might look a little more swanky on someone taller than myself, but they are attractive pants. To me, the color is more of a periwinkle, than a true blue, but they are a pretty color. I wish there was a petite option, but these are stylish pants that can be dressed up or down. I would recommend, if fuller pants appeal to you.
